## Your task

Examine pull request  using the GitHub CLI command.

Ignore any changes to:
- .specstory/history files
- .claude/ files
- .cursor/ files
- ./specstory binary
- CLAUDE.md
- AGENT.md
- changelog.md

Please code review the pull request.

Review line by line and explain to me the purpose of each change. Use file names and line numbers to reference the changes, but also sequentially number every observation so we can easily reference them.

In the line by line review, pay attention to:

- clarity of variable names
- Go lang idiomatic code
- code clarity and simplicity, readable over clever
- "why" comments, not "how" comments
- missing comments
- missing log output
- missing analytics tracking
- single function exit point where possible (immediate guard clauses are OK)
- verify the code has to exist, is actually needed, and is in use
- verify the code is DRY, and doesn't replicate the same or similar code
- for test cases, ensure a data-driven approach is being used rather than lots of repetitive test code

Format your line by line review like this example:

```
  cmd/remote.go

  Line 12 (removed): Removed unused import of pkg/service package
  - (1) ✅ Good - Cleaning up unused imports is proper Go hygiene

  Lines 157-162 (modified): Changed from loading config directly to using RPC client
  // Old: config, err := service.LoadConfig(dir)
  // New: rpcClient, err := client.NewRPCClient(dir)
  - (2) ✅ Good architectural decision - Now operates via daemon RPC instead of direct file access
  - (3) ✅ Proper defer cleanup pattern for RPC client
  - (4) ✅ Variable name rpcClient is clear and follows conventions

  Lines 164-189 (new): Added remote status check before disabling
  - (5) ✅ Good UX - Detects current connection state to provide better feedback
  - (6) ✅ Type assertions use the two-value form (ok pattern) for safety
  - (7) ⚠️ Nested if statements become deeply indented (4 levels) - could be refactored for readability
  - (8) ✅ Variable names wasConnected, oldURL are descriptive
  - (9) ❓ Missing error handling - if remote.status call fails, we continue anyway. Should we?

  ---
  pkg/remote/sync.go
  Lines 260-265 (modified): Success messages now come after RPC call
  - (10) ✅ Comment "config is now saved" is helpful context
  - (11) ⚠️ Misleading comment placement - comment says "config is now saved" but we can't be certain from this code's perspective (that happens in the daemon)
```

In addition to the line by line review, suggest the 2-5 most important improvements for this code review.

In addition to the most important improvements, let's also think about if any of these changes could benefit from new, updated or expanded unit tests. We focus our unit tests on complicated logic, and combinatorial scenarios, not on coverage or completeness for completeness sake. Better to not have a unit test than to have tests that are simplistic or tautological. We also don't test 3rd party library or language features, only our own code.

For any new test cases that were added, confirm the tests are:

- Not trivial - They test real logic in our code and aren't just testing Go lang or 3rd party libraries
- Not tautological - They would catch real bugs
- Well-designed - Each assertion accurately verifies specific logic and complexity in the code being tested
- Reliable - They won't be flaky, don't rely on external state, and don't catch false positives.
- Not repetitive - They test different scenarios, and use data-driven testing rather than repetitive test code.

---

54	+// SaveResumePrefs persists the resume view mode and last-resumed agent to the
55	+// user-level config (~/.specstory/cli/config.toml). It upserts ONLY the [resume]
56	+// section, preserving the rest of the file (comments and other sections), so the

58	+func SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, lastAgent string) error {
59	+	path := getUserConfigPath()
60	+	if path == "" {
61	+		return fmt.Errorf("could not determine user config path")
62	+	}
63	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
64	+		return fmt.Errorf("creating config directory: %w", err)
65	+	}
66	+
67	+	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
68	+	if err != nil {
69	+		if !os.IsNotExist(err) {
70	+			return fmt.Errorf("reading config: %w", err)
71	+		}
72	+		data = []byte(processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "user"))
73	+	}
74	+
75	+	updated := upsertResumeSection(string(data), viewMode, lastAgent)
76	+	if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(updated), 0o644); err != nil {
77	+		return fmt.Errorf("writing config: %w", err)
78	+	}
79	+	return nil
80	+}
81	+
82	+// upsertResumeSection replaces the [resume] section of a TOML document with active
83	+// view_mode/last_agent keys, preserving every other line. If no [resume] section
84	+// exists it is appended. Only the [resume] block is rewritten — other sections and
85	+// their comments are untouched.
86	+func upsertResumeSection(content, viewMode, lastAgent string) string {
87	+	var block strings.Builder
88	+	block.WriteString("[resume]\n")
89	+	if viewMode != "" {
90	+		fmt.Fprintf(&block, "view_mode = %q\n", viewMode)
91	+	}
92	+	if lastAgent != "" {
93	+		fmt.Fprintf(&block, "last_agent = %q\n", lastAgent)
94	+	}
95	+
96	+	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
97	+	start := -1
98	+	for i, ln := range lines {
99	+		if strings.TrimSpace(ln) == "[resume]" {
100	+			start = i
101	+			break
102	+		}
103	+	}
104	+
105	+	// No existing [resume] section — append it after a blank-line separator.
106	+	if start == -1 {
107	+		out := strings.TrimRight(content, "\n")
108	+		if out != "" {
109	+			out += "\n\n"
110	+		}
111	+		return out + block.String()
112	+	}
113	+
114	+	// Replace from [resume] up to (but not including) the next top-level section.
115	+	end := len(lines)
116	+	for j := start + 1; j < len(lines); j++ {
117	+		t := strings.TrimSpace(lines[j])
118	+		if strings.HasPrefix(t, "[") && strings.HasSuffix(t, "]") {
119	+			end = j
120	+			break
121	+		}
122	+	}
123	+
124	+	var b strings.Builder
125	+	for i := 0; i < start; i++ {
126	+		b.WriteString(lines[i])
127	+		b.WriteString("\n")
128	+	}
129	+	b.WriteString(block.String())
130	+	for i := end; i < len(lines); i++ {
131	+		b.WriteString(lines[i])
132	+		if i < len(lines)-1 {
133	+			b.WriteString("\n")
134	+		}
135	+	}
136	+	return b.String()
137	+}

151	+func TestUpsertResumeSection_AppendWhenAbsent(t *testing.T) {

153	+	out := upsertResumeSection(in, "sparse", "codex")
154	+	if !strings.Contains(out, "[local_sync]") || !strings.Contains(out, "# enabled = false") {
155	+		t.Errorf("existing section/comments not preserved:\n%s", out)
156	+	}
157	+	if !strings.Contains(out, "[resume]") || !strings.Contains(out, `view_mode = "sparse"`) || !strings.Contains(out, `last_agent = "codex"`) {

199	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go

201	index 0000000..205065e

203	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go
204	@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
205	+package spi
206	+
207	+import "sync/atomic"
208	+
209	+// GlobalSessionRef is a lightweight, project-discovering reference to a single
210	+// native session, returned by Provider.ListAllAgentChatSessions.
211	+//
212	+// Unlike the project-scoped ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath), the project is NOT
213	+// an input here: each ref carries the originating working directory (read from inside
214	+// the session) so the caller — the `specstory reindex` command — can resolve project
215	+// identity with utils.ComputeProjectID. The project is discovered, not supplied.
216	+//
217	+// It is intentionally metadata-only (no full SessionData parse); reindex re-fetches
218	+// full data per ref via GetAgentChatSession(OriginCwd, SessionID) when it needs the
219	+// conversation body. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
220	+type GlobalSessionRef struct {
221	+	SessionID  string // native session id (uuid)
222	+	CreatedAt  string // ISO 8601 creation timestamp (first turn), may be empty
223	+	Slug       string // filename-safe slug derived from the first user message
224	+	Name       string // human-readable description (may be empty)
225	+	NativePath string // absolute path the provider opens to read this session
226	+	OriginCwd  string // working directory the session was launched from (-> project_id)
227	+}
228	+
229	+// PathSessionReader is an OPTIONAL capability a Provider may implement: parse a single
230	+// session directly from its already-known native file path, skipping the by-id discovery
231	+// search that GetAgentChatSession performs.
232	+//
233	+// Why: some providers locate a session by scanning their native store. Codex's by-id
234	+// lookup walks the entire ~/.codex/sessions tree on every call, so resolving N sessions
235	+// that way is O(N²). reindex already holds each session's exact file path
236	+// (GlobalSessionRef.NativePath) from enumeration, so a path-keyed parse turns that back
237	+// into O(N). reindex prefers this when a provider implements it and the ref carries a
238	+// NativePath, and falls back to GetAgentChatSession otherwise.
239	+//
240	+// originCwd is the session's originating working directory (GlobalSessionRef.OriginCwd),
241	+// passed through as the workspace root for path normalization — matching what
242	+// GetAgentChatSession receives as projectPath.
243	+type PathSessionReader interface {
244	+	GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*AgentChatSession, error)
245	+}
246	+
247	+// ScanReporter accumulates a provider's enumeration progress (sessions found) so the CLI can
248	+// render a live "scanning" display. It is safe for concurrent use and safe to call on a nil
249	+// receiver (a no-op), so providers can report unconditionally.
250	+type ScanReporter struct {
251	+	found atomic.Int64
252	+}
253	+
254	+// Add records that n more sessions have been found. Files that yield no session (warmup-only or
255	+// sidechain-only transcripts) are deliberately not counted, so the running total reflects real
256	+// sessions rather than raw files.
257	+func (r *ScanReporter) Add(n int) {
258	+	if r != nil {
259	+		r.found.Add(int64(n))
260	+	}
261	+}
262	+
263	+// Found returns the number of sessions found so far.
264	+func (r *ScanReporter) Found() int64 {
265	+	if r == nil {
266	+		return 0
267	+	}
268	+	return r.found.Load()
269	+}
270	+
271	+// ProgressEnumerator is an OPTIONAL Provider capability: enumerate all sessions while reporting
272	+// scan progress into r (which may be nil — ScanReporter is nil-safe, so report unconditionally).
273	+// Providers that don't implement it are enumerated via ListAllAgentChatSessions with no live
274	+// feedback. reindex uses this to render a per-agent "Scanning agents…" display.
275	+type ProgressEnumerator interface {
276	+	ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *ScanReporter) ([]GlobalSessionRef, error)
277	+}

283	 	// sessionCallback: called with AgentChatSession on each update (provider should not block on callback)
284	 	// The implementation should handle its own file watching and session tracking

286	+
287	+	// ReconstructSession rebuilds the provider's native session format from the
288	+	// neutral SessionData so the agent can resume the conversation (the reverse of
289	+	// the parse/generate path). It is a pure transform: it returns the native file
290	+	// bytes, a freshly minted native-format session ID, and a suggested filename,
291	+	// but does NOT touch the filesystem — writing into the live agent store is the
292	+	// caller's responsibility.
293	+	//
294	+	// Every provider carries this responsibility; providers that do not yet have a
295	+	// native serializer return ErrReconstructionUnsupported.
296	+	// See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md for the design.
297	+	ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) (*ReconstructedSession, error)
298	+
299	+	// NativeSessionPath resolves the absolute path where a reconstructed session
300	+	// file (with the given base filename, from ReconstructedSession.Filename)
301	+	// belongs in this provider's native store for the given project, WITHOUT
302	+	// requiring the directory to exist — the caller creates it and writes the file.
303	+	// Providers without a native serializer return ErrReconstructionUnsupported.
304	+	NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error)
305	+
306	+	// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native
307	+	// store, regardless of project — the inverse of the project-scoped
308	+	// ListAgentChatSessions. Each returned ref carries the originating cwd (read from
309	+	// inside the session) so the caller can resolve project identity; the project is
310	+	// discovered, not supplied. Lightweight: metadata only, no full SessionData parse.
311	+	// Used by `specstory reindex` to (re)build the restore index. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
312	+	ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]GlobalSessionRef, error)
313	 }
314	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/reconstruct.go
315	new file mode 100644
316	index 0000000..3445363
317	--- /dev/null
318	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/reconstruct.go
319	@@ -0,0 +1,229 @@
320	+package spi
321	+
322	+import (
323	+	"errors"
324	+	"strings"
325	+
326	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
327	+)
328	+
329	+// ErrReconstructionUnsupported is returned by a provider that carries the
330	+// ReconstructSession responsibility but does not yet have a native serializer.
331	+// Reconstruction is on the Provider interface so every provider implements the
332	+// method, but only some providers (initially Claude Code and Codex CLI) produce
333	+// real native output.
334	+var ErrReconstructionUnsupported = errors.New("session reconstruction not supported by this provider")
335	+
336	+// ReconstructOptions controls how a session is reconstructed into native form.
337	+type ReconstructOptions struct {
338	+	// WorkspaceRoot is the target working directory the reconstructed session
339	+	// should belong to. Native paths/IDs are derived from it. When empty, the
340	+	// provider falls back to the SessionData's own WorkspaceRoot.
341	+	WorkspaceRoot string
342	+
343	+	// MigrationNote, when set, is prepended as a leading agent turn so the agent
344	+	// understands the prior turns were imported from another session. Optional.
345	+	MigrationNote string
346	+}
347	+
348	+// ReconstructedSession is the native output of reconstruction. It is data only;
349	+// the caller decides where (and whether) to write it.
350	+type ReconstructedSession struct {
351	+	SessionID string // freshly minted, native-format session ID
352	+	Filename  string // suggested native filename (base name, not a full path)
353	+	Content   []byte // native session file bytes
354	+}
355	+
356	+// Turn is a single role-tagged text turn in the flattened transcript. The
357	+// reconstruction model collapses everything an agent said, thought, or did into
358	+// plain user/agent text turns (see docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md), which each
359	+// provider then serializes into its native format.
360	+type Turn struct {
361	+	Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
362	+	Text string
363	+}
364	+
365	+// FlattenSessionData reduces a SessionData into an ordered list of plain
366	+// user/agent text turns. This is the single place the cross-provider flattening
367	+// policy lives, so every provider's serializer consumes the same transcript.
368	+//
369	+// Rules (per the locked design):
370	+//   - user/agent text content -> a turn of that role
371	+//   - thinking content -> folded into the agent turn's text (not dropped)
372	+//   - tool calls -> folded into the agent turn's text via the pre-rendered
373	+//     Summary / FormattedMarkdown captured on the forward pass
374	+//   - model, usage, and path hints -> dropped
375	+//
376	+// migrationNote, when non-empty, becomes a leading agent turn.
377	+func FlattenSessionData(data *schema.SessionData, migrationNote string) []Turn {
378	+	var turns []Turn
379	+
380	+	if strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote) != "" {
381	+		turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote)})
382	+	}
383	+
384	+	if data == nil {
385	+		return turns
386	+	}
387	+
388	+	for i := range data.Exchanges {
389	+		for j := range data.Exchanges[i].Messages {
390	+			msg := &data.Exchanges[i].Messages[j]
391	+
392	+			// Only user and agent roles produce turns; anything else is skipped.
393	+			if msg.Role != schema.RoleUser && msg.Role != schema.RoleAgent {
394	+				continue
395	+			}
396	+
397	+			text := messageTurnText(msg)
398	+			if strings.TrimSpace(text) == "" {
399	+				continue
400	+			}
401	+
402	+			// Drop synthetic, non-conversational turns (slash-command invocations,
403	+			// local-command output, etc.) so they are not replayed into the resumed
404	+			// session. This is intentionally reconstruction-only — archival markdown
405	+			// stays faithful to the source session.
406	+			if isSyntheticTurnText(text) {
407	+				continue
408	+			}
409	+
410	+			turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: msg.Role, Text: text})
411	+		}
412	+	}
413	+
414	+	return turns
415	+}
416	+
417	+// PrepareTurns validates session data and flattens it into the neutral turn list
418	+// every provider's serializer consumes. It centralizes the two guards each
419	+// ReconstructSession must apply before it can build native output: the source
420	+// SessionData must be non-nil, and flattening must yield at least one turn.
421	+// Keeping these here means every provider rejects the same cases with the same
422	+// messages and cannot drift apart.
423	+func PrepareTurns(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) ([]Turn, error) {
424	+	if data == nil {
425	+		return nil, errors.New("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
426	+	}
427	+
428	+	turns := FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
429	+	if len(turns) == 0 {
430	+		return nil, errors.New("session has no content to reconstruct")
431	+	}
432	+
433	+	return turns, nil
434	+}
435	+
436	+// ResolveWorkspaceRoot picks the working directory a reconstructed session
437	+// belongs to: the explicit target from opts when set, otherwise the source
438	+// session's own recorded root. Providers derive native paths/IDs from the
439	+// result; the cursor provider is the lone exception that needs no workspace.
440	+func ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts ReconstructOptions, data *schema.SessionData) string {
441	+	if opts.WorkspaceRoot != "" {
442	+		return opts.WorkspaceRoot
443	+	}
444	+	if data == nil {
445	+		return ""
446	+	}
447	+	return data.WorkspaceRoot
448	+}
449	+
450	+// SyntheticCommandTags are the wrapper tags coding agents inject for
451	+// non-conversational slash-command records: the command invocation, its piped
452	+// local stdout/stderr, and the local-command caveat. They are conversation
453	+// scaffolding, never a real user prompt. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for
454	+// the tag set, shared by reconstruction's turn filter here (matched anywhere in a
455	+// flattened turn) and Claude Code's title-selection filter
456	+// (claudecode.isSyntheticMessage, matched as a leading tag), so the two cannot
457	+// drift apart.
458	+var SyntheticCommandTags = []string{
459	+	"<command-name>",
460	+	"<command-message>",
461	+	"<command-args>",
462	+	"<local-command-stdout>",
463	+	"<local-command-stderr>",
464	+	"<local-command-caveat>",
465	+}
466	+
467	+// extraSyntheticTurnMarkers are synthetic markers beyond the shared command tags
468	+// that must also never be replayed into a resumed session: Claude Code's
469	+// <TEXTBLOCK> title-generation wrapper and its interrupt marker (which also has a
470	+// "…for tool use" variant, caught by the prefix).
471	+var extraSyntheticTurnMarkers = []string{
472	+	"<TEXTBLOCK>",
473	+	"[Request interrupted by user",
474	+}
475	+
476	+// isSyntheticTurnText reports whether a flattened turn is a synthetic,
477	+// non-conversational artifact that should not be replayed into a resumed session.
478	+// It matches by Contains because a flattened turn concatenates content parts, so a
479	+// marker can appear anywhere in the text. Markdown generation does NOT use this
480	+// filter and stays faithful to source.
481	+func isSyntheticTurnText(text string) bool {
482	+	for _, marker := range SyntheticCommandTags {
483	+		if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
484	+			return true
485	+		}
486	+	}
487	+	for _, marker := range extraSyntheticTurnMarkers {
488	+		if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
489	+			return true
490	+		}
491	+	}
492	+	return false
493	+}
494	+
495	+// messageTurnText flattens a single message into its turn text: all text/thinking
496	+// content parts joined, followed by the tool's pre-rendered markdown when present.
497	+func messageTurnText(msg *schema.Message) string {
498	+	var parts []string
499	+
500	+	for _, part := range msg.Content {
501	+		// Both "text" and "thinking" parts carry their content in Text; thinking
502	+		// is intentionally treated as ordinary agent text.
503	+		if strings.TrimSpace(part.Text) != "" {
504	+			parts = append(parts, part.Text)
505	+		}
506	+	}
507	+
508	+	text := strings.Join(parts, "\n\n")
509	+
510	+	if msg.Tool != nil {
511	+		toolText := toolTurnText(msg.Tool)
512	+		if toolText != "" {
513	+			if text != "" {
514	+				text += "\n\n" + toolText
515	+			} else {
516	+				text = toolText
517	+			}
518	+		}
519	+	}
520	+
521	+	return text
522	+}
523	+
524	+// toolTurnText renders a tool call as agent text, reusing the markdown the
525	+// forward pass already produced (Summary + FormattedMarkdown). Falls back to a
526	+// minimal description when neither is available.
527	+func toolTurnText(tool *schema.ToolInfo) string {
528	+	var b strings.Builder
529	+
530	+	if tool.Summary != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary) != "" {
531	+		b.WriteString(strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary))
532	+	}
533	+
534	+	if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.FormattedMarkdown) != "" {
535	+		if b.Len() > 0 {
536	+			b.WriteString("\n\n")
537	+		}
538	+		b.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(*tool.FormattedMarkdown, "\n"))
539	+	}
540	+
541	+	if b.Len() == 0 {
542	+		// Nothing pre-rendered; emit a minimal, non-empty description so the tool
543	+		// activity is at least acknowledged in the transcript.
544	+		b.WriteString("Tool use: " + tool.Name)
545	+	}
546	+
547	+	return b.String()
548	+}

1	package spi
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
8	)
9	
10	// ErrReconstructionUnsupported is returned by a provider that carries the
11	// ReconstructSession responsibility but does not yet have a native serializer.
12	// Reconstruction is on the Provider interface so every provider implements the
13	// method, but only some providers (initially Claude Code and Codex CLI) produce
14	// real native output.
15	var ErrReconstructionUnsupported = errors.New("session reconstruction not supported by this provider")
16	
17	// ReconstructOptions controls how a session is reconstructed into native form.
18	type ReconstructOptions struct {
19		// WorkspaceRoot is the target working directory the reconstructed session
20		// should belong to. Native paths/IDs are derived from it. When empty, the
21		// provider falls back to the SessionData's own WorkspaceRoot.
22		WorkspaceRoot string
23	
24		// MigrationNote, when set, is prepended as a leading agent turn so the agent
25		// understands the prior turns were imported from another session. Optional.
26		MigrationNote string
27	}
28	
29	// ReconstructedSession is the native output of reconstruction. It is data only;
30	// the caller decides where (and whether) to write it.
31	type ReconstructedSession struct {
32		SessionID string // freshly minted, native-format session ID
33		Filename  string // suggested native filename (base name, not a full path)
34		Content   []byte // native session file bytes
35	}
36	
37	// Turn is a single role-tagged text turn in the flattened transcript. The
38	// reconstruction model collapses everything an agent said, thought, or did into
39	// plain user/agent text turns (see docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md), which each
40	// provider then serializes into its native format.
41	type Turn struct {
42		Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
43		Text string
44	}
45	
46	// FlattenSessionData reduces a SessionData into an ordered list of plain
47	// user/agent text turns. This is the single place the cross-provider flattening
48	// policy lives, so every provider's serializer consumes the same transcript.
49	//
50	// Rules (per the locked design):
51	//   - user/agent text content -> a turn of that role
52	//   - thinking content -> folded into the agent turn's text (not dropped)
53	//   - tool calls -> folded into the agent turn's text via the pre-rendered
54	//     Summary / FormattedMarkdown captured on the forward pass
55	//   - model, usage, and path hints -> dropped
56	//
57	// migrationNote, when non-empty, becomes a leading agent turn.
58	func FlattenSessionData(data *schema.SessionData, migrationNote string) []Turn {
59		var turns []Turn
60	
61		if strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote) != "" {
62			turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote)})
63		}
64	
65		if data == nil {
66			return turns
67		}
68	
69		for i := range data.Exchanges {
70			for j := range data.Exchanges[i].Messages {
71				msg := &data.Exchanges[i].Messages[j]
72	
73				// Only user and agent roles produce turns; anything else is skipped.
74				if msg.Role != schema.RoleUser && msg.Role != schema.RoleAgent {
75					continue
76				}
77	
78				text := messageTurnText(msg)
79				if strings.TrimSpace(text) == "" {
80					continue
81				}
82	
83				// Drop synthetic, non-conversational turns (slash-command invocations,
84				// local-command output, etc.) so they are not replayed into the resumed
85				// session. This is intentionally reconstruction-only — archival markdown
86				// stays faithful to the source session.
87				if isSyntheticTurnText(text) {
88					continue
89				}
90	
91				turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: msg.Role, Text: text})
92			}
93		}
94	
95		return turns
96	}
97	
98	// PrepareTurns validates session data and flattens it into the neutral turn list
99	// every provider's serializer consumes. It centralizes the two guards each
100	// ReconstructSession must apply before it can build native output: the source
101	// SessionData must be non-nil, and flattening must yield at least one turn.
102	// Keeping these here means every provider rejects the same cases with the same
103	// messages and cannot drift apart.
104	func PrepareTurns(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) ([]Turn, error) {
105		if data == nil {
106			return nil, errors.New("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
107		}
108	
109		turns := FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
110		if len(turns) == 0 {
111			return nil, errors.New("session has no content to reconstruct")
112		}
113	
114		return turns, nil
115	}
116	
117	// ResolveWorkspaceRoot picks the working directory a reconstructed session
118	// belongs to: the explicit target from opts when set, otherwise the source
119	// session's own recorded root. Providers derive native paths/IDs from the
120	// result; the cursor provider is the lone exception that needs no workspace.
121	func ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts ReconstructOptions, data *schema.SessionData) string {
122		if opts.WorkspaceRoot != "" {
123			return opts.WorkspaceRoot
124		}
125		if data == nil {
126			return ""
127		}
128		return data.WorkspaceRoot
129	}
130	
131	// SyntheticCommandTags are the wrapper tags coding agents inject for
132	// non-conversational slash-command records: the command invocation, its piped
133	// local stdout/stderr, and the local-command caveat. They are conversation
134	// scaffolding, never a real user prompt. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for
135	// the tag set, shared by reconstruction's turn filter here (matched anywhere in a
136	// flattened turn) and Claude Code's title-selection filter
137	// (claudecode.isSyntheticMessage, matched as a leading tag), so the two cannot
138	// drift apart.
139	var SyntheticCommandTags = []string{
140		"<command-name>",
141		"<command-message>",
142		"<command-args>",
143		"<local-command-stdout>",
144		"<local-command-stderr>",
145		"<local-command-caveat>",
146	}
147	
148	// extraSyntheticTurnMarkers are synthetic markers beyond the shared command tags
149	// that must also never be replayed into a resumed session: Claude Code's
150	// <TEXTBLOCK> title-generation wrapper and its interrupt marker (which also has a
151	// "…for tool use" variant, caught by the prefix).
152	var extraSyntheticTurnMarkers = []string{
153		"<TEXTBLOCK>",
154		"[Request interrupted by user",
155	}
156	
157	// isSyntheticTurnText reports whether a flattened turn is a synthetic,
158	// non-conversational artifact that should not be replayed into a resumed session.
159	// It matches by Contains because a flattened turn concatenates content parts, so a
160	// marker can appear anywhere in the text. Markdown generation does NOT use this
161	// filter and stays faithful to source.
162	func isSyntheticTurnText(text string) bool {
163		for _, marker := range SyntheticCommandTags {
164			if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
165				return true
166			}
167		}
168		for _, marker := range extraSyntheticTurnMarkers {
169			if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
170				return true
171			}
172		}
173		return false
174	}
175	
176	// messageTurnText flattens a single message into its turn text: all text/thinking
177	// content parts joined, followed by the tool's pre-rendered markdown when present.
178	func messageTurnText(msg *schema.Message) string {
179		var parts []string
180	
181		for _, part := range msg.Content {
182			// Both "text" and "thinking" parts carry their content in Text; thinking
183			// is intentionally treated as ordinary agent text.
184			if strings.TrimSpace(part.Text) != "" {
185				parts = append(parts, part.Text)
186			}
187		}
188	
189		text := strings.Join(parts, "\n\n")
190	
191		if msg.Tool != nil {
192			toolText := toolTurnText(msg.Tool)
193			if toolText != "" {
194				if text != "" {
195					text += "\n\n" + toolText
196				} else {
197					text = toolText
198				}
199			}
200		}
201	
202		return text
203	}
204	
205	// toolTurnText renders a tool call as agent text, reusing the markdown the
206	// forward pass already produced (Summary + FormattedMarkdown). Falls back to a
207	// minimal description when neither is available.
208	func toolTurnText(tool *schema.ToolInfo) string {
209		var b strings.Builder
210	
211		if tool.Summary != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary) != "" {
212			b.WriteString(strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary))
213		}
214	
215		if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.FormattedMarkdown) != "" {
216			if b.Len() > 0 {
217				b.WriteString("\n\n")
218			}
219			b.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(*tool.FormattedMarkdown, "\n"))
220		}
221	
222		if b.Len() == 0 {
223			// Nothing pre-rendered; emit a minimal, non-empty description so the tool
224			// activity is at least acknowledged in the transcript.
225			b.WriteString("Tool use: " + tool.Name)
226		}
227	
228		return b.String()
229	}
230
```

+// Package sessionindex implements sessions.db — the machine-level index of every coding-agent
+// session SpecStory knows about, across all projects and providers. It backs the
+// `specstory resume` selection UX and is (re)built by `specstory reindex`.
+//
+// sessions.db is a DERIVED CACHE over the native session stores: it can be deleted and
+// fully rebuilt at any time. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md for the design and schema.
+package sessionindex
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"database/sql"
+	"errors"
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"sort"
+	"strings"
+
+	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // SQLite driver (pure Go), same as pkg/provenance
+)
+
+// Connection-pool sizing. Writes (reindex) must serialize on a single connection to
+// avoid SQLITE_BUSY/deadlocks. The interactive browse path (resume/search) only reads,
+// so it opens several connections — WAL permits many concurrent readers, which keeps a
+// single slow full-text query (a broad prefix can take seconds) from starving the UI.
+const (
+	writerConns = 1
+	readerConns = 4
+)
+
+// Session is one row of the restore index (the `sessions` table), plus Body, which is
+// indexed into the FTS table rather than stored on the row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
+type Session struct {
+	ProjectID    string // resolved git_id (else workspace_id)
+	ProjectName  string
+	Agent        string // provider id: claude, codex, gemini, droid, deepseek, cursor
+	SessionID    string // native session id (uuid)
+	CreatedAt    string // ISO 8601, first turn
+	UpdatedAt    string // ISO 8601, last activity (last turn, else file mtime)
+	UserTurns    int    // count of user prompts
+	TotalTurns   int    // count of all messages
+	Slug         string
+	Name         string
...
</persisted-output>

7	+// Package sessionindex implements sessions.db — the machine-level index of every coding-agent
8	+// session SpecStory knows about, across all projects and providers. It backs the
9	+// `specstory resume` selection UX and is (re)built by `specstory reindex`.
10	+//
11	+// sessions.db is a DERIVED CACHE over the native session stores: it can be deleted and
12	+// fully rebuilt at any time. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md for the design and schema.
13	+package sessionindex
14	+
15	+import (
16	+	"context"
17	+	"database/sql"
18	+	"errors"
19	+	"fmt"
20	+	"os"
21	+	"path/filepath"
22	+	"sort"
23	+	"strings"
24	+
25	+	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // SQLite driver (pure Go), same as pkg/provenance
26	+)
27	+
28	+// Connection-pool sizing. Writes (reindex) must serialize on a single connection to
29	+// avoid SQLITE_BUSY/deadlocks. The interactive browse path (resume/search) only reads,
30	+// so it opens several connections — WAL permits many concurrent readers, which keeps a
31	+// single slow full-text query (a broad prefix can take seconds) from starving the UI.
32	+const (
33	+	writerConns = 1
34	+	readerConns = 4
35	+)
36	+
37	+// Session is one row of the restore index (the `sessions` table), plus Body, which is
38	+// indexed into the FTS table rather than stored on the row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
39	+type Session struct {
40	+	ProjectID    string // resolved git_id (else workspace_id)
41	+	ProjectName  string
42	+	Agent        string // provider id: claude, codex, gemini, droid, deepseek, cursor
43	+	SessionID    string // native session id (uuid)
44	+	CreatedAt    string // ISO 8601, first turn
45	+	UpdatedAt    string // ISO 8601, last activity (last turn, else file mtime)
46	+	UserTurns    int    // count of user prompts
47	+	TotalTurns   int    // count of all messages
48	+	Slug         string
49	+	Name         string
50	+	NativePath   string // absolute path the provider opens to read this session
51	+	OriginCwd    string // working directory the session was launched from
52	+	Size         int64  // native file size, bytes — part of the freshness fingerprint
53	+	Mtime        int64  // native file mtime, epoch ms — part of the freshness fingerprint
54	+	IndexVersion int    // reindex logic version that wrote this row — part of the fingerprint
55	+	IndexedAt    string // ISO 8601, when reindex last wrote this row
56	+
57	+	// Body is the full conversation text, indexed into sessions_fts (not persisted on
58	+	// the sessions row). Left empty on rows read back out of the index.
59	+	Body string
60	+
61	+	// IsNew is a write-time hint, not a persisted column: when true, the caller has
62	+	// determined this (agent, session_id) has no existing index row, so upsertOne can skip
63	+	// looking up and deleting a prior FTS row before insert. Leave false when unsure — the
64	+	// lookup-and-delete is then kept, which is always correct (a missing prior row is a no-op).
65	+	IsNew bool
66	+}
67	+
68	+// Fingerprint identifies an indexed session's freshness: the native file's size and
69	+// mtime, plus the reindex logic version that produced the row. reindex skips a session
70	+// whose fingerprint is unchanged. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
71	+type Fingerprint struct {
72	+	Size    int64
73	+	Mtime   int64
74	+	Version int
75	+}
76	+
77	+// Store is a handle to sessions.db.
78	+type Store struct {
79	+	db *sql.DB
80	+}
81	+
82	+// DefaultPath returns ~/.specstory/sessions.db.
83	+func DefaultPath() (string, error) {
84	+	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
85	+	if err != nil {
86	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve home directory: %w", err)
87	+	}
88	+	return filepath.Join(home, ".specstory", "sessions.db"), nil
89	+}
90	+
91	+// Open opens (or creates) sessions.db for writing — a single serialized connection,
92	+// applying WAL + performance pragmas (matching pkg/provenance) and ensuring the schema
93	+// exists. Use OpenReader for the read-only interactive browse path.
94	+func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
95	+	return openWith(path, writerConns)
96	+}
97	+
98	+// OpenReader opens sessions.db for the interactive browse path (resume/search), which
99	+// only reads. It allows several concurrent connections so one slow full-text query can't
100	+// starve the UI (WAL permits many simultaneous readers). Never write through this handle.
101	+func OpenReader(path string) (*Store, error) {
102	+	return openWith(path, readerConns)
103	+}
104	+
105	+func openWith(path string, maxConns int) (*Store, error) {
106	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
107	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating database directory: %w", err)
108	+	}
109	+
110	+	dsn := fmt.Sprintf("file:%s?_pragma=busy_timeout=15000&_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)", filepath.ToSlash(path))
111	+	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
112	+	if err != nil {
113	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening sessions.db: %w", err)
114	+	}
115	+
116	+	db.SetMaxOpenConns(maxConns)
117	+	db.SetMaxIdleConns(maxConns)
118	+
119	+	for _, p := range []string{
120	+		"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL",
121	+		"PRAGMA cache_size = -64000",
122	+		"PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY",
123	+		"PRAGMA mmap_size = 268435456",
124	+		"PRAGMA page_size = 8192",
125	+	} {
126	+		if _, err := db.Exec(p); err != nil {
127	+			_ = db.Close()
128	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting pragma %q: %w", p, err)
129	+		}
130	+	}
131	+
132	+	s := &Store{db: db}
133	+	if err := s.ensureSchema(); err != nil {
134	+		_ = db.Close()
135	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ensuring schema: %w", err)
136	+	}
137	+	return s, nil
138	+}
139	+
140	+// Close closes the database connection.
141	+func (s *Store) Close() error {
142	+	return s.db.Close()
143	+}
144	+
145	+// sessionColumns is the canonical sessions column list, shared by every SELECT so the
146	+// scan order in scanSessions stays in lockstep with it.
147	+const sessionColumns = `project_id, project_name, agent, session_id, created_at, updated_at,
148	+	user_turns, total_turns, slug, name, native_path, origin_cwd, size, mtime, index_version, indexed_at`
149	+
150	+// sessionInsertColumns is sessionColumns plus fts_rowid, the internal link to the session's
151	+// FTS row. fts_rowid is write-only — set on insert, read only by SessionBody's join — so it is
152	+// deliberately kept out of sessionColumns and the Session struct rather than threaded through
153	+// every read path.
154	+const sessionInsertColumns = sessionColumns + `, fts_rowid`
155	+
156	+func (s *Store) ensureSchema() error {
157	+	schema := `
158	+	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
159	+		project_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
160	+		project_name TEXT,
161	+		agent        TEXT NOT NULL,
162	+		session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
163	+		created_at   TEXT,
164	+		updated_at   TEXT,
165	+		user_turns   INTEGER,
166	+		total_turns  INTEGER,
167	+		slug         TEXT,
168	+		name         TEXT,
169	+		native_path  TEXT,
170	+		origin_cwd   TEXT,
171	+		size          INTEGER,
172	+		mtime         INTEGER,
173	+		index_version INTEGER,
174	+		indexed_at    TEXT,
175	+		fts_rowid     INTEGER,
176	+		PRIMARY KEY (agent, session_id)
177	+	);
178	+	-- Composite over the project filter PLUS the picker's sort order. ListByProject
179	+	-- (the resume picker's hot path) filters by project_id and orders by
180	+	-- updated_at DESC, created_at DESC; this index serves both, so SQLite walks it
181	+	-- backward instead of filtering then sorting in a temp b-tree. project_id is the
182	+	-- left prefix, so ProjectCount/UnattributedCount still use it. Replaces the old
183	+	-- single-column idx_sessions_project (dropped in the migration below).
184	+	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_project_recent ON sessions(project_id, updated_at, created_at);
185	+
186	+	-- Standalone FTS5 index over the conversation body + name. session_id/agent ride
187	+	-- along UNINDEXED as join keys back to the sessions row. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
188	+	CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions_fts USING fts5(
189	+		session_id UNINDEXED,
190	+		agent UNINDEXED,
191	+		name,
192	+		body
193	+	);
194	+	`
195	+	if _, err := s.db.Exec(schema); err != nil {
196	+		return fmt.Errorf("executing schema: %w", err)
197	+	}
198	+	// Migration for indexes created before index_version existed (the column is part of
199	+	// the freshness fingerprint). Ignore the error when it already exists.
200	+	_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN index_version INTEGER DEFAULT 0`)
201	+	// fts_rowid links a session row to its sessions_fts row, so the body read and the
202	+	// delete-before-insert are O(1) rowid lookups instead of whole-FTS scans (session_id/agent
203	+	// are UNINDEXED). NULL on rows written before this column existed; a reindex (reindexVersion
204	+	// bump) repopulates it. Ignore the error when the column already exists.
205	+	_, _ = s.db.Exec(`ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN fts_rowid INTEGER`)
206	+	// Drop the old single-column project index now superseded by the composite
207	+	// idx_sessions_project_recent (project_id is its left prefix). Idempotent; a no-op
208	+	// on fresh databases that never had it.
209	+	_, _ = s.db.Exec(`DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_sessions_project`)
210	+	return nil
211	+}
212	+
213	+// Fingerprints returns the freshness fingerprint of every indexed session, keyed by
214	+// fingerprintKey(agent, session_id). reindex loads this once and skips any session
215	+// whose native file is unchanged (same size + mtime) and was indexed by the current
216	+// logic version.
217	+func (s *Store) Fingerprints() (map[string]Fingerprint, error) {
218	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT agent, session_id, size, mtime, index_version FROM sessions`)
219	+	if err != nil {
220	+		return nil, err
221	+	}
222	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
223	+
224	+	out := make(map[string]Fingerprint)
225	+	for rows.Next() {
226	+		var agent, sessionID string
227	+		var fp Fingerprint
228	+		if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &fp.Size, &fp.Mtime, &fp.Version); err != nil {
229	+			return nil, err
230	+		}
231	+		out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = fp
232	+	}
233	+	return out, rows.Err()
234	+}
235	+
236	+// FingerprintKey is the map key for a session's fingerprint: agent + NUL + session_id.
237	+func FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID string) string {
238	+	return agent + "\x00" + sessionID
239	+}
240	+
241	+// sessionUpsertStmts are the per-row statements UpsertBatch prepares once and reuses across
242	+// every row in a transaction, so the SQL is parsed once per batch instead of once per row.
243	+type sessionUpsertStmts struct {
244	+	insSession    *sql.Stmt
245	+	selOldRowid   *sql.Stmt
246	+	delFTSByRowid *sql.Stmt
247	+	delFTSByKey   *sql.Stmt
248	+	insFTS        *sql.Stmt
249	+}
250	+
251	+// upsertOne writes one session's row and full-text row using the batch's prepared statements.
252	+// FTS5 standalone tables are not auto-synced, so the FTS row is maintained by hand. The new FTS
253	+// row is inserted first so its rowid can be stored on the sessions row (fts_rowid) — that link
254	+// is what makes later body reads and replace-deletes O(1) rowid lookups instead of whole-FTS
255	+// scans (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED). For an existing session the prior FTS row is removed
256	+// first; brand-new sessions (sess.IsNew) skip that lookup-and-delete entirely.
257	+func upsertOne(st sessionUpsertStmts, sess Session) error {
258	+	if !sess.IsNew {
259	+		if err := deleteOldFTSRow(st, sess); err != nil {
260	+			return err
261	+		}
262	+	}
263	+	res, err := st.insFTS.Exec(sess.SessionID, sess.Agent, sess.Name, sess.Body)
264	+	if err != nil {
265	+		return fmt.Errorf("insert fts row: %w", err)
266	+	}
267	+	ftsRowid, err := res.LastInsertId()
268	+	if err != nil {
269	+		return fmt.Errorf("read fts rowid: %w", err)
270	+	}
271	+	if _, err := st.insSession.Exec(
272	+		sess.ProjectID, sess.ProjectName, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID, sess.CreatedAt, sess.UpdatedAt,
273	+		sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns, sess.Slug, sess.Name, sess.NativePath, sess.OriginCwd,
274	+		sess.Size, sess.Mtime, sess.IndexVersion, sess.IndexedAt, ftsRowid); err != nil {
275	+		return fmt.Errorf("upsert session row: %w", err)
276	+	}
277	+	return nil
278	+}
279	+
280	+// deleteOldFTSRow removes an existing session's current FTS row before the sessions row is
281	+// replaced (INSERT OR REPLACE would otherwise drop the fts_rowid link and orphan the FTS row).
282	+// It resolves the row by its stored fts_rowid (O(1)); for rows written before fts_rowid existed
283	+// (NULL) it falls back to the by-key delete — a whole-FTS scan that a reindex retires by
284	+// repopulating fts_rowid.
285	+func deleteOldFTSRow(st sessionUpsertStmts, sess Session) error {
286	+	var oldRowid sql.NullInt64
287	+	switch err := st.selOldRowid.QueryRow(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID).Scan(&oldRowid); {
288	+	case errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows):
289	+		return nil // no prior row to remove
290	+	case err != nil:
291	+		return fmt.Errorf("look up old fts rowid: %w", err)
292	+	}
293	+	if oldRowid.Valid {
294	+		if _, err := st.delFTSByRowid.Exec(oldRowid.Int64); err != nil {
295	+			return fmt.Errorf("clear fts row by rowid: %w", err)
296	+		}
297	+		return nil
298	+	}
299	+	if _, err := st.delFTSByKey.Exec(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID); err != nil {
300	+		return fmt.Errorf("clear legacy fts row: %w", err)
301	+	}
302	+	return nil
303	+}
304	+
305	+// prepareUpsert prepares the per-row statements on tx and returns them with a closer.
306	+func prepareUpsert(tx *sql.Tx) (sessionUpsertStmts, func(), error) {
307	+	var prepared []*sql.Stmt
308	+	closer := func() {
309	+		for _, s := range prepared {
310	+			_ = s.Close()
311	+		}
312	+	}
313	+	prep := func(what, query string) (*sql.Stmt, error) {
314	+		s, err := tx.Prepare(query)
315	+		if err != nil {
316	+			closer()
317	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("prepare %s: %w", what, err)
318	+		}
319	+		prepared = append(prepared, s)
320	+		return s, nil
321	+	}
322	+
323	+	insSession, err := prep("session upsert", `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sessions (`+sessionInsertColumns+`)
324	+		VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
325	+	if err != nil {
326	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
327	+	}
328	+	selOldRowid, err := prep("fts rowid lookup", `SELECT fts_rowid FROM sessions WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`)
329	+	if err != nil {
330	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
331	+	}
332	+	delFTSByRowid, err := prep("fts delete by rowid", `DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE rowid = ?`)
333	+	if err != nil {
334	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
335	+	}
336	+	delFTSByKey, err := prep("fts delete by key", `DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`)
337	+	if err != nil {
338	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
339	+	}
340	+	insFTS, err := prep("fts insert", `INSERT INTO sessions_fts (session_id, agent, name, body) VALUES (?,?,?,?)`)
341	+	if err != nil {
342	+		return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, err
343	+	}
344	+	return sessionUpsertStmts{
345	+		insSession:    insSession,
346	+		selOldRowid:   selOldRowid,
347	+		delFTSByRowid: delFTSByRowid,
348	+		delFTSByKey:   delFTSByKey,
349	+		insFTS:        insFTS,
350	+	}, closer, nil
351	+}
352	+
353	+// Upsert inserts or replaces a single session row and its full-text row, atomically. A
354	+// re-index of the same session (same agent + session_id) replaces both in lockstep.
355	+func (s *Store) Upsert(sess Session) error {
356	+	return s.UpsertBatch([]Session{sess})
357	+}
358	+
359	+// UpsertBatch writes many sessions in one transaction — the write path for `reindex`,
360	+// which batches to avoid thousands of tiny WAL commits.
361	+func (s *Store) UpsertBatch(sessions []Session) error {
362	+	if len(sessions) == 0 {
363	+		return nil
364	+	}
365	+	tx, err := s.db.Begin()
366	+	if err != nil {
367	+		return fmt.Errorf("begin upsert: %w", err)
368	+	}
369	+	committed := false
370	+	defer func() {
371	+		if !committed {
372	+			_ = tx.Rollback()
373	+		}
374	+	}()
375	+
376	+	st, closeStmts, err := prepareUpsert(tx)
377	+	if err != nil {
378	+		return err
379	+	}
380	+	defer closeStmts()
381	+
382	+	for _, sess := range sessions {
383	+		if err := upsertOne(st, sess); err != nil {
384	+			return err
385	+		}
386	+	}
387	+
388	+	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
389	+		return fmt.Errorf("commit upsert: %w", err)
390	+	}
391	+	committed = true
392	+	return nil
393	+}
394	+
395	+// Exists reports whether a session row is already indexed, looked up by the primary key
396	+// (agent, session_id) so it stays O(log n) instead of scanning. Because a session's sessions
397	+// row and its sessions_fts row are always written together (upsertOne, one transaction), a
398	+// missing sessions row guarantees a missing FTS row — which lets the live writer set
399	+// Session.IsNew and skip the whole-table FTS delete for genuinely new sessions.
400	+func (s *Store) Exists(agent, sessionID string) (bool, error) {
401	+	var one int
402	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`, agent, sessionID).Scan(&one)
403	+	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
404	+		return false, nil
405	+	}
406	+	if err != nil {
407	+		return false, err
408	+	}
409	+	return true, nil
410	+}
411	+
412	+// Count returns the number of indexed sessions.
413	+func (s *Store) Count() (int, error) {
414	+	var n int
415	+	if err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions`).Scan(&n); err != nil {
416	+		return 0, err
417	+	}
418	+	return n, nil
419	+}
420	+
421	+// ProjectCount returns the number of distinct attributed projects in the index
422	+// (excluding the unknownID bucket).
423	+func (s *Store) ProjectCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
424	+	var n int
425	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT project_id) FROM sessions WHERE project_id != ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
426	+	return n, err
427	+}
428	+
429	+// UnattributedCount returns the number of sessions in the unknownID bucket.
430	+func (s *Store) UnattributedCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
431	+	var n int
432	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
433	+	return n, err
434	+}
435	+
436	+// ListByProject returns a project's sessions, newest activity first. Body is not
437	+// populated (it lives only in the FTS index). Used by the `specstory resume` picker.
438	+func (s *Store) ListByProject(projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
439	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT `+sessionColumns+`
440	+		FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC, created_at DESC`, projectID)
441	+	if err != nil {
442	+		return nil, err
443	+	}
444	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
445	+	return scanSessions(rows)
446	+}
447	+
448	+// ProjectSummary is a rolled-up view of one project for the all-projects picker.
449	+type ProjectSummary struct {
450	+	ProjectID    string
451	+	ProjectName  string
452	+	Sessions     int            // total sessions in the project
453	+	LastActivity string         // most recent updated_at across the project
454	+	AgentCounts  map[string]int // sessions per agent (claude, codex, …)
455	+}
456	+
457	+// ListProjects returns one rolled-up summary per project, most recently active first.
458	+// Used by the all-projects view (date-bucketed). The unknown-project bucket is included;
459	+// the caller decides how to present it.
460	+func (s *Store) ListProjects() ([]ProjectSummary, error) {
461	+	rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT project_id, project_name, agent, COUNT(*), MAX(updated_at)
462	+		FROM sessions GROUP BY project_id, agent`)
463	+	if err != nil {
464	+		return nil, err
465	+	}
466	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
467	+
468	+	byID := map[string]*ProjectSummary{}
469	+	for rows.Next() {
470	+		var pid, pname, agent, last string
471	+		var n int
472	+		if err := rows.Scan(&pid, &pname, &agent, &n, &last); err != nil {
473	+			return nil, err
474	+		}
475	+		ps, ok := byID[pid]
476	+		if !ok {
477	+			ps = &ProjectSummary{ProjectID: pid, ProjectName: pname, AgentCounts: map[string]int{}}
478	+			byID[pid] = ps
479	+		}
480	+		ps.Sessions += n
481	+		ps.AgentCounts[agent] += n
482	+		if ps.ProjectName == "" {
483	+			ps.ProjectName = pname
484	+		}
485	+		if last > ps.LastActivity {
486	+			ps.LastActivity = last
487	+		}
488	+	}
489	+	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
490	+		return nil, err
491	+	}
492	+
493	+	out := make([]ProjectSummary, 0, len(byID))
494	+	for _, ps := range byID {
495	+		out = append(out, *ps)
496	+	}
497	+	sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].LastActivity > out[j].LastActivity })
498	+	return out, nil
499	+}
500	+
501	+// SessionBody returns the full-text conversation body for a session (for the preview
502	+// pane), or "" if the session has no indexed body (e.g. Cursor, metadata-only).
503	+func (s *Store) SessionBody(agent, sessionID string) (string, error) {
504	+	var body string
505	+	// Fast path: resolve the FTS row by the rowid stored on the sessions row (O(1)), instead of
506	+	// scanning the whole FTS (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED).
507	+	err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT f.body FROM sessions s
508	+		JOIN sessions_fts f ON f.rowid = s.fts_rowid
509	+		WHERE s.agent = ? AND s.session_id = ?`, agent, sessionID).Scan(&body)
510	+	if err == nil {
511	+		return body, nil
512	+	}
513	+	if !errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
514	+		return "", err
515	+	}
516	+	// No fts_rowid link (a row written before the column existed). Fall back to the by-key scan;
517	+	// a reindex repopulates fts_rowid and retires this path.
518	+	err = s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT body FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`,
519	+		agent, sessionID).Scan(&body)
520	+	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
521	+		return "", nil
522	+	}
523	+	return body, err
524	+}
525	+
526	+// Search runs a full-text query and returns matching sessions, most recent first.
527	+// projectID scopes the search to one project; "" searches across all projects.
528	+func (s *Store) Search(query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
529	+	return s.SearchContext(context.Background(), query, projectID)
530	+}
531	+
532	+// SearchContext is Search bound to a context. The interactive search cancels the context
533	+// when a newer keystroke arrives, which aborts a slow in-flight query and frees its
534	+// connection. Snippets are deliberately fetched separately for only the visible rows:
535	+// FTS5 snippet generation over hundreds of full transcripts dominates broad searches.
536	+func (s *Store) SearchContext(ctx context.Context, query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
537	+	q := `SELECT ` + prefixed("s", sessionColumns) + `
538	+		FROM sessions_fts
539	+		JOIN sessions s ON s.agent = sessions_fts.agent AND s.session_id = sessions_fts.session_id
540	+		WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ?`
541	+	args := []any{query}
542	+	if projectID != "" {
543	+		q += ` AND s.project_id = ?`
544	+		args = append(args, projectID)
545	+	}
546	+	// Order by recency, not FTS5's BM25 rank. For full-conversation transcripts BM25 is
547	+	// noisy (length-normalization dominates) and a single-term query gets no IDF signal,
548	+	// so relevance order looks arbitrary; newest-first is what's useful when browsing your
549	+	// own history. It also makes LIMIT keep the 500 most RECENT matches (vs the 500 densest)
550	+	// and skips BM25 scoring entirely. updated_at is ISO 8601, so TEXT sort = chronological.
551	+	q += ` ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC LIMIT 500`
552	+
553	+	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
554	+	if err != nil {
555	+		return nil, err
556	+	}
557	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
558	+
559	+	return scanSessions(rows)
560	+}
561	+
562	+// Snippets returns highlighted match snippets for the provided sessions. The result is
563	+// keyed by FingerprintKey(agent, session_id). Matched terms are wrapped in \x02 and \x03
564	+// (STX/ETX) so callers can highlight without colliding with conversation text.
565	+func (s *Store) Snippets(query string, sessions []Session) (map[string]string, error) {
566	+	return s.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), query, sessions)
567	+}
568	+
569	+// SnippetsContext is Snippets bound to a context. Callers should pass only the currently
570	+// visible rows; snippet() is intentionally lazy because it is the expensive part of FTS
571	+// search on long transcripts.
572	+func (s *Store) SnippetsContext(ctx context.Context, query string, sessions []Session) (map[string]string, error) {
573	+	out := map[string]string{}
574	+	if query == "" || len(sessions) == 0 {
575	+		return out, nil
576	+	}
577	+
578	+	clauses := make([]string, 0, len(sessions))
579	+	args := []any{query}
580	+	seen := map[string]bool{}
581	+	for _, sess := range sessions {
582	+		key := FingerprintKey(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
583	+		if seen[key] {
584	+			continue
585	+		}
586	+		seen[key] = true
587	+		clauses = append(clauses, `(agent = ? AND session_id = ?)`)
588	+		args = append(args, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
589	+	}
590	+	if len(clauses) == 0 {
591	+		return out, nil
592	+	}
593	+
594	+	q := `SELECT agent, session_id, snippet(sessions_fts, 3, char(2), char(3), '…', 12)
595	+		FROM sessions_fts
596	+		WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ? AND (` + strings.Join(clauses, ` OR `) + `)`
597	+	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
598	+	if err != nil {
599	+		return nil, err
600	+	}
601	+	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
602	+
603	+	for rows.Next() {
604	+		var agent, sessionID, snippet string
605	+		if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &snippet); err != nil {
606	+			return nil, err
607	+		}
608	+		out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = snippet
609	+	}
610	+	return out, rows.Err()
611	+}
612	+
613	+// prefixed qualifies each comma-separated column in cols with the given table alias,
614	+// e.g. prefixed("s", "a, b") -> "s.a, s.b". Used to disambiguate the sessions columns
615	+// in the FTS join (where session_id/agent also exist on the FTS table).
616	+func prefixed(alias, cols string) string {
617	+	parts := strings.Split(cols, ",")
618	+	for i, p := range parts {
619	+		parts[i] = alias + "." + strings.TrimSpace(p)
620	+	}
621	+	return strings.Join(parts, ", ")
622	+}
623	+
624	+// scanSessions scans rows selected with sessionColumns (in order) into Sessions.
625	+func scanSessions(rows *sql.Rows) ([]Session, error) {
626	+	var out []Session
627	+	for rows.Next() {
628	+		var s Session
629	+		if err := rows.Scan(
630	+			&s.ProjectID, &s.ProjectName, &s.Agent, &s.SessionID, &s.CreatedAt, &s.UpdatedAt,
631	+			&s.UserTurns, &s.TotalTurns, &s.Slug, &s.Name, &s.NativePath, &s.OriginCwd,
632	+			&s.Size, &s.Mtime, &s.IndexVersion, &s.IndexedAt,
633	+		); err != nil {
634	+			return nil, err
635	+		}
636	+		out = append(out, s)
637	+	}
638	+	return out, rows.Err()
639	+}

655	+func newSession(agent, id, projectID, name, body string) Session {
656	+	return Session{
657	+		ProjectID:   projectID,
658	+		ProjectName: "widgets",
659	+		Agent:       agent,
660	+		SessionID:   id,
661	+		CreatedAt:   "2026-06-18T10:00:00Z",
662	+		UpdatedAt:   "2026-06-18T11:00:00Z",
663	+		UserTurns:   3,
664	+		TotalTurns:  9,
665	+		Slug:        "do-the-thing",
666	+		Name:        name,
667	+		NativePath:  "/store/" + id + ".jsonl",
668	+		OriginCwd:   "/repo",
669	+		Size:        1234,
670	+		Mtime:       1700000000000,
671	+		IndexedAt:   "2026-06-18T12:00:00Z",
672	+		Body:        body,
673	+	}
674	+}
675	+
676	+func openTemp(t *testing.T) *Store {
677	+	t.Helper()
678	+	s, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
679	+	if err != nil {
680	+		t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
681	+	}
682	+	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = s.Close() })
683	+	return s
684	+}
685	+
686	+func TestUpsertAndListByProject(t *testing.T) {
687	+	s := openTemp(t)
688	+
689	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth refactor", "rewrote the login flow")); err != nil {
690	+		t.Fatal(err)
691	+	}
692	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Billing migration", "moved billing to stripe")); err != nil {
693	+		t.Fatal(err)
694	+	}
695	+	if err := s.Upsert(newSession("claude", "c2", "proj-b", "Other project", "unrelated work")); err != nil {
696	+		t.Fatal(err)
697	+	}
698	+
699	+	if n, err := s.Count(); err != nil || n != 3 {
700	+		t.Fatalf("Count = %d, %v; want 3", n, err)
701	+	}
702	+
703	+	got, err := s.ListByProject("proj-a")
704	+	if err != nil {
705	+		t.Fatal(err)
706	+	}
707	+	if len(got) != 2 {
708	+		t.Fatalf("ListByProject(proj-a) = %d rows; want 2", len(got))
709	+	}
710	+	for _, sess := range got {
711	+		if sess.ProjectID != "proj-a" {
712	+			t.Errorf("got project_id %q; want proj-a", sess.ProjectID)
713	+		}
714	+		if sess.UserTurns != 3 || sess.TotalTurns != 9 {
715	+			t.Errorf("turn counts not round-tripped: user=%d total=%d", sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns)
716	+		}
717	+	}
718	+}
719	+
720	+func TestSearchFTS(t *testing.T) {
721	+	s := openTemp(t)
722	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Auth refactor", "rewrote the login flow with oauth"))
723	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("codex", "x1", "proj-a", "Billing migration", "moved billing to stripe webhooks"))
724	+
725	+	// Body match
726	+	hits, err := s.Search("oauth", "")
727	+	if err != nil {
728	+		t.Fatal(err)
729	+	}
730	+	if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "c1" {
731	+		t.Fatalf("Search(oauth) = %+v; want only c1", hits)
732	+	}
733	+
734	+	// Name match
735	+	hits, err = s.Search("billing", "")
736	+	if err != nil {
737	+		t.Fatal(err)
738	+	}
739	+	if len(hits) != 1 || hits[0].SessionID != "x1" {
740	+		t.Fatalf("Search(billing) = %+v; want only x1", hits)
741	+	}
742	+}
743	+
744	+func TestUpsertIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
745	+	s := openTemp(t)
746	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "First name", "first body text"))
747	+	// Re-index the same (agent, session_id) with changed content.
748	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Renamed", "second body text"))
749	+
750	+	if n, _ := s.Count(); n != 1 {
751	+		t.Fatalf("Count = %d after re-upsert; want 1 (replaced, not duplicated)", n)
752	+	}
753	+	// Old FTS content must be gone; new content searchable.
754	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("first", ""); len(hits) != 0 {
755	+		t.Errorf("stale FTS row survived re-upsert: %+v", hits)
756	+	}
757	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("second", ""); len(hits) != 1 {
758	+		t.Errorf("new FTS content not searchable after re-upsert")
759	+	}
760	+}
761	+
762	+// TestUpsertHandlesLegacyNullRowid covers the migration window: rows written before fts_rowid
763	+// existed have it NULL. Both the body read and the replace-before-insert must fall back to the
764	+// by-key path for such a row, and a re-index must repopulate fts_rowid without duplicating or
765	+// orphaning the FTS row.
766	+func TestUpsertHandlesLegacyNullRowid(t *testing.T) {
767	+	s := openTemp(t)
768	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Legacy", "old body text"))
769	+
770	+	// Simulate a pre-migration row: clear the fts_rowid link the writer just set.
771	+	if _, err := s.db.Exec(`UPDATE sessions SET fts_rowid = NULL WHERE agent = 'claude' AND session_id = 'c1'`); err != nil {
772	+		t.Fatalf("clear fts_rowid: %v", err)
773	+	}
774	+
775	+	// Body read must fall back to the by-key lookup and still find the body.
776	+	if body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "c1"); err != nil || body != "old body text" {
777	+		t.Errorf("SessionBody (NULL fts_rowid) = %q, %v; want fallback to old body", body, err)
778	+	}
779	+
780	+	// Re-index the same session: with no fts_rowid to seek, the delete must fall back to the
781	+	// by-key path so the stale FTS row is removed rather than duplicated.
782	+	mustUpsert(t, s, newSession("claude", "c1", "proj-a", "Repopulated", "new body text"))
783	+
784	+	if n, _ := s.Count(); n != 1 {
785	+		t.Fatalf("Count = %d after re-upsert of legacy row; want 1", n)
786	+	}
787	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("old", ""); len(hits) != 0 {
788	+		t.Errorf("stale FTS row survived re-upsert of legacy row: %+v", hits)
789	+	}
790	+	if hits, _ := s.Search("new", ""); len(hits) != 1 {
791	+		t.Errorf("new FTS content not searchable after re-upsert of legacy row")
792	+	}
793	+	// fts_rowid is now repopulated, so the body read takes the fast path again.
794	+	var rowid sql.NullInt64
795	+	if err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT fts_rowid FROM sessions WHERE agent = 'claude' AND session_id = 'c1'`).Scan(&rowid); err != nil {
796	+		t.Fatal(err)
797	+	}
798	+	if !rowid.Valid {
799	+		t.Error("fts_rowid still NULL after re-upsert; want repopulated")
800	+	}
801	+	if body, err := s.SessionBody("claude", "c1"); err != nil || body != "new body text" {
802	+		t.Errorf("SessionBody after repopulate = %q, %v; want new body via fast path", body, err)
803	+	}
804	+}

1018	+// caseInsensitiveFilesystem reports whether the host OS treats paths case-insensitively.
1019	+// macOS (APFS/HFS+) and Windows (NTFS) are case-insensitive/case-preserving; Linux is
1020	+// case-sensitive. It is a var so tests can exercise both behaviors. Windows is included
1021	+// even though the app is built only for macOS/Linux today: the in-flight Windows support
1022	+// lives on a branch that does not carry this file, so folding case here keeps that branch
1023	+// from silently reintroducing case-divergent workspace ids when it merges.
1024	+var caseInsensitiveFilesystem = runtime.GOOS == "darwin" || runtime.GOOS == "windows"
1025	+
1026	+// canonicalizeWorkspacePath normalizes a path before it is hashed into a workspace_id. On
1027	+// a case-insensitive filesystem the same directory can be named with different casing,
1028	+// which would otherwise hash to different ids for one physical directory; case-folding
1029	+// makes the id stable. On case-sensitive filesystems the path is left byte-exact, since
1030	+// there two differently-cased paths are genuinely different directories.
1031	+func canonicalizeWorkspacePath(path string) string {
1032	+	if caseInsensitiveFilesystem {
1033	+		return strings.ToLower(path)
1034	+	}
1035	+	return path
1036	 }

1095	+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1096	+// Walk-up identity resolution (shared by ComputeProjectID and the writer).
1097	+//
1098	+// The stored .specstory/.project.json fragments a monorepo: it is written per
1099	+// launch directory, and generateGitID looks for .git at exactly that directory and
1100	+// never walks up — so a session launched from a subdirectory gets no git_id and a
1101	+// path-based workspace_id all its own. The resolver below instead walks UP to the
1102	+// git root and computes identity from there, so every directory inside one repo
1103	+// resolves to a single project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
1104	+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1105	+
1106	+// originRemoteURLRegex captures the origin remote's url value from a git config file.
1107	+var originRemoteURLRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`\[remote "origin"\][^\[]*url\s*=\s*([^\n\r]+)`)
1108	+
1109	+// readOriginURL returns the origin remote URL from a git config file, or "" if the
1110	+// file is unreadable or has no origin remote.
1111	+func readOriginURL(gitConfigPath string) string {
1112	+	gitConfig, err := os.ReadFile(gitConfigPath)
1113	+	if err != nil {
1114	+		return ""
1115	+	}
1116	+	matches := originRemoteURLRegex.FindSubmatch(gitConfig)
1117	+	if len(matches) < 2 {
1118	+		return ""
1119	+	}
1120	+	return strings.TrimSpace(string(matches[1]))
1121	+}
1122	+
1123	+// findGitRoot walks up from dir to the nearest ancestor containing a .git entry and
1124	+// returns it. The .git entry may be a directory (normal clone) or a FILE (git
1125	+// worktree / submodule); both satisfy the search. Returns false if no .git is found
1126	+// before the filesystem root. The walk-up is what lets a session launched from a
1127	+// monorepo subdirectory resolve to the repo's identity.
1128	+func findGitRoot(dir string) (string, bool) {
1129	+	if abs, err := filepath.Abs(dir); err == nil {
1130	+		dir = abs
1131	+	}
1132	+	dir = filepath.Clean(dir)
1133	+	for {
1134	+		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, ".git")); err == nil {
1135	+			return dir, true
1136	+		}
1137	+		parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
1138	+		if parent == dir {
1139	+			return "", false // reached the filesystem root without finding .git
1140	+		}
1141	+		dir = parent
1142	+	}
1143	+}
1144	+
1145	+// resolveGitConfigPath returns the path to the git config file that holds a root's
1146	+// remotes, handling both a normal `.git` directory and a `.git` FILE (worktrees /
1147	+// submodules, whose private gitdir points at a commondir that holds the shared
1148	+// config). Returns "" when it cannot be resolved.
1149	+func resolveGitConfigPath(gitRoot string) string {
1150	+	gitPath := filepath.Join(gitRoot, ".git")
1151	+	info, err := os.Stat(gitPath)
1152	+	if err != nil {
1153	+		return ""
1154	+	}
1155	+	if info.IsDir() {
1156	+		return filepath.Join(gitPath, "config")
1157	+	}
1158	+
1159	+	// `.git` is a file of the form "gitdir: <path>" pointing at the worktree's private
1160	+	// git dir; the shared config lives in the common dir, not that private dir.
1161	+	data, err := os.ReadFile(gitPath)
1162	+	if err != nil {
1163	+		return ""
1164	+	}
1165	+	content := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
1166	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(content, "gitdir:") {
1167	+		return ""
1168	+	}
1169	+	gitDir := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(content, "gitdir:"))
1170	+	if gitDir == "" {
1171	+		return ""
1172	+	}
1173	+	if !filepath.IsAbs(gitDir) {
1174	+		gitDir = filepath.Join(gitRoot, gitDir)
1175	+	}
1176	+	commonDir := gitDir
1177	+	if cd, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(gitDir, "commondir")); err == nil {
1178	+		c := strings.TrimSpace(string(cd))
1179	+		if !filepath.IsAbs(c) {
1180	+			c = filepath.Join(gitDir, c)
1181	+		}
1182	+		commonDir = filepath.Clean(c)
1183	+	}

333	// processTemplate processes the default config template for a given level
334	// ("user" or "project"). Template markers {u ...} and {p ...} delimit content
335	// specific to user-level or project-level config files respectively.
336	//
337	// For level "user": content inside {u ...} is kept (markers stripped),
338	// content inside {p ...} is removed entirely.
339	// For level "project": the opposite applies.
340	//
341	// Markers can span multiple lines. The opening marker ({u or {p) appears at
342	// the start of a line (after optional whitespace/comment prefix), and the
343	// closing } appears at the end of a line.
344	func processTemplate(template, level string) string {
345		var result strings.Builder
346		keepTag := "{u"
347		stripTag := "{p"
348		if level == "project" {
349			keepTag = "{p"
350			stripTag = "{u"
351		}
352	
353		insideKeep := false
354		insideStrip := false
355	
356		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(template, "\n") {
357			trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
358			// Remove any leading "# " to inspect the marker
359			bare := strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "# ")
360	
361			switch {
362			case insideStrip:
363				// Check for closing brace — ends the stripped block
364				if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
365					insideStrip = false
366				}
367				// Drop the entire line regardless
368				continue
369

1187	+// resolveIdentity computes a project's identity from a starting directory by walking
1188	+// up to the git root. It reads only git metadata — never any .specstory/.project.json
1189	+// — so it cannot be fooled by the per-directory identity files that fragment a
1190	+// monorepo. Returns the git_id (empty when there is no resolvable origin remote), the
1191	+// workspace_id (hash of the resolved root), the project name, and the resolved root.
1192	+func resolveIdentity(startDir string) (gitID, workspaceID, name, root string) {
1193	+	root = startDir
1194	+	if gr, ok := findGitRoot(startDir); ok {
1195	+		root = gr
1196	+	}
1197	+
1198	+	// A zero-state manager rooted at the resolved root reuses the existing hashing /
1199	+	// normalization / repo-name helpers (they do not depend on manager state).
1200	+	rooted := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: root}
1201	+	workspaceID = rooted.generateWorkspaceID()
1202	+	if cfg := resolveGitConfigPath(root); cfg != "" {
1203	+		if originURL := readOriginURL(cfg); originURL != "" {
1204	+			gitID = rooted.createHash(rooted.normalizeGitURL(originURL))
1205	+			name = rooted.parseGitRemoteURL(originURL)
1206	+		}
1207	+	}
1208	+	if name == "" {
1209	+		name = filepath.Base(root)
1210	+	}
1211	+	return gitID, workspaceID, name, root
1212	+}
1213	+
1214	+// ComputeProjectID resolves the restore-index project identity for a session's
1215	+// working directory. It walks up to the git root and computes identity fresh,
1216	+// ignoring any stored .specstory/.project.json (which fragments a monorepo) and
1217	+// never writing one. Returns the project id (git_id when a remote is resolvable,
1218	+// else the path-based workspace_id) and the project name.
1219	+func ComputeProjectID(cwd string) (id, name string, err error) {
1220	+	if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
1221	+		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve project id: empty working directory")
1222	+	}
1223	+	gitID, workspaceID, name, _ := resolveIdentity(cwd)
1224	+
1225	+	// Prefer a resolvable git_id: it hashes the normalized remote URL, not a path, so it is
1226	+	// immune to the case divergence handled below and never fragments a monorepo.
1227	+	if gitID != "" {
1228	+		return gitID, name, nil
1229	+	}
1230	+
1231	+	// No git remote → use the path-based workspace_id. Prefer an id already persisted in the
1232	+	// launch dir's .project.json so every consumer (reindex, resume, cloud sync) agrees on a
1233	+	// single value, even for projects whose file predates path canonicalization. Only when no
1234	+	// file exists (e.g. a portable session whose recorded cwd is not present locally) do we
1235	+	// fall back to the freshly computed — and now canonical — hash.
1236	+	if stored := persistedWorkspaceID(cwd); stored != "" {
1237	+		return stored, name, nil
1238	+	}
1239	+	if workspaceID == "" {
1240	+		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot resolve project id for %q", cwd)
1241	+	}
1242	+	return workspaceID, name, nil
1243	+}
1244	+
1245	+// persistedWorkspaceID returns the workspace_id recorded in dir's .specstory/.project.json,
1246	+// or "" when there is no readable file or it carries no workspace_id. The file is read from
1247	+// the launch directory (where the writer puts it), not the resolved git root.
1248	+func persistedWorkspaceID(dir string) string {
1249	+	m := &ProjectIdentityManager{projectRoot: dir}
1250	+	identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
1251	+	if err != nil || identity == nil {
1252	+		return ""
1253	+	}
1254	+	return identity.WorkspaceID
1255	+}

177		absPath, err := filepath.Abs(m.projectRoot)
178		if err != nil {
179			slog.Warn("Failed to get absolute path, using relative path", "error", err)
180			absPath = m.projectRoot
181		}
182	
183		// The TypeScript version hashes the workspace URI, but in our case
184		// we'll hash the absolute path for compatibility. The path is canonicalized first so
185		// the same physical directory yields one id regardless of the casing a caller passes
186		// (os.Getwd may echo a lowercase $PWD; a session's recorded cwd may differ in case).
187		return m.createHash(canonicalizeWorkspacePath(absPath))
188	}
189	
190	// caseInsensitiveFilesystem reports whether the host OS treats paths case-insensitively.
191	// macOS (APFS/HFS+) and Windows (NTFS) are case-insensitive/case-preserving; Linux is
192	// case-sensitive. It is a var so tests can exercise both behaviors. Windows is included
193	// even though the app is built only for macOS/Linux today: the in-flight Windows support
194	// lives on a branch that does not carry this file, so folding case here keeps that branch
195	// from silently reintroducing case-divergent workspace ids when it merges.
196	var caseInsensitiveFilesystem = runtime.GOOS == "darwin" || runtime.GOOS == "windows"
197	
198	// canonicalizeWorkspacePath normalizes a path before it is hashed into a workspace_id. On
199	// a case-insensitive filesystem the same directory can be named with different casing,
200	// which would otherwise hash to different ids for one physical directory; case-folding
201	// makes the id stable. On case-sensitive filesystems the path is left byte-exact, since
202	// there two differently-cased paths are genuinely different directories.
203	func canonicalizeWorkspacePath(path string) string {
204		if caseInsensitiveFilesystem {
205			return strings.ToLower(path)
206		}
207		return path
208	}
209	
210	// generateGitID generates a git ID by finding and hashing the git origin URL
211	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) generateGitID() (string, error) {
212		gitConfigPath := filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, ".git", "config")
213	
214		// Check if git config exists
215		if _, err := os.Stat(gitConfigPath); err != nil {
216			return "", fmt.Errorf("no git config found: %w", err)
217		}
218	
219		// Read the origin remote URL from the config (shared with the walk-up resolver).
220		originURL := readOriginURL(gitConfigPath)
221		if originURL == "" {
222			return "", fmt.Errorf("no origin URL found in git config")
223		}
224	
225		// Normalize git URLs to ensure HTTPS and SSH URLs for the same repo generate the same ID
226		normalizedURL := m.normalizeGitURL(originURL)
227	
228		return m.createHash(normalizedURL), nil
229	}
230	
231	// normalizeGitURL normalizes a git URL to ensure HTTPS and SSH URLs for the same repo generate the same ID
232	// It extracts the host and repository path, normalizing different URL formats to a common form
233	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) normalizeGitURL(gitURL string) string {
234		// Remove .git suffix if present
235		normalized := strings.TrimSuffix(gitURL, ".git")
236	
237		// Handle SSH format (git@host:owner/repo or user@host:owner/repo)
238		if strings.Contains(normalized, "@") && strings.Contains(normalized, ":") {
239			// Split by @ to get the host part
240			parts := strings.SplitN(normalized, "@", 2)
241			if len(parts) == 2 {
242				// Split by : to separate host from path
243				hostAndPath := strings.SplitN(parts[1], ":", 2)
244				if len(hostAndPath) == 2 {
245					// Return normalized as "host/path"
246					return hostAndPath[0] + "/" + hostAndPath[1]
247				}
248			}
249		}
