```text
27	const (
28		// unknownProjectID buckets sessions whose originating cwd could not be resolved
29		// (a few Claude warmups; all Cursor sessions until the deferred cwd recovery lands).
30		unknownProjectID = "unknown"
31	
32		// reindexWriteBatch is how many sessions the writer goroutine commits per transaction.
33		reindexWriteBatch = 200
34	
35		// reindexVersion is the logic version stamped on every indexed row. Bump it whenever
36		// the parse/flatten/derivation logic changes so the next reindex re-parses everything
37		// even when files are unchanged. It is part of the freshness fingerprint.
38		//   2: skip slash-command noise when choosing a Claude session title (fixes UUID titles)
39		//   3: also skip "[Request interrupted by user…]" markers
40		//   4: canonicalize (case-fold) the workspace_id path hash + prefer a persisted
41		//      .project.json workspace_id, so a project's id no longer varies with path casing
42		//   5: parse Codex bodies from the enumerated NativePath (was a by-id tree search), so a
43		//      session's body and its freshness fingerprint now derive from the same file
44		//   6: populate sessions.fts_rowid (the O(1) link to each session's FTS row) — existing
45		//      rows have it NULL until re-parsed, so bump to repopulate the whole index once
46		reindexVersion = 6
47	)
48	
49	// CreateReindexCommand builds the `specstory reindex` command: a full, from-scratch
50	// rebuild of the restore index (~/.specstory/sessions.db) of every coding-agent session
51	// SpecStory can find, across all projects and providers. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
52	func CreateReindexCommand() *cobra.Command {
53		cmd := &cobra.Command{
54			Use:   "reindex",
55			Short: "Rebuild the restore index of all known agent sessions",
56			Long: `Rebuild the restore index used by 'specstory resume'.
57	
58	'reindex' enumerates every session across all installed agents and projects and writes a
59	searchable index to ~/.specstory/sessions.db. It is incremental: a session whose native
60	file is unchanged since it was last indexed is skipped, so re-runs are fast. Use --force to
61	re-index everything regardless. The index is a derived cache: it is safe to delete.`,
62			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
63			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
64				force, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
65				return runReindex(force)
66			},
67		}
68		cmd.Flags().Bool("force", false, "re-index every session even if unchanged")
69		return cmd
70	}
71	
72	// reindexItem is one unit of work: a session to parse and index. size/mtime are the
73	// native file's stat, captured during the freshness check and reused (no re-stat).
74	type reindexItem struct {
75		agent string
76		prov  spi.Provider
77		ref   spi.GlobalSessionRef
78		size  int64
79		mtime int64
80		isNew bool // no existing index row → writer can skip the standalone-FTS delete
81	}
82	
83	func runReindex(force bool) error {
84		start := time.Now()
85	
86		// Graceful Ctrl+C: stop feeding work, flush what's written, exit cleanly.
87		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
88		defer cancel()
89	
90		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
91		if err != nil {
92			return err
93		}
94		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
95		if err != nil {
96			return fmt.Errorf("opening restore index: %w", err)
97		}
98		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
99	
100		registry := factory.GetRegistry()
101	
102		// ---- Phase 1: enumerate every provider concurrently, then dedup ----
103		ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, true)
104	
105		// Existing fingerprints, so unchanged sessions can be skipped (the incremental path).
106		fingerprints := map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{}
107		if !force {
108			if fps, err := store.Fingerprints(); err != nil {
109				slog.Warn("reindex: could not load fingerprints, re-indexing all", "error", err)
110			} else {
111				fingerprints = fps
112			}
113		}
114	
115		best, order, foundPerAgent := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
116		found := len(order)
117		cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
118		work, totals, unchanged := selectWork(order, best, fingerprints, "", force, cache)
119	
120		if found == 0 {
121			fprintln(os.Stderr, "No agent sessions found to index.")
122			return nil
123		}
124		fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   Found %d sessions  ·  %s\n", found, summarizeCounts(ids, foundPerAgent))
125		if len(work) == 0 {
126			fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   All %d sessions already up to date.  (%.1fs)\n", found, time.Since(start).Seconds())
127			return nil
128		}
129		if unchanged > 0 {
130			fprintf(os.Stderr, "    %d unchanged · indexing %d…\n\n", unchanged, len(work))
131		} else {
132			fprintln(os.Stderr, "")
133		}
134	
135		// ---- Phase 2+3: parse (worker pool) → write (single goroutine), with live progress ----
136		prog := newReindexProgress(ids, totals)
137		prog.begin()
138		indexedAt := start.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
139		writeErr := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12), prog)
140		prog.end()
141	
142		if writeErr != nil {
143			return fmt.Errorf("writing restore index: %w", writeErr)
144		}
145	
146		// ---- Summary (counts reflect the WHOLE index, not just this run's work) ----
147		elapsed := time.Since(start)
148		projects, _ := store.ProjectCount(unknownProjectID)
149		unattributed, _ := store.UnattributedCount(unknownProjectID)
150		printReindexSummary(ids, foundPerAgent, dbPath, elapsed, prog.totalDone(), unchanged, projects, unattributed)
151		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventReindexCompleted, analytics.Properties{
152			"sessions":     len(work),
153			"unchanged":    unchanged,
154			"projects":     projects,
155			"unattributed": unattributed,
156			"duration_ms":  elapsed.Milliseconds(),
157		})
158		return nil
159	}
160	
161	// ---- reindex engine (shared by the foreground command and the background warm) ----
162	
163	// enumerateAll concurrently lists every installed provider's sessions. The returned slices
164	// are index-aligned: provs[i]/perProvider[i] correspond to ids[i] (a nil provs[i] marks a
165	// provider that failed to load). When visible, it renders a live "Scanning agents…" line
166	// (foreground reindex); the background warm passes visible=false and stays silent.
167	func enumerateAll(registry *factory.Registry, visible bool) (ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) {
168		ids = registry.ListIDs()
169		perProvider = make([][]spi.GlobalSessionRef, len(ids))
170		provs = make([]spi.Provider, len(ids))
171	
172		var scan *scanProgress
173		if visible {
174			scan = newScanProgress(ids)
175			scan.begin()
176			defer scan.end()
177		}
178	
179		var ewg sync.WaitGroup
180		for i, id := range ids {
181			prov, err := registry.Get(id)
182			if err != nil {
183				continue
184			}
185			provs[i] = prov
186			reporter := scan.reporterFor(id)
187			ewg.Add(1)
188			go func(i int, id string, prov spi.Provider, reporter *spi.ScanReporter) {
189				defer ewg.Done()
190				var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
191				var err error
192				// Providers that can report progress (Codex, the long pole) tick the live line; the
193				// rest snap to their final count via markDone when they finish.
194				if pe, ok := prov.(spi.ProgressEnumerator); ok {
195					refs, err = pe.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(reporter)
196				} else {
197					refs, err = prov.ListAllAgentChatSessions()
198				}
199				if err != nil {
200					slog.Warn("reindex: enumeration failed", "provider", id, "error", err)
201				}
202				perProvider[i] = refs
203				scan.markDone(id, len(refs))
204			}(i, id, prov, reporter)
205		}
206		ewg.Wait()
207		return ids, provs, perProvider
208	}
209	
210	// dedupRefs collapses enumerated refs by (agent, session_id), keeping the freshest file. A
211	// resumed session can span MULTIPLE native files sharing one id; they all map to one
212	// (agent, session_id) row, so without dedup the duplicates ping-pong — each run, one file's
213	// stat never matches the fingerprint the other file just stored, re-indexing both forever.
214	// Keeping the freshest by mtime makes the fingerprint stable. Sessions with no native id are
215	// dropped (unresumable, and would collide on the PK). order is first-seen order (deterministic
216	// processing); foundPerAgent is the distinct-session count per agent (for the "Found" line).
217	func dedupRefs(ids []string, provs []spi.Provider, perProvider [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef) (best map[string]reindexItem, order []string, foundPerAgent map[string]int) {
218		best = map[string]reindexItem{}
219		foundPerAgent = map[string]int{}
220		for i, id := range ids {
221			if provs[i] == nil {
222				continue
223			}
224			for _, ref := range perProvider[i] {
225				if strings.TrimSpace(ref.SessionID) == "" {
226					slog.Debug("reindex: skipping session with no id", "agent", id, "path", ref.NativePath)
227					continue
228				}
229				size, mtime := statNative(ref.NativePath)
230				item := reindexItem{agent: id, prov: provs[i], ref: ref, size: size, mtime: mtime}
231				key := sessionindex.FingerprintKey(id, ref.SessionID)
232				if cur, ok := best[key]; ok {
233					if mtime > cur.mtime {
234						best[key] = item
235					}
236					continue
237				}
238				best[key] = item
239				order = append(order, key)
240			}
241		}
242		for _, key := range order {
243			foundPerAgent[best[key].agent]++
244		}
245		return best, order, foundPerAgent
246	}
247	
248	// selectWork applies the incremental fingerprint skip (and an optional project filter) to the
249	// deduped set. projectFilter "" means all projects; otherwise only sessions whose originating
250	// cwd resolves to that project id are kept (used by the background warm's current-project
251	// pass). totals is the per-agent to-do count (drives the foreground progress bars).
252	func selectWork(order []string, best map[string]reindexItem, fingerprints map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint, projectFilter string, force bool, cache *projectIDCache) (work []reindexItem, totals map[string]int, unchanged int) {
253		totals = map[string]int{}
254		for _, key := range order {
255			item := best[key]
256			if projectFilter != "" {
257				if pid, _ := cache.resolve(item.ref.OriginCwd); pid != projectFilter {
258					continue
259				}
260			}
261			fp, ok := fingerprints[key]
262			if ok && fp.Size == item.size && fp.Mtime == item.mtime && fp.Version == reindexVersion {
263				unchanged++
264				continue // already indexed and unchanged
265			}
266			// A session absent from the fingerprint set has no sessions row, hence no sessions_fts
267			// row either (the pair is written atomically) — so the writer can skip the O(rows)
268			// standalone-FTS delete. Under --force the fingerprint map is deliberately empty, so
269			// absence proves nothing; keep the delete to avoid duplicate FTS rows.
270			item.isNew = !force && !ok
271			work = append(work, item)
272			totals[item.agent]++
273		}
274		return work, totals, unchanged
275	}
276	
277	// reindexReporter receives per-session progress from processWork. The foreground command
278	// passes *reindexProgress (live bars); the background warm passes nopReporter (silent).
279	type reindexReporter interface {
280		observe(projectID string)
281		inc(agent string)
282	}
283	
284	// nopReporter is the silent reporter used by the background warm.
285	type nopReporter struct{}
286	
287	func (nopReporter) observe(string) {}
288	func (nopReporter) inc(string)     {}
289	
290	// processWork parses each work item (worker pool) and writes the results to the store via a
291	// single writer goroutine, reporting progress through rep. It stops feeding work when ctx is
292	// cancelled; the writer flushes what it already received. Returns the writer's error, if any.
293	func processWork(ctx context.Context, store *sessionindex.Store, work []reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string, workers int, rep reindexReporter) error {
294		if len(work) == 0 {
295			return nil
296		}
297		if workers < 1 {
298			workers = 1
299		}
300	
301		// Schedule the largest sessions first. A few huge Codex files (hundreds of MB) would
302		// otherwise be end-of-run stragglers that leave most worker cores idle; starting them
303		// first overlaps them with the long tail of small sessions. Order does not affect the
304		// resulting index.
305		sort.SliceStable(work, func(i, j int) bool { return work[i].size > work[j].size })
306	
307		sessionsCh := make(chan sessionindex.Session, 256)
308		var writeErr error
309		writerDone := make(chan struct{})
310		go func() {
311			defer close(writerDone)
312			writeErr = drainToStore(store, sessionsCh)
313		}()
314	
315		workCh := make(chan reindexItem)
316		var wwg sync.WaitGroup
317		for w := 0; w < workers; w++ {
318			wwg.Add(1)
319			go func() {
320				defer wwg.Done()
321				for item := range workCh {
322					sess := buildSession(item, cache, indexedAt)
323					rep.observe(sess.ProjectID)
324					sessionsCh <- sess
325					rep.inc(item.agent)
326				}
327			}()
328		}
329	
330		for _, item := range work {
331			select {
332			case <-ctx.Done():
333				goto closing
334			case workCh <- item:
335			}
336		}
337	closing:
338		close(workCh)
339		wwg.Wait()
340		close(sessionsCh)
341		<-writerDone
342		return writeErr
343	}
344	
345	// warmIndexInBackground keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db warm as a side effect of resume/search.
346	// It runs two SILENT incremental passes over its own writer handle: the current project first
347	// (so its rows refresh quickly, and the open TUI re-queries via onProjectWarmed), then the full
348	// corpus. It enumerates once and reuses the result for both passes. It stops promptly when ctx
349	// is cancelled (the TUI exited); an abandoned write batch is safe under WAL. Errors are logged
350	// at debug and never surfaced — warmth is best-effort.
351	func warmIndexInBackground(ctx context.Context, dbPath, currentProjectID string, onProjectWarmed func()) {
352		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
353		if err != nil {
354			slog.Debug("warm: could not open index", "error", err)
355			return
356		}
357		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
358	
359		registry := factory.GetRegistry()
360		ids, provs, perProvider := enumerateAll(registry, false)
361		if ctx.Err() != nil {
362			return
363		}
364		best, order, _ := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
365		cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
366		indexedAt := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
367	
368		// Gentle: fewer workers than a foreground reindex, since the user is interacting.
369		workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 4)
370	
371		// Pass 1 — the current project, so its rows are fresh almost immediately.
372		fps, _ := store.Fingerprints()
373		work, _, _ := selectWork(order, best, fps, currentProjectID, false, cache)
374		if err := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, workers, nopReporter{}); err != nil {
375			slog.Debug("warm: current-project pass failed", "error", err)
376		}
377		if ctx.Err() != nil {
378			return
379		}
380		if onProjectWarmed != nil {
381			onProjectWarmed() // let the open TUI re-query the (now fresh) current project
382		}
383	
384		// Pass 2 — everything else. Reload fingerprints so pass-1's writes now register as
385		// unchanged and are skipped, leaving only the rest of the corpus to parse.
386		fps, _ = store.Fingerprints()
387		work, _, _ = selectWork(order, best, fps, "", false, cache)
388		if err := processWork(ctx, store, work, cache, indexedAt, workers, nopReporter{}); err != nil {
389			slog.Debug("warm: full pass failed", "error", err)
390		}
391	}
392	
393	// ---- real-time indexing (watch / run / resume launch) ----
394	
395	// LiveIndexer keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db current in real time, alongside the markdown
396	// files that watch/run/resume already write as a session changes. It holds one writer handle
397	// for the command's lifetime and upserts a single row per detected update, reusing the same
398	// content derivation as reindex. The project is resolved once (a watch/run has a fixed cwd).
399	//
400	// Everything is best-effort: real-time indexing must never disrupt the agent or the markdown
401	// write, so construction failures yield a nil *LiveIndexer (whose methods are no-ops) and
402	// per-update errors are logged at debug. A live row carries no native-file fingerprint
403	// (Size/Mtime/NativePath are unknown without enumerating), so the next reindex re-verifies and
404	// rewrites it with a stable fingerprint — the live row is correct and queryable in the interim.
405	type LiveIndexer struct {
406		store       *sessionindex.Store
407		cwd         string
408		projectID   string
409		projectName string
410		indexedAt   string
411	
412		mu       sync.Mutex
413		lastSeen map[string]string // session id -> last indexed UpdatedAt, to skip no-op upserts
414	}
415	
416	// NewLiveIndexer opens the index for writing and resolves the project for cwd. It returns nil
417	// (not an error) when the index can't be opened, so the caller keeps running with real-time
418	// indexing simply disabled. The caller owns Close.
419	func NewLiveIndexer(cwd string) *LiveIndexer {
420		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
421		if err != nil {
422			slog.Debug("live index: no db path; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
423			return nil
424		}
425		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
426		if err != nil {
427			slog.Debug("live index: cannot open index; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
428			return nil
429		}
430		projectID, projectName, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
431		if err != nil || projectID == "" {
432			projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, ""
433		}
434		return &LiveIndexer{
435			store:       store,
436			cwd:         cwd,
437			projectID:   projectID,
438			projectName: projectName,
439			indexedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
440			lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
441		}
442	}
443	
444	// Record upserts the live session into the index. agentID is the provider's registry id (e.g.
445	// "claude"). It skips the write when the session's last activity hasn't advanced since the
446	// previous record (the watcher can fire repeatedly without new content). Safe on a nil
447	// receiver, and serialized so concurrent provider watchers don't contend on the single writer.
448	func (li *LiveIndexer) Record(agentID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
449		if li == nil || sess == nil || sess.SessionData == nil {
450			return
451		}
452		data := sess.SessionData
453	
454		updatedAt := sess.CreatedAt
455		if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
456			updatedAt = ts
457		}
458	
459		li.mu.Lock()
460		defer li.mu.Unlock()
461	
462		// Nothing advanced since the last write → skip the (FTS-heavy) upsert.
463		if updatedAt != "" && li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] == updatedAt {
464			return
465		}
466	
467		// Skip the whole-table FTS delete (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED, so it scans the entire
468		// FTS) when this session has no existing row to replace. Only the first write of a session
469		// in this process can be new, and even then only if it isn't already in the index from a
470		// prior run — so we check by primary key just once, before lastSeen records the write.
471		isNew := false
472		if _, written := li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID]; !written {
473			if exists, err := li.store.Exists(agentID, sess.SessionID); err == nil {
474				isNew = !exists
475			}
476		}
477	
478		userTurns, totalTurns := countTurns(data)
479		row := sessionindex.Session{
480			ProjectID:    li.projectID,
481			ProjectName:  li.projectName,
482			Agent:        agentID,
483			SessionID:    sess.SessionID,
484			CreatedAt:    sess.CreatedAt,
485			UpdatedAt:    updatedAt,
486			UserTurns:    userTurns,
487			TotalTurns:   totalTurns,
488			Slug:         sess.Slug,
489			OriginCwd:    li.cwd,
490			IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
491			IndexedAt:    li.indexedAt,
492			Body:         flattenBody(data),
493			IsNew:        isNew,
494		}
495		if err := li.store.Upsert(row); err != nil {
496			slog.Debug("live index: upsert failed", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", err)
497			return
498		}
499		li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] = updatedAt
500	}
501	
502	// Close releases the writer handle. Safe on a nil receiver.
503	func (li *LiveIndexer) Close() {
504		if li == nil {
505			return
506		}
507		if err := li.store.Close(); err != nil {
508			slog.Debug("live index: close failed", "error", err)
509		}
510	}
511	
512	// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
513	// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
514	// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
515	// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
516	// dropped.
517	func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) sessionindex.Session {
518		ref := item.ref
519		projectID, projectName := cache.resolve(ref.OriginCwd)
520	
521		sess := sessionindex.Session{
522			ProjectID:    projectID,
523			ProjectName:  projectName,
524			Agent:        item.agent,
525			SessionID:    ref.SessionID,
526			CreatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt,
527			UpdatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt, // default; overwritten by last-turn timestamp below
528			Slug:         ref.Slug,
529			Name:         ref.Name,
530			NativePath:   ref.NativePath,
531			OriginCwd:    ref.OriginCwd,
532			Size:         item.size, // captured during the freshness check (no re-stat)
533			Mtime:        item.mtime,
534			IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
535			IndexedAt:    indexedAt,
536			IsNew:        item.isNew,
537		}
538	
539		if ref.OriginCwd == "" {
540			return sess // cannot re-locate without a cwd → metadata-only
541		}
542		full, err := parseFullSession(item.prov, ref)
543		if err != nil || full == nil || full.SessionData == nil {
544			slog.Debug("reindex: full parse unavailable, indexing metadata only",
545				"agent", item.agent, "session", ref.SessionID, "error", err)
546			return sess
547		}
548	
549		data := full.SessionData
550		sess.Body = flattenBody(data)
551		sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns = countTurns(data)
552		if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
553			sess.UpdatedAt = ts
554		}
555		return sess
556	}
557	
558	// parseFullSession reads a session's full data for indexing. It prefers a provider's
559	// path-keyed parse (spi.PathSessionReader) using the ref's already-known NativePath, which
560	// avoids the provider's by-id discovery search — for Codex that search walks the entire
561	// ~/.codex/sessions tree, so this is the difference between an O(N) and an O(N²) reindex.
562	// Providers without the capability (or refs lacking a NativePath) fall back to the by-id
563	// lookup. Parsing the known NativePath is also more consistent: the row's freshness
564	// fingerprint is stat'd from NativePath, so the body now comes from that same file.
565	func parseFullSession(prov spi.Provider, ref spi.GlobalSessionRef) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
566		if pr, ok := prov.(spi.PathSessionReader); ok && ref.NativePath != "" {
567			return pr.GetAgentChatSessionByPath(ref.NativePath, ref.OriginCwd, false)
568		}
569		return prov.GetAgentChatSession(ref.OriginCwd, ref.SessionID, false)
570	}
571	
572	// drainToStore writes parsed sessions to the index in batched transactions.
573	func drainToStore(store *sessionindex.Store, ch <-chan sessionindex.Session) error {
574		batch := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, reindexWriteBatch)
575		flush := func() error {
576			if err := store.UpsertBatch(batch); err != nil {
577				return err
578			}
579			batch = batch[:0]
580			return nil
581		}
582		for sess := range ch {
583			batch = append(batch, sess)
584			if len(batch) >= reindexWriteBatch {
585				if err := flush(); err != nil {
586					return err
587				}
588			}
589		}
590		return flush()
591	}
592	
593	// ---- session field derivation ----
594	
595	// flattenBody renders SessionData to plain user/agent text for full-text indexing,
596	// reusing the reconstruction flattener (synthetic noise already stripped). No
597	// migration note is prepended — this is index content, not a resumed transcript.
598	func flattenBody(data *schema.SessionData) string {
599		turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
600		var b strings.Builder
601		for _, t := range turns {
602			if t.Text == "" {
603				continue
604			}
605			if b.Len() > 0 {
606				b.WriteString("\n\n")
607			}
608			b.WriteString(t.Text)
609		}
610		return b.String()
611	}
612	
613	// countTurns returns (user prompts, all messages) across the session's exchanges.
614	func countTurns(data *schema.SessionData) (userTurns, totalTurns int) {
615		for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
616			for _, m := range ex.Messages {
617				totalTurns++
618				if m.Role == schema.RoleUser {
619					userTurns++
620				}
621			}
622		}
623		return userTurns, totalTurns
624	}
625	
626	// lastTimestamp returns the last non-empty message timestamp in the session, or "".
627	func lastTimestamp(data *schema.SessionData) string {
628		last := ""
629		for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
630			for _, m := range ex.Messages {
631				if m.Timestamp != "" {
632					last = m.Timestamp
633				}
634			}
635		}
636		return last
637	}
638	
639	// statNative returns the native file's size (bytes) and mtime (epoch ms), or zeros.
640	func statNative(path string) (size, mtimeMs int64) {
641		info, err := os.Stat(path)
642		if err != nil {
643			return 0, 0
644		}
645		return info.Size(), info.ModTime().UnixMilli()
646	}
647	
648	// ---- project identity cache (cwd -> project id/name) ----
649	
650	type projectIDName struct{ id, name string }
651	
652	// projectIDCache memoizes ComputeProjectID by cwd, since many sessions share a cwd and
653	// each resolution walks the filesystem to the git root.
654	type projectIDCache struct {
655		mu sync.Mutex
656		m  map[string]projectIDName
657	}
658	
659	func (c *projectIDCache) resolve(cwd string) (string, string) {
660		if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
661			return unknownProjectID, ""
662		}
663		c.mu.Lock()
664		defer c.mu.Unlock()
665		if v, ok := c.m[cwd]; ok {
666			return v.id, v.name
667		}
668		id, name, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
669		if err != nil || id == "" {
670			id, name = unknownProjectID, ""
671		}
672		c.m[cwd] = projectIDName{id, name}
673		return id, name
674	}
675	
676	// ---- helpers ----
677	
678	// summarizeCounts renders "claude 683 · codex 70 · …" in registry order, omitting zeros.
679	func summarizeCounts(ids []string, totals map[string]int) string {
680		var parts []string
681		for _, id := range ids {
682			if totals[id] > 0 {
683				parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", id, totals[id]))
684			}
685		}
686		return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
687	}
688	
689	// ---- live progress (per-agent bars) ----
690	
691	// reindexProgress renders live per-agent progress. Workers bump atomic per-agent
692	// counters and observe project ids; a single render goroutine redraws the block in
693	// place on a TTY (ANSI cursor-up), or prints periodic plain lines otherwise. No worker
694	// draws directly. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md "Reindex Progress UX".
695	type reindexProgress struct {
696		agents []string          // agents with >0 sessions, in registry order
697		totals map[string]int    // per-agent session totals
698		done   map[string]*int64 // per-agent completed counters (atomic)
699		tty    bool
700	
701		mu       sync.Mutex
702		projects map[string]struct{} // distinct non-unknown project ids seen
703	
704		stopCh     chan struct{}
705		doneCh     chan struct{}
706		linesDrawn int
707	}
708	
709	func newReindexProgress(ids []string, totals map[string]int) *reindexProgress {
710		done := make(map[string]*int64)
711		var agents []string
712		for _, id := range ids {
713			if totals[id] > 0 {
714				agents = append(agents, id)
715				done[id] = new(int64)
716			}
717		}
718		return &reindexProgress{
719			agents:   agents,
720			totals:   totals,
721			done:     done,
722			tty:      isTerminal(os.Stderr),
723			projects: map[string]struct{}{},
724			stopCh:   make(chan struct{}),
725			doneCh:   make(chan struct{}),
726		}
727	}
728	
729	func (p *reindexProgress) begin() {
730		go p.loop()
731	}
732	
733	func (p *reindexProgress) end() {
734		close(p.stopCh)
735		<-p.doneCh
736		p.draw(true) // final frame at 100%
737	}
738	
739	func (p *reindexProgress) inc(agent string) {
740		if c := p.done[agent]; c != nil {
741			atomic.AddInt64(c, 1)
742		}
743	}
744	
745	func (p *reindexProgress) observe(projectID string) {
746		// Unknown-project sessions are not counted toward the live "projects" total (the
747		// final unattributed count comes from the DB at summary time).
748		if projectID == unknownProjectID {
749			return
750		}
751		p.mu.Lock()
752		p.projects[projectID] = struct{}{}
753		p.mu.Unlock()
754	}
755	
756	func (p *reindexProgress) projectCount() int {
757		p.mu.Lock()
758		defer p.mu.Unlock()
759		return len(p.projects)
760	}
761	
762	func (p *reindexProgress) totalDone() int {
763		var n int64
764		for _, c := range p.done {
765			n += atomic.LoadInt64(c)
766		}
767		return int(n)
768	}
769	
770	func (p *reindexProgress) grandTotal() int {
771		n := 0
772		for _, a := range p.agents {
773			n += p.totals[a]
774		}
775		return n
776	}
777	
778	func (p *reindexProgress) loop() {
779		defer close(p.doneCh)
780		interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
781		if !p.tty {
782			interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
783		}
784		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
785		defer ticker.Stop()
786		for {
787			select {
788			case <-p.stopCh:
789				return
790			case <-ticker.C:
791				p.draw(false)
792			}
793		}
794	}
795	
796	func (p *reindexProgress) draw(final bool) {
797		if !p.tty {
798			if !final { // the final state is covered by the summary line
799				fprintf(os.Stderr, "indexed %d/%d…\n", p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal())
800			}
801			return
802		}
803	
804		lines := p.frameLines()
805		var b strings.Builder
806		if p.linesDrawn > 0 {
807			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "\033[%dA", p.linesDrawn) // move cursor up to the block's top
808		}
809		for _, ln := range lines {
810			b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear the line, then redraw it
811			b.WriteString(ln)
812			b.WriteString("\n")
813		}
814		fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
815		p.linesDrawn = len(lines)
816	}
817	
818	func (p *reindexProgress) frameLines() []string {
819		doneN, totalN := p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal()
820		lines := []string{
821			fmt.Sprintf("Indexing  %d/%d · %d projects", doneN, totalN, p.projectCount()),
822			"",
823		}
824	
825		nameW := 0
826		for _, a := range p.agents {
827			if len(a) > nameW {
828				nameW = len(a)
829			}
830		}
831		for _, a := range p.agents {
832			d := atomic.LoadInt64(p.done[a])
833			t := int64(p.totals[a])
834			check := ""
835			if d >= t {
836				check = " ✓"
837			}
838			lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  %-*s %s %d/%d%s", nameW, a, progressBar(d, t), d, t, check))
839		}
840		return lines
841	}
842	
843	// progressBar renders a fixed-width unicode bar, e.g. ▕███████░░░░░░░▏.
844	func progressBar(done, total int64) string {
845		const w = 14
846		filled := 0
847		if total > 0 {
848			filled = int(done * int64(w) / total)
849		}
850		if filled > w {
851			filled = w
852		}
853		return "▕" + strings.Repeat("█", filled) + strings.Repeat("░", w-filled) + "▏"
854	}
855	
856	// isTerminal reports whether f is a character device (an interactive terminal),
857	// using only the standard library (no isatty dependency).
858	func isTerminal(f *os.File) bool {
859		info, err := f.Stat()
860		if err != nil {
861			return false
862		}
863		return info.Mode()&os.ModeCharDevice != 0
864	}
865	
866	// ---- scan progress (live "Scanning agents…" line) ----
867	
868	// scanSpinner is the braille spinner cycled while enumerating.
869	var scanSpinner = []string{"⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"}
870	
871	// scanAgent holds one provider's live enumeration counter.
872	type scanAgent struct {
873		id string
874		r  *spi.ScanReporter
875	}
876	
877	// scanProgress renders one live line while enumerateAll scans providers in parallel:
878	//
879	//	🔍  Scanning agents…  ⠹  codex 18420 · claude 3195 · cursor 10
880	//
881	// Codex and Claude (the long poles) report sessions-found via spi.ProgressEnumerator; every
882	// provider snaps to its final count via markDone when it finishes. Counts are sessions, not raw
883	// files (warmup/sidechain transcripts are excluded). On a non-TTY it prints periodic plain lines.
884	// A nil *scanProgress is fully silent — the background warm passes nil.
885	type scanProgress struct {
886		agents []*scanAgent
887		byID   map[string]*scanAgent
888		tty    bool
889		spin   int
890	
891		stopCh     chan struct{}
892		doneCh     chan struct{}
893		linesDrawn int
894	}
895	
896	func newScanProgress(ids []string) *scanProgress {
897		agents := make([]*scanAgent, 0, len(ids))
898		byID := make(map[string]*scanAgent, len(ids))
899		for _, id := range ids {
900			a := &scanAgent{id: id, r: &spi.ScanReporter{}}
901			agents = append(agents, a)
902			byID[id] = a
903		}
904		return &scanProgress{
905			agents: agents,
906			byID:   byID,
907			tty:    isTerminal(os.Stderr),
908			stopCh: make(chan struct{}),
909			doneCh: make(chan struct{}),
910		}
911	}
912	
913	// reporterFor returns a provider's live reporter (nil-safe: a nil scanProgress yields nil, which
914	// providers treat as "do not report").
915	func (s *scanProgress) reporterFor(id string) *spi.ScanReporter {
916		if s == nil {
917			return nil
918		}
919		if a := s.byID[id]; a != nil {
920			return a.r
921		}
922		return nil
923	}
924	
925	// markDone reconciles a provider's running count to its final session count — a no-op for the
926	// reporting providers (Codex/Claude), and the only count for providers that never report
927	// (Cursor/Gemini/etc., which appear the moment they finish).
928	func (s *scanProgress) markDone(id string, final int) {
929		if s == nil {
930			return
931		}
932		if a := s.byID[id]; a != nil {
933			if delta := final - int(a.r.Found()); delta != 0 {
934				a.r.Add(delta)
935			}
936		}
937	}
938	
939	func (s *scanProgress) begin() {
940		if s == nil {
941			return
942		}
943		if !s.tty {
944			fprintln(os.Stderr, "\n🔍  Scanning agents…")
945		} else {
946			fprintln(os.Stderr) // blank line above the live line
947			s.draw()            // show immediately, before the first tick
948		}
949		go s.loop()
950	}
951	
952	func (s *scanProgress) end() {
953		if s == nil {
954			return
955		}
956		close(s.stopCh)
957		<-s.doneCh
958		if s.tty && s.linesDrawn > 0 {
959			// Erase the live line so the caller's "✓ Found…" line takes its place.
960			fprint(os.Stderr, "\033[1A\033[2K")
961		}
962	}
963	
964	func (s *scanProgress) loop() {
965		defer close(s.doneCh)
966		interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
967		if !s.tty {
968			interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
969		}
970		t := time.NewTicker(interval)
971		defer t.Stop()
972		for {
973			select {
974			case <-s.stopCh:
975				return
976			case <-t.C:
977				s.spin++
978				if s.tty {
979					s.draw()
980				} else {
981					fprintf(os.Stderr, "    scanning… %s\n", s.counts())
982				}
983			}
984		}
985	}
986	
987	func (s *scanProgress) draw() {
988		var b strings.Builder
989		if s.linesDrawn > 0 {
990			b.WriteString("\033[1A") // up to the live line
991		}
992		b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear it, then redraw
993		b.WriteString(s.line())
994		b.WriteString("\n")
995		fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
996		s.linesDrawn = 1
997	}
998	
999	func (s *scanProgress) line() string {
1000		return fmt.Sprintf("🔍  Scanning agents…  %s  %s", scanSpinner[s.spin%len(scanSpinner)], s.counts())
1001	}
1002	
1003	func (s *scanProgress) counts() string {
1004		var parts []string
1005		for _, a := range s.agents {
1006			found := a.r.Found()
1007			if found == 0 {
1008				continue // not started, or finished with no sessions
1009			}
1010			// Plain integers (no thousands separators), matching the "Found …" summary line.
1011			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", a.id, found))
1012		}
1013		if len(parts) == 0 {
1014			return "…"
1015		}
1016		return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
1017	}
1018	
1019	// ---- summary ----
1020	
1021	func printReindexSummary(ids []string, perAgent map[string]int, dbPath string, elapsed time.Duration, indexed, unchanged, projects, unattributed int) {
1022		w := os.Stderr
1023		if unchanged > 0 {
1024			fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions (%d unchanged) into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, unchanged, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
1025		} else {
1026			fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
1027		}
1028		fprintf(w, "      %s\n\n", summarizeCounts(ids, perAgent))
1029		if unattributed > 0 {
1030			fprintf(w, "      %d projects  ·  %d unattributed\n", projects, unattributed)
1031		} else {
1032			fprintf(w, "      %d projects\n", projects)
1033		}
1034		fprintf(w, "\n")
1035	}
1036	
1037	// prettyPath abbreviates the user's home directory to ~ for display.
1038	func prettyPath(p string) string {
1039		if home, err := os.UserHomeDir(); err == nil && strings.HasPrefix(p, home) {
1040			return "~" + p[len(home):]
1041		}
1042		return p
1043	}
1044
```

```text
1	package cmd

16	
17	func userMsg(ts, text string) schema.Message {
18		return schema.Message{Role: schema.RoleUser, Timestamp: ts,
19			Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}}}
20	}
21	func agentMsg(ts, text string) schema.Message {
22		return schema.Message{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Timestamp: ts,
23			Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: text}}}
24	}
25	
26	func sampleData() *schema.SessionData {
27		return &schema.SessionData{
28			Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
29				{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("2026-06-18T10:00:00Z", "fix the bug"), agentMsg("2026-06-18T10:01:00Z", "on it")}},
30				{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("2026-06-18T10:05:00Z", "now ship it"), agentMsg("2026-06-18T10:06:00Z", "done")}},
31			},
32		}
33	}
34	
35	func TestCountTurns(t *testing.T) {
36		user, total := countTurns(sampleData())
37		if user != 2 {
38			t.Errorf("user turns = %d; want 2", user)
39		}
40		if total != 4 {
41			t.Errorf("total turns = %d; want 4", total)
42		}
43	}
44	
45	func TestLastTimestamp(t *testing.T) {
46		if got := lastTimestamp(sampleData()); got != "2026-06-18T10:06:00Z" {
47			t.Errorf("lastTimestamp = %q; want the final message ts", got)
48		}
49		// Empty when no messages carry a timestamp.
50		noTS := &schema.SessionData{Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{{Messages: []schema.Message{userMsg("", "hi")}}}}
51		if got := lastTimestamp(noTS); got != "" {
52			t.Errorf("lastTimestamp with no timestamps = %q; want empty", got)
53		}
54	}
55	
56	func TestFlattenBody(t *testing.T) {
57		body := flattenBody(sampleData())
58		for _, want := range []string{"fix the bug", "on it", "now ship it", "done"} {
59			if !strings.Contains(body, want) {
60				t.Errorf("flattened body missing %q; got %q", want, body)
61			}
62		}
63	}
64	
65	func TestProjectIDCacheUnknownForEmptyCwd(t *testing.T) {
66		c := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
67		id, name := c.resolve("")
68		if id != unknownProjectID || name != "" {
69			t.Errorf("resolve(\"\") = (%q,%q); want (unknown, \"\")", id, name)
70		}
71	}
72	
73	func TestProjectIDCacheMemoizes(t *testing.T) {
74		c := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
75		// Pre-seed the cache and confirm resolve returns it without recomputing.
76		c.m["/some/repo"] = projectIDName{id: "abcd-1234", name: "repo"}
77		id, name := c.resolve("/some/repo")
78		if id != "abcd-1234" || name != "repo" {
79			t.Errorf("cached resolve = (%q,%q); want the seeded value", id, name)
80		}
81	}
82	
83	func TestProgressBar(t *testing.T) {
84		cases := []struct {
85			done, total int64
86			wantFull    int // number of █ expected
87		}{
88			{0, 10, 0},
89			{10, 10, 14},
90			{5, 10, 7},
91			{1, 0, 0},    // zero total → no divide-by-zero, empty bar
92			{20, 10, 14}, // clamped, never overflows
93		}
94		for _, tc := range cases {
95			got := progressBar(tc.done, tc.total)
96			if full := strings.Count(got, "█"); full != tc.wantFull {
97				t.Errorf("progressBar(%d,%d) had %d full cells; want %d (%q)", tc.done, tc.total, full, tc.wantFull, got)
98			}
99		}
100	}
101	
102	func TestSummarizeCounts(t *testing.T) {
103		ids := []string{"claude", "codex", "cursor"}
104		got := summarizeCounts(ids, map[string]int{"claude": 683, "codex": 0, "cursor": 49})
105		if got != "claude 683 · cursor 49" {
106			t.Errorf("summarizeCounts = %q; want zeros omitted, registry order", got)
107		}
108	}
109	
110	// TestDedupRefs verifies the collapse done before indexing: a session enumerated more than
111	// once (same agent+id) keeps the freshest native file by mtime, sessions with a blank id are
112	// dropped, a failed provider (nil) is skipped entirely, and foundPerAgent counts distinct
113	// sessions. statNative reads real files, so the duplicates point at temp files with set mtimes.
114	func TestDedupRefs(t *testing.T) {
115		dir := t.TempDir()
116		older := filepath.Join(dir, "older.jsonl")
117		newer := filepath.Join(dir, "newer.jsonl")
118		for _, p := range []string{older, newer} {
119			if err := os.WriteFile(p, []byte("x"), 0o644); err != nil {
120				t.Fatal(err)
121			}
122		}
123		oldT := time.Unix(1_000_000, 0)
124		newT := time.Unix(2_000_000, 0)
125		if err := os.Chtimes(older, oldT, oldT); err != nil {
126			t.Fatal(err)
127		}
128		if err := os.Chtimes(newer, newT, newT); err != nil {
129			t.Fatal(err)
130		}
131	
132		ids := []string{"claude", "codex"}
133		provs := []spi.Provider{&fakeProvider{}, nil} // codex failed to load → its refs must be skipped
134		perProvider := [][]spi.GlobalSessionRef{
135			{
136				{SessionID: "s1", NativePath: older},
137				{SessionID: "s1", NativePath: newer}, // same session, fresher file → wins
138				{SessionID: "  ", NativePath: older}, // blank id → dropped
139				{SessionID: "s2", NativePath: newer},
140			},
141			{{SessionID: "x1", NativePath: newer}}, // belongs to the nil provider → skipped
142		}
143	
144		best, order, found := dedupRefs(ids, provs, perProvider)
145	
146		s1 := sessionindex.FingerprintKey("claude", "s1")
147		if best[s1].ref.NativePath != newer {
148			t.Errorf("dedup kept %q for s1, want the fresher file %q", best[s1].ref.NativePath, newer)
149		}
150		if len(order) != 2 {
151			t.Fatalf("order = %v, want exactly s1 and s2 (blank + nil-provider dropped)", order)
152		}
153		if found["claude"] != 2 || found["codex"] != 0 {
154			t.Errorf("foundPerAgent = %v, want claude:2 codex:0", found)
155		}
156	}
157	
158	// TestSelectWork covers the incremental skip: a session is reindexed only when its native
159	// file's size or mtime changed, or the logic version was bumped; a session with no prior
160	// fingerprint is flagged isNew (so the writer can skip the FTS delete) unless --force, where
161	// the fingerprint set is empty and absence proves nothing.
162	func TestSelectWork(t *testing.T) {
163		const cwd = "/work/proj"
164		key := sessionindex.FingerprintKey("claude", "s1")
165		best := map[string]reindexItem{
166			key: {agent: "claude", ref: spi.GlobalSessionRef{SessionID: "s1", OriginCwd: cwd}, size: 100, mtime: 5000},
167		}
168		order := []string{key}
169	
170		tests := []struct {
171			name          string
172			fingerprints  map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint
173			force         bool
174			wantWork      int
175			wantUnchanged int
176			wantIsNew     bool // checked only when wantWork == 1
177		}{
178			{
179				name:          "unchanged: same size+mtime+version is skipped",
180				fingerprints:  map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{key: {Size: 100, Mtime: 5000, Version: reindexVersion}},
181				wantWork:      0,
182				wantUnchanged: 1,
183			},
184			{
185				name:         "changed mtime forces reindex, not new",
186				fingerprints: map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{key: {Size: 100, Mtime: 9999, Version: reindexVersion}},
187				wantWork:     1,
188				wantIsNew:    false,
189			},
190			{
191				name:         "version bump forces reindex even when the file is unchanged",
192				fingerprints: map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{key: {Size: 100, Mtime: 5000, Version: reindexVersion - 1}},
193				wantWork:     1,
194				wantIsNew:    false,
195			},
196			{
197				name:         "new session (no fingerprint) is flagged isNew",
198				fingerprints: map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{},
199				wantWork:     1,
200				wantIsNew:    true,
201			},
202			{
203				name:         "force: empty fingerprints, not flagged new so deletes are kept",
204				fingerprints: map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{},
205				force:        true,
206				wantWork:     1,
207				wantIsNew:    false,
208			},
209		}
210	
211		for _, tt := range tests {
212			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
213				cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
214				work, totals, unchanged := selectWork(order, best, tt.fingerprints, "", tt.force, cache)
215				if len(work) != tt.wantWork {
216					t.Fatalf("work = %d, want %d", len(work), tt.wantWork)
217				}
218				if unchanged != tt.wantUnchanged {
219					t.Errorf("unchanged = %d, want %d", unchanged, tt.wantUnchanged)
220				}
221				if tt.wantWork == 1 {
222					if work[0].isNew != tt.wantIsNew {
223						t.Errorf("isNew = %v, want %v", work[0].isNew, tt.wantIsNew)
224					}
225					if totals["claude"] != 1 {
226						t.Errorf("totals[claude] = %d, want 1", totals["claude"])
227					}
228				}
229			})
230		}
231	}
232	
233	// TestSelectWorkProjectFilter verifies the background warm's current-project pass: only
234	// sessions whose origin cwd resolves to the requested project id are kept. The cache is
235	// pre-seeded so resolution is deterministic and touches no real paths.
236	func TestSelectWorkProjectFilter(t *testing.T) {
237		k1 := sessionindex.FingerprintKey("claude", "s1")
238		k2 := sessionindex.FingerprintKey("claude", "s2")
239		best := map[string]reindexItem{
240			k1: {agent: "claude", ref: spi.GlobalSessionRef{SessionID: "s1", OriginCwd: "/work/a"}, size: 1, mtime: 1},
241			k2: {agent: "claude", ref: spi.GlobalSessionRef{SessionID: "s2", OriginCwd: "/work/b"}, size: 1, mtime: 1},
242		}
243		order := []string{k1, k2}
244		cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{
245			"/work/a": {id: "proj-a", name: "a"},
246			"/work/b": {id: "proj-b", name: "b"},
247		}}
248	
249		work, _, _ := selectWork(order, best, map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{}, "proj-a", false, cache)
250		if len(work) != 1 || work[0].ref.SessionID != "s1" {
251			t.Fatalf("project filter kept %d items, want only s1: %+v", len(work), work)
252		}
253	}
254	
255	// TestLiveIndexerRecordConcurrent exercises the mutex that serializes concurrent provider
256	// watchers: many goroutines record distinct sessions at once, and each must land exactly one
257	// row with no data race on lastSeen or the writer. Run with -race to catch a dropped lock.
258	func TestLiveIndexerRecordConcurrent(t *testing.T) {
259		store, err := sessionindex.Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "sessions.db"))
260		if err != nil {
261			t.Fatalf("open index: %v", err)
262		}
263		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
264	
265		li := &LiveIndexer{
266			store:       store,
267			cwd:         "/work/proj",
268			projectID:   "proj-1",
269			projectName: "proj",
270			indexedAt:   "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
271			lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
272		}
273	
274		const n = 24
275		var wg sync.WaitGroup
276		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
277			wg.Add(1)
278			go func(i int) {
279				defer wg.Done()
280				sid := fmt.Sprintf("sess-%d", i)
281				li.Record("claude", &spi.AgentChatSession{
282					SessionID: sid,
283					CreatedAt: "2026-06-25T00:00:00Z",
284					SessionData: &schema.SessionData{
285						Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{{Messages: []schema.Message{
286							userMsg("2026-06-25T00:01:00Z", "hi "+sid),
287						}}},
288					},
289				})
290			}(i)
291		}
292		wg.Wait()
293	
294		rows, err := store.ListByProject("proj-1")
295		if err != nil {
296			t.Fatalf("list: %v", err)
297		}
298		if len(rows) != n {
299			t.Errorf("got %d rows, want %d (one per distinct session)", len(rows), n)
300		}
301	}
302
```

```text
144	+package cmd

169	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
170	+)
171	+
172	+// Best-effort UI writers. The destination is the user's terminal, so write
173	+// errors are not actionable and are intentionally ignored.
174	+func fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) { _, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, format, a...) }
175	+func fprintln(w io.Writer, a ...any)               { _, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, a...) }
176	+func fprint(w io.Writer, a ...any)                 { _, _ = fmt.Fprint(w, a...) }
177	+
178	+// resumePlan captures the user's choices from the interactive selection.
179	+type resumePlan struct {
180	+	from      spi.Provider
181	+	fromID    string
182	+	sessionID string
183	+	// fromCwd is the directory the source session was originally launched from
184	+	// (the index's origin_cwd). It can differ from the user's current cwd when a
185	+	// session is picked from another project via the all-projects browser. The
186	+	// source data load must use this, because some providers (e.g. Codex) locate a
187	+	// session by matching its recorded cwd — looking under the current cwd would
188	+	// find nothing. Empty for older index rows; callers fall back to the current cwd.
189	+	fromCwd string
190	+	to      spi.Provider
191	+	toID    string
192	+}
193	+
194	+// agentChoice pairs a registry ID with its provider.
195	+type agentChoice struct {
196	+	id       string
197	+	provider spi.Provider
198	+}
199	+
200	+// CreateResumeCommand builds the `specstory resume` command. It opens the interactive
201	+// picker (a Bubble Tea TUI over the sessions.db index — see docs/RESUME-TUI.md): browse
202	+// the current project's sessions across all agents, pick one, choose a target agent, then
203	+// reconstruct (cross-agent) or native-resume (same agent) and launch with auto-save.
204	+func CreateResumeCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
205	+	longDesc := `Resume a past coding-agent session — in the same agent, or a different one.
206	+
207	+'resume' opens an interactive picker of the sessions in the current project across all
208	+agents. Pick one, choose which installed agent to continue it in, and go. Resuming into a
209	+different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.
210	+
211	+Pass an agent to set the resume target up front, e.g. 'specstory resume codex' — the picker
212	+then skips the target-selection step and resumes straight into that agent. The agent must be
213	+a known, installed provider, or the command errors.`
214	+
215	+	resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
216	+		Use:   "resume [agent]",
217	+		Short: "Resume a past session, optionally in a different agent",
218	+		Long:  longDesc,
219	+		Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
220	+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
221	+			config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
222	+			slog.Info("Running in resume mode")
223	+
224	+			registry := factory.GetRegistry()
225	+
226	+			// Optional positional arg pre-selects the target agent (e.g. `resume codex`).
227	+			// An unknown agent name should fail the run rather than be silently ignored, so
228	+			// validate it against the registry up front. When set and valid, the picker skips
229	+			// the target-selection step entirely (see selectResumeViaTUI / beginResume); whether
230	+			// the named agent is actually installed is checked there, where the installed set
231	+			// is known.
232	+			presetTarget := ""
233	+			if len(args) == 1 {
234	+				presetTarget = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(args[0]))
235	+				if _, err := registry.Get(presetTarget); err != nil {
236	+					return utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
237	+						"unknown agent %q. Valid agents: %s", presetTarget, registry.GetProviderList())}
238	+				}
239	+			}
240	+
241	+			// Read the run/watch flags that affect the resumed session (shared with `search`).
242	+			launchOpts := readResumeLaunchOpts(cmd)
243	+
244	+			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
245	+			if err != nil {
246	+				slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
247	+				return err
248	+			}
249	+
250	+			// Open (building if needed) the session index and run the interactive picker.
251	+			// builtFresh = the index was just rebuilt in the foreground, so the picker skips
252	+			// the background index warm (it would be redundant).
253	+			store, builtFresh, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
254	+			if err != nil {
255	+				return err
256	+			}
257	+			defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
258	+
259	+			projectID, projectName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
260	+			if idErr != nil || projectID == "" {
261	+				projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
262	+			}
263	+
264	+			plan, err := selectResumeViaTUI(registry, store, projectID, projectName, presetTarget, builtFresh)
265	+			if err != nil {
266	+				return err
267	+			}
268	+			if plan == nil {
269	+				// User quit or nothing to resume; the picker already explained why.
270	+				return nil
271	+			}
272	+
273	+			return launchResume(plan, cwd, launchOpts)
274	+		},
275	+	}
276	+
277	+	registerResumeLaunchFlags(resumeCmd, cloudURL, localTimeZone, debugDir)
278	+	return resumeCmd
279	+}
280	+
281	+// registerResumeLaunchFlags registers the run/watch flags that affect the resumed session,
282	+// shared by `resume` and `search` so the two stay in lockstep with each other and with watch.
283	+// cloud-url binds to the shared pointer the root applies in PersistentPreRunE. The telemetry
284	+// endpoint/service-name flags are inert here — they are consumed process-wide by main's startup
285	+// arg scan — and registered only so cobra accepts them. debug-raw, provenance and cloud-url are
286	+// hidden, matching run/watch.
287	+func registerResumeLaunchFlags(cmd *cobra.Command, cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) {
288	+	cmd.Flags().String("output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
289	+	cmd.Flags().String("debug-dir", debugDir, "custom output directory for debug data (default: ./.specstory/debug)")
290	+	cmd.Flags().Bool("only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
291	+	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
292	+	cmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
293	+	_ = cmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw")
294	+	cmd.Flags().Bool("local-time-zone", localTimeZone, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
295	+	cmd.Flags().Bool("provenance", false, "enable AI provenance tracking (correlate file changes to agent activity)")
296	+	_ = cmd.Flags().MarkHidden("provenance")
297	+	cmd.Flags().StringVar(cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
298	+	_ = cmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url")
299	+	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-telemetry-prompts", false, "exclude prompt text from telemetry spans, if telemetry is enabled")
300	+	cmd.Flags().String("telemetry-endpoint", "", "Open Telemetry Protocol (OTLP) gRPC collector endpoint (default is off, e.g., localhost:4317)")
301	+	cmd.Flags().String("telemetry-service-name", "", "override the default service name for telemetry, if telemetry is enabled")
302	+}
303	+
304	+// readResumeLaunchOpts reads the session-affecting flags registered by registerResumeLaunchFlags
305	+// into the launch options, applying the debug-dir override as a side effect (mirrors run/watch).
306	+// cloud-url and the telemetry endpoint/service-name are consumed elsewhere (PersistentPreRunE and
307	+// main's startup scan, respectively), so they are deliberately absent from the returned opts.
308	+func readResumeLaunchOpts(cmd *cobra.Command) resumeLaunchOpts {
309	+	debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
310	+	useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
311	+	outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
312	+	flagDebugDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("debug-dir")
313	+	noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
314	+	onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
315	+	provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")
316	+	noTelemetryPrompts, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-telemetry-prompts")
317	+
318	+	if flagDebugDir != "" {
319	+		spi.SetDebugBaseDir(flagDebugDir)
320	+	}
321	+
322	+	return resumeLaunchOpts{
323	+		outputDir:          outputDir,
324	+		flagDebugDir:       flagDebugDir,
325	+		debugRaw:           debugRaw,
326	+		useUTC:             !useLocalTimezone,
327	+		noCloudSync:        noCloudSync,
328	+		onlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
329	+		provenanceEnabled:  provenanceEnabled,
330	+		noTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
331	+	}
332	+}
333	+
334	+// resumeLaunchOpts carries the resume launch configuration shared by `resume` and
335	+// `search` (whose `r` action resumes a found session through the same path).
336	+type resumeLaunchOpts struct {
337	+	outputDir          string
338	+	flagDebugDir       string
339	+	debugRaw           bool
340	+	useUTC             bool
341	+	noCloudSync        bool
342	+	onlyCloudSync      bool
343	+	provenanceEnabled  bool
344	+	noTelemetryPrompts bool
345	+}
346	+
347	+// launchResume reconstructs (cross-agent) or natively resumes the planned session and
348	+// runs the agent with auto-save + provenance — the shared tail of `resume` and `search`.
349	+func launchResume(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, o resumeLaunchOpts) error {
350	+	// Setup output configuration and project identity (needed for auto-save + cloud).
351	+	outConfig, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(o.outputDir, o.flagDebugDir)
352	+	if err != nil {
353	+		return err
354	+	}
355	+	if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(outConfig); err != nil {
356	+		return err
357	+	}
358	+	if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
359	+		slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
360	+	}
361	+
362	+	CheckAndWarnAuthentication(o.noCloudSync)
363	+	if o.onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
364	+		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
365	+	}
366	+
367	+	analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{plan.to.Name()})
368	+	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventResumeActivated, analytics.Properties{
369	+		"from_provider": plan.fromID,
370	+		"to_provider":   plan.toID,
371	+		"cross_agent":   plan.fromID != plan.toID,
372	+	})
373	+
374	+	resumeSessionID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, cwd, os.Stdout)
375	+	if err != nil {
376	+		return err
377	+	}
378	+
379	+	// Context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C).
380	+	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
381	+	defer cancel()
382	+
383	+	// Provenance infrastructure (mirrors run/watch).
384	+	provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(o.provenanceEnabled)
385	+	if err != nil {
386	+		return err
387	+	}
388	+	defer provenanceCleanup()
389	+	fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
390	+	if err != nil {
391	+		return err
392	+	}
393	+	defer fsCleanup()
394	+
395	+	// Keep sessions.db current in real time alongside the markdown writes (nil/no-op if the
396	+	// index can't be opened — never block the resumed agent on it).
397	+	liveIndex := NewLiveIndexer(cwd)
398	+	defer liveIndex.Close()
399	+
400	+	// Auto-save callback: identical behavior to run/watch.
401	+	sessionCallback := func(sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
402	+		if sess == nil {
403	+			return
404	+		}
405	+		_, perr := session.ProcessSingleSession(context.Background(), sess, outConfig, session.ProcessingOptions{
406	+			OnlyCloudSync:      o.onlyCloudSync,
407	+			IsAutosave:         true,
408	+			DebugRaw:           o.debugRaw,
409	+			UseUTC:             o.useUTC,
410	+			NoTelemetryPrompts: o.noTelemetryPrompts,
411	+		})
412	+		if perr != nil {
413	+			slog.Error("Failed to process session update", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", perr)
414	+		}
415	+		// Mirror the markdown write into the restore index (agent we resumed INTO).
416	+		liveIndex.Record(plan.toID, sess)
417	+		provenance.ProcessEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, sess)
418	+	}
419	+
420	+	slog.Info("Launching resume", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID)
421	+	if err := plan.to.ExecAgentAndWatch(cwd, "", resumeSessionID, o.debugRaw, sessionCallback); err != nil {
422	+		slog.Error("Agent resume failed", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "error", err)
423	+		return err
424	+	}
425	+	return nil
426	+}
427	+
428	+// prepareResumeTarget produces the native session ID to hand to ExecAgentAndWatch.
429	+// Same-agent resume reuses the existing session; cross-agent reconstructs the
430	+// source SessionData into the target's native store and returns the fresh ID.
431	+func prepareResumeTarget(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, out io.Writer) (string, error) {
432	+	// Same agent: native resume on the existing session, no transform.
433	+	if plan.fromID == plan.toID {
434	+		fprintf(out, "\nResuming %s session %s in place...\n", plan.from.Name(), shortID(plan.sessionID))
435	+		return plan.sessionID, nil
436	+	}
437	+
438	+	// One event records every cross-agent reconstruction outcome so portability usage and
439	+	// failure rates are visible. Fired at each terminal branch below.
440	+	track := func(outcome string) {
441	+		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventResumeReconstructed, analytics.Properties{
442	+			"from_agent": plan.fromID,
443	+			"to_agent":   plan.toID,
444	+			"outcome":    outcome,
445	+		})
446	+	}
447	+	slog.Info("resume: reconstructing cross-agent session",
448	+		"from", plan.fromID, "to", plan.toID, "sessionID", plan.sessionID)
449	+
450	+	// Cross-agent: load source SessionData, reconstruct, write into target store.
451	+	// The source must be read from the directory it was originally launched in, not the
452	+	// user's current cwd — a session picked from another project (all-projects browser)
453	+	// lives elsewhere, and providers like Codex locate it by its recorded cwd. The
454	+	// reconstructed session is still written and launched under the current cwd below.
455	+	fromCwd := plan.fromCwd
456	+	if fromCwd == "" {
457	+		fromCwd = cwd
458	+	}
459	+	fromSession, err := plan.from.GetAgentChatSession(fromCwd, plan.sessionID, false)
460	+	if err != nil {
461	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to load source session: %w", err)
462	+	}
463	+	if fromSession == nil || fromSession.SessionData == nil {
464	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("source session %s has no data to reconstruct", plan.sessionID)
465	+	}
466	+
467	+	note := fmt.Sprintf("Resumed from a %s session via SpecStory.", plan.from.Name())
468	+	rec, err := plan.to.ReconstructSession(fromSession.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions{
469	+		WorkspaceRoot: cwd,
470	+		MigrationNote: note,
471	+	})
472	+	if err != nil {
473	+		if errors.Is(err, spi.ErrReconstructionUnsupported) {
474	+			track("unsupported")
475	+			return "", utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
476	+				"%s can't yet be a cross-agent resume target. Choose Claude Code or Codex CLI (or resume in %s itself).",
477	+				plan.to.Name(), plan.from.Name())}
478	+		}
479	+		slog.Warn("resume: reconstruction failed", "from", plan.fromID, "to", plan.toID, "error", err)
480	+		track("error")
481	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to reconstruct session for %s: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
482	+	}
483	+
484	+	path, err := plan.to.NativeSessionPath(cwd, rec.Filename)
485	+	if err != nil {
486	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve target session path: %w", err)
487	+	}
488	+	if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, rec.Content); err != nil {
489	+		return "", err
490	+	}
491	+	// The agent runs in a separate process and resolves the session by reading this file the
492	+	// instant it starts. On a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the freshly
493	+	// written file can lag, so confirm it is actually readable before handing the agent a
494	+	// --resume it would otherwise reject as "session not found".
495	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(path, sessionFileVisibleTimeout); err != nil {
496	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("reconstructed %s session is not ready to resume: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
497	+	}
498	+
499	+	slog.Info("resume: wrote reconstructed session", "path", path, "newID", rec.SessionID)
500	+	track("success")
501	+	fprintf(out, "\nReconstructed %s session into %s as %s.\n", plan.from.Name(), plan.to.Name(), shortID(rec.SessionID))
502	+	return rec.SessionID, nil
503	+}
504	+
505	+// shortID abbreviates a session ID for display.
506	+func shortID(id string) string {
507	+	if len(id) <= 13 {
508	+		return id
509	+	}
510	+	return id[:5] + "..." + id[len(id)-5:]
511	+}
512	+
513	+// sessionFileVisibleTimeout bounds how long resume waits for a just-written reconstructed
514	+// session file to become readable to other processes before launching the agent. It is
515	+// generous enough to absorb the propagation lag of a weak-coherence filesystem (network or
516	+// roaming home, cloud-synced folder, VM/container shared mount) yet short enough not to hang
517	+// an interactive command — on a local disk the very first check passes, so it never waits.
518	+const sessionFileVisibleTimeout = 10 * time.Second
519	+
520	+// writeReconstructedSession durably and atomically writes a reconstructed session file. A
521	+// plain os.WriteFile leaves the data in the page cache and the new directory entry possibly
522	+// non-durable; on a filesystem without strong close-to-open coherence the separately-spawned
523	+// agent process can then fail to see a (large) file and report the session as missing. To
524	+// avoid that, the content is written to a temp file in the SAME directory (so the rename is
525	+// atomic rather than cross-device), fsync'd, renamed into place, and the parent directory is
526	+// fsync'd so the entry itself is durable.
527	+func writeReconstructedSession(path string, content []byte) (err error) {
528	+	dir := filepath.Dir(path)
529	+	if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); mkErr != nil {
530	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create target session directory: %w", mkErr)
531	+	}
532	+
533	+	tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, ".resume-*.tmp")
534	+	if err != nil {
535	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp session file: %w", err)
536	+	}
537	+	tmpPath := tmp.Name()
538	+	// Best-effort cleanup of the temp file on any early return; a no-op once it is renamed away.
539	+	defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
540	+
541	+	// Preserve the world-readable mode the previous os.WriteFile(…, 0o644) produced
542	+	// (CreateTemp makes the file 0o600).
543	+	if chErr := tmp.Chmod(0o644); chErr != nil {
544	+		_ = tmp.Close()
545	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to set session file mode: %w", chErr)
546	+	}
547	+	if _, wErr := tmp.Write(content); wErr != nil {
548	+		_ = tmp.Close()
549	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write temp session file: %w", wErr)
550	+	}
551	+	// Flush the bytes to the backing store before the rename so a reader cannot observe a
552	+	// renamed-but-empty file.
553	+	if syncErr := tmp.Sync(); syncErr != nil {
554	+		_ = tmp.Close()
555	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to flush temp session file: %w", syncErr)
556	+	}
557	+	if closeErr := tmp.Close(); closeErr != nil {
558	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp session file: %w", closeErr)
559	+	}
560	+
561	+	if rnErr := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); rnErr != nil {
562	+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to move session file into place: %w", rnErr)
563	+	}
564	+
565	+	// Fsync the directory so the new entry is durable and propagates. Best-effort: not all
566	+	// platforms permit fsync on a directory handle, and it is a negligible cost on local disks.
567	+	if d, openErr := os.Open(dir); openErr == nil {
568	+		_ = d.Sync()
569	+		_ = d.Close()
570	+	}
571	+	return nil
572	+}
573	+
574	+// waitForSessionFileVisible polls until path is readable — present AND with at least its
575	+// first line available, since on a weak-coherence filesystem a stat can succeed before the
576	+// content propagates — or the timeout elapses, in which case it returns a diagnostic error
577	+// so the caller can fail clearly rather than launch a --resume the agent would reject.
578	+func waitForSessionFileVisible(path string, timeout time.Duration) error {
579	+	deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
580	+	for {
581	+		readable, checkErr := sessionFileReadable(path)
582	+		if readable {
583	+			return nil
584	+		}
585	+		if time.Now().After(deadline) {
586	+			return fmt.Errorf("session file %q did not become readable within %s "+
587	+				"(the target filesystem may lack close-to-open coherence): %w", path, timeout, checkErr)
588	+		}
589	+		time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
590	+	}
591	+}
592	+
593	+// sessionFileReadable reports whether path exists and at least its first line can be read.
594	+// A reconstructed session always has at least one record, so an empty read means the content
595	+// has not propagated yet even though the directory entry exists.
596	+func sessionFileReadable(path string) (bool, error) {
597	+	f, err := os.Open(path)
598	+	if err != nil {
599	+		return false, err
600	+	}
601	+	defer func() { _ = f.Close() }()
602	+
603	+	line, err := bufio.NewReader(f).ReadString('\n')
604	+	if len(line) > 0 {
605	+		return true, nil
606	+	}
607	+	if err == io.EOF || err == nil {
608	+		return false, fmt.Errorf("session file is empty")
609	+	}
610	+	return false, err
611	+}

633	+func TestFTSQuery(t *testing.T) {
634	+	cases := []struct{ name, in, want string }{
635	+		{"empty", "", ""},
636	+		{"single word is a prefix", "thank", "thank*"},
637	+		{"bare words are independent prefixes", "thank you", "thank* you*"},
638	+		{"punctuation in a bare word splits into an adjacency phrase", "max-cpu!", "max + cpu*"},
639	+		{"a bare filename is an adjacency phrase with prefix last", "poem.txt", "poem + txt*"},
640	+		{"a quoted filename is a committed phrase, no prefix", `"poem.txt"`, `"poem txt"`},
641	+		{"an open-quoted filename keeps the last token a prefix", `"poem.txt`, "poem + txt*"},
642	+		{"closed phrase is exact adjacency", `"thank you"`, `"thank you"`},
643	+		{"closed single-word phrase has no prefix", `"thank"`, `"thank"`},
644	+		{"open phrase keeps last word a prefix", `"thank yo`, "thank + yo*"},
645	+		{"open single-word phrase is a prefix", `"thank`, "thank*"},
646	+		{"phrase plus trailing bare word", `"thank you" now`, `"thank you" now*`},
647	+		{"bare word before a closed phrase", `please "thank you"`, `please* "thank you"`},
648	+		{"whitespace inside a phrase is collapsed", `"thank    you"`, `"thank you"`},
649	+	}
650	+	for _, c := range cases {
651	+		t.Run(c.name, func(t *testing.T) {
652	+			if got := ftsQuery(c.in); got != c.want {
653	+				t.Errorf("ftsQuery(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
654	+			}
655	+		})
656	+	}
657	+}
658	+
659	+func TestQueryReady(t *testing.T) {
660	+	cases := []struct {
661	+		in   string
662	+		want bool
663	+	}{
664	+		{"", false},
665	+		{"a", false},   // one alnum char is below minQueryLen
666	+		{`"a"`, false}, // quotes don't count toward the threshold
667	+		{"ab", true},   // two alnum chars
668	+		{"a!", false},  // punctuation doesn't count
669	+		{"a b", true},  // two alnum chars across words
670	+		{`"hi"`, true}, // alnum inside quotes counts
671	+		{"日本", true},   // letters in other scripts count
672	+	}
673	+	for _, c := range cases {
674	+		if got := queryReady(c.in); got != c.want {
675	+			t.Errorf("queryReady(%q) = %v, want %v", c.in, got, c.want)
676	+		}
677	+	}
678	+}
679	+
680	+func TestShortID(t *testing.T) {
681	+	cases := []struct{ in, want string }{
682	+		{"short", "short"},
683	+		{"1234567890abc", "1234567890abc"}, // <= 13 chars: unchanged
684	+		{"1234567890abcdef", "12345...bcdef"},
685	+	}
686	+	for _, c := range cases {
687	+		if got := shortID(c.in); got != c.want {
688	+			t.Errorf("shortID(%q) = %q, want %q", c.in, got, c.want)
689	+		}
690	+	}
691	+}

693	+func TestWriteReconstructedSession(t *testing.T) {
694	+	dir := t.TempDir()
695	+	path := filepath.Join(dir, "sub", "session.jsonl")
696	+	content := []byte(`{"type":"user"}` + "\n")
697	+
698	+	if err := writeReconstructedSession(path, content); err != nil {
699	+		t.Fatalf("writeReconstructedSession: %v", err)
700	+	}
701	+
702	+	got, err := os.ReadFile(path)
703	+	if err != nil {
704	+		t.Fatalf("reading written file: %v", err)
705	+	}
706	+	if string(got) != string(content) {
707	+		t.Errorf("content = %q, want %q", got, content)
708	+	}
709	+
710	+	// The atomic-write temp file must not be left behind in the target directory.
711	+	entries, err := os.ReadDir(filepath.Dir(path))
712	+	if err != nil {
713	+		t.Fatal(err)
714	+	}
715	+	for _, e := range entries {
716	+		if e.Name() != "session.jsonl" {
717	+			t.Errorf("unexpected leftover file in target dir: %q", e.Name())
718	+		}
719	+	}
720	+}

722	+func TestSessionFileReadable(t *testing.T) {
723	+	dir := t.TempDir()
724	+
725	+	missing := filepath.Join(dir, "nope.jsonl")
726	+	if ok, _ := sessionFileReadable(missing); ok {
727	+		t.Error("missing file reported readable")
728	+	}
729	+
730	+	empty := filepath.Join(dir, "empty.jsonl")
731	+	if err := os.WriteFile(empty, nil, 0o644); err != nil {
732	+		t.Fatal(err)
733	+	}
734	+	if ok, _ := sessionFileReadable(empty); ok {
735	+		t.Error("empty file reported readable")
736	+	}
737	+
738	+	full := filepath.Join(dir, "full.jsonl")
739	+	if err := os.WriteFile(full, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
740	+		t.Fatal(err)
741	+	}
742	+	if ok, err := sessionFileReadable(full); !ok {
743	+		t.Errorf("file with content reported not readable: %v", err)
744	+	}
745	+}

747	+func TestWaitForSessionFileVisible(t *testing.T) {
748	+	dir := t.TempDir()
749	+
750	+	present := filepath.Join(dir, "present.jsonl")
751	+	if err := os.WriteFile(present, []byte(`{"type":"user"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
752	+		t.Fatal(err)
753	+	}
754	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(present, time.Second); err != nil {
755	+		t.Errorf("expected an already-present file to be visible: %v", err)
756	+	}
757	+
758	+	// A file that never appears must time out with a diagnostic error rather than block forever.
759	+	if err := waitForSessionFileVisible(filepath.Join(dir, "never.jsonl"), 100*time.Millisecond); err == nil {
760	+		t.Error("expected timeout error for a file that never becomes visible")
761	+	}
762	+}

764	+// TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep verifies the `resume <agent>` contract: when a
765	+// target agent was pre-selected, choosing a session resumes immediately into that agent
766	+// rather than prompting for a target.
767	+func TestBeginResumeWithPresetSkipsTargetStep(t *testing.T) {
768	+	sess := &sessionindex.Session{SessionID: "s1", Agent: "codex"}
769	+	m := sessionTUI{presetTo: "claude"}
770	+
771	+	next, cmd := m.beginResume(sess)
772	+	rm := next.(sessionTUI)
773	+
774	+	if cmd == nil {
775	+		t.Error("expected an immediate quit command when a target is preset")
776	+	}
777	+	if rm.mode == modeTarget {
778	+		t.Error("a preset target must skip the target-selection step")
779	+	}
780	+	if rm.result.session != sess {
781	+		t.Errorf("result session = %v, want the chosen session", rm.result.session)
782	+	}
783	+	if rm.result.targetID != "claude" {
784	+		t.Errorf("result target = %q, want %q", rm.result.targetID, "claude")
785	+	}
786	+}
787	+
788	+// TestBeginResumeWithoutPresetEntersTargetStep verifies the default flow: with no preset,
789	+// choosing a session advances to the target-selection step (no immediate resume).
790	+func TestBeginResumeWithoutPresetEntersTargetStep(t *testing.T) {
791	+	sess := &sessionindex.Session{SessionID: "s1", Agent: "codex"}
792	+	m := sessionTUI{}
793	+
794	+	next, cmd := m.beginResume(sess)
795	+	rm := next.(sessionTUI)
796	+
797	+	if cmd != nil {
798	+		t.Error("expected no immediate quit without a preset target")
799	+	}
800	+	if rm.mode != modeTarget {
801	+		t.Errorf("mode = %v, want modeTarget", rm.mode)
802	+	}
803	+	if rm.chosen != sess {
804	+		t.Error("chosen session must be recorded before target selection")
805	+	}
806	+}
807	+
808	+// fakeProvider is a minimal spi.Provider for exercising prepareResumeTarget. It records
809	+// the projectPath it is asked to load the source session from, and reconstructs into a
810	+// caller-provided directory so the write/visibility tail succeeds.
811	+type fakeProvider struct {
812	+	name        string
813	+	gotLoadPath string // projectPath captured from GetAgentChatSession
814	+	nativeDir   string // where NativeSessionPath places the reconstructed file

1038	+			initialQuery := strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(args, " "))

1054	+
1055	+			if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
```
