```text
265	// createHash creates a hash from the input string matching the TypeScript implementation
266	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) createHash(input string) string {
267		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(input))
268		fullHash := fmt.Sprintf("%x", hash)
269	
270		// Take first 16 characters and format as UUID-like string
271		truncated := fullHash[:16]
272	
273		// Insert dashes after every 4 characters: xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx
274		formatted := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s-%s-%s",
275			truncated[0:4],
276			truncated[4:8],
277			truncated[8:12],
278			truncated[12:16])
279	
280		return formatted
281	}
282	
283	// GetWorkspaceID returns the workspace ID from the project identity
284	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetWorkspaceID() (string, error) {
285		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
286		if err != nil {
287			return "", err
288		}
289		if identity == nil || identity.WorkspaceID == "" {
290			return "", fmt.Errorf("no workspace ID found")
291		}
292		return identity.WorkspaceID, nil
293	}
294	
295	// GetGitID returns the git ID from the project identity
296	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetGitID() (string, error) {
297		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
298		if err != nil {
299			return "", err
300		}
301		if identity == nil || identity.GitID == "" {
302			return "", fmt.Errorf("no git ID found")
303		}
304		return identity.GitID, nil
305	}
306	
307	// GetProjectID returns the git ID if available, otherwise the workspace ID
308	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetProjectID() (string, error) {
309		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
310		if err != nil {
311			return "", err
312		}
313		if identity == nil {
314			return "", fmt.Errorf("no project identity found")
315		}
316	
317		// Prefer git_id over workspace_id (like the TypeScript implementation)
318		if identity.GitID != "" {
319			return identity.GitID, nil
320		}
321		if identity.WorkspaceID != "" {
322			return identity.WorkspaceID, nil
323		}
324	
325		return "", fmt.Errorf("no project ID available")
326	}
327	
328	// ValidateUUID checks if a string is a valid UUID format
329	func ValidateUUID(s string) bool {
330		_, err := uuid.Parse(s)
331		return err == nil
332	}
333	
334	// IsValidProjectID checks if a string matches the expected project ID format
335	// Project IDs are 16 hex characters formatted as: xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx
336	func IsValidProjectID(s string) bool {
337		// Match the format: 4 groups of 4 hex characters separated by dashes
338		matched, _ := regexp.MatchString(`^[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}$`, s)
339		return matched
340	}
341	
342	// parseGitRemoteURL parses a git remote URL and returns the repository name
343	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) parseGitRemoteURL(remoteURL string) string {
344		// Remove .git suffix if present
345		withoutDotGit := strings.TrimSuffix(remoteURL, ".git")
346	
347		var repoName string
348	
349		// Case 1: SSH format (git@github.com:owner/repo)
350		if strings.HasPrefix(withoutDotGit, "git@") {
351			// Split by : and get the part after it
352			parts := strings.Split(withoutDotGit, ":")
353			if len(parts) >= 2 {
354				pathParts := strings.Split(parts[1], "/")
355				if len(pathParts) > 0 {
356					repoName = pathParts[len(pathParts)-1]
357				}
358			}
359		} else if matched := regexp.MustCompile(`^(https?|git|git\+ssh)://`).MatchString(withoutDotGit); matched {
360			// Case 2: HTTP-like protocols (http://, https://, git://, git+ssh://)
361			// Remove the protocol part and extract repo name
362			urlParts := strings.Split(withoutDotGit, "/")
363			if len(urlParts) > 0 {
364				repoName = urlParts[len(urlParts)-1]
365			}
366		} else {
367			// Case 3: Implicit HTTPS (github.com/owner/repo)
368			// Match domain/path pattern
369			if matched := regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+/(.+)`).FindStringSubmatch(withoutDotGit); len(matched) > 1 {
370				pathParts := strings.Split(matched[1], "/")
371				if len(pathParts) > 0 {
372					repoName = pathParts[len(pathParts)-1]
373				}
374			}
375		}
376	
377		return repoName
378	}
```

```text
27	// sessionTUIResult is what the picker hands back: the chosen session and target agent,
28	// or a cancel. It is read off the final model after the program exits.
29	type sessionTUIResult struct {
30		session   *sessionindex.Session
31		targetID  string
32		cancelled bool
33	}
34	
35	// tuiMode is the picker's top-level screen.
36	type tuiMode int
37	
38	const (
39		modeList     tuiMode = iota // browsing the session list (current or a drilled-in project)
40		modeTarget                  // choosing which agent to resume into
41		modeProjects                // the all-projects browser (Stage B)
42	)
43	
44	// agentMeta carries an agent's display name + accent color for the list.
45	type agentMeta struct {
46		name   string
47		accent color.Color
48	}
49	
50	// sessionTUI is the shared model behind BOTH `specstory resume` and `specstory search`.
51	// The two commands are the same UI with different entry points: resume opens on the current
52	// project's session list; search opens straight into the all-projects FTS with the input
53	// focused. Everything else — keys, preview, agent filter, dense/sparse, the target-agent
54	// step — is identical by construction. See docs/RESUME-TUI.md and docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
55	type sessionTUI struct {
56		store       *sessionindex.Store
57		registry    *factory.Registry // re-parses a session for the glamour preview
58		title       string            // header title, e.g. "SpecStory Resume" / "SpecStory Search"
59		projectID   string
60		projectName string
61		agents      map[string]agentMeta // provider id -> display meta
62		installed   []agentChoice        // installed agents, for the target step
63		presetTo    string               // pre-selected target (from `resume <agent>`), or ""
64		lastAgent   string               // default target (last resumed), or ""
65	
66		// homeProjectID/Name + homeSessions are the current directory's project; the picker
67		// can drill into other projects via the browser and toggle back to "home" with tab.
68		homeProjectID   string
69		homeProjectName string
70		homeSessions    []sessionindex.Session
71	
72		all              []sessionindex.Session // sessions for the active project (projectID), newest first
73		filtered         []sessionindex.Session // after agent filter + search
74		filteredSnippets map[string]string      // match snippets for visible filtered rows, keyed by agent/session
75		cursor           int                    // index into filtered
76		top              int                    // first visible row (scroll)
77		inBrowser        bool                   // the active session list was reached via the browser
78	
79		agentCycle  []string // "" (all) followed by each present agent id
80		agentFilter string   // "" = all
81		viewMode    string   // "dense" | "sparse"
82	
83		// all-projects browser (Stage B)
84		projects         []sessionindex.ProjectSummary // all projects, most recent first
85		projFiltered     []sessionindex.ProjectSummary // after project-name search
86		projCursor       int
87		projTop          int
88		projectsLoaded   bool
89		startedInBrowser bool // launched straight into the browser (empty home project)
90		startedInSearch  bool // launched straight into the all-projects search (`specstory search`)
91		projSearching    bool
92		projSearch       textinput.Model
93		projSearchQuery  string
94	
95		// global session search: FTS across all projects, or scoped to a single project with tab
96		// (the highlighted hit's project, else the current directory's). Opened with / in the
97		// browser or as `search`.
98		globalActive    bool
99		globalSearching bool
100		globalScopeID   string // "" = all projects; else the project id the search is scoped to
101		globalScopeName string // display name for the scoped project
102		globalInput     textinput.Model
103		globalQuery     string
104		globalResults   []sessionindex.Session
105		globalSnippets  map[string]string

107		globalTop       int

113		snippetSeq  int // bumped per lazy snippet request; stale snippet results are discarded
114		// searchCancel aborts the in-flight FTS query when a newer keystroke supersedes it,
115		// freeing the database connection (a broad prefix query can rank the whole corpus).
116		searchCancel context.CancelFunc
117	
118		// previewing shows a glamour-rendered, scrollable reader for the highlighted session,

132	// sessionTUIOpts carries the per-command entry configuration. The only real differences
133	// between `resume` and `search` live here: the header title, what the positional arg means
134	// (presetTo vs. initialQuery), and whether to open in the all-projects search.
135	type sessionTUIOpts struct {
136		title         string // header title ("SpecStory Resume" / "SpecStory Search")
137		presetTo      string // resume: pre-selected target agent (from `resume <agent>`)
138		lastAgent     string // default target (last resumed), or ""
139		viewMode      string // "dense" | "sparse"
140		initialQuery  string // search: pre-seed the all-projects query
141		startInSearch bool   // search: open in the all-projects FTS with the input focused
142	}
143	
144	func newSessionTUI(store *sessionindex.Store, registry *factory.Registry, projectID, projectName string,
145		sessions []sessionindex.Session, agents map[string]agentMeta, installed []agentChoice, opts sessionTUIOpts) sessionTUI {
146	
147		ti := textinput.New()
148		ti.Prompt = "/ "
149		pi := textinput.New()
150		pi.Prompt = "p "
151		gi := textinput.New()
152		gi.Prompt = "/ "
153	
154		m := sessionTUI{
155			store:           store,
156			registry:        registry,
157			title:           opts.title,
158			reader:          viewport.New(),
159			projectID:       projectID,
160			projectName:     projectName,
161			homeProjectID:   projectID,
162			homeProjectName: projectName,
163			homeSessions:    sessions,
164			agents:          agents,
165			installed:       installed,
166			presetTo:        opts.presetTo,
167			lastAgent:       opts.lastAgent,
168			all:             sessions,
169			viewMode:        opts.viewMode,
170			search:          ti,
171			projSearch:      pi,
172			globalInput:     gi,
173		}
174		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
175		m.applyFilter()
176	
177		switch {
178		case opts.startInSearch:
179			// `search`: land directly in the all-projects FTS, input focused (so the user types
180			// immediately, exactly like before). Init fires the pre-seeded query, if any. The
181			// search IS the root view here, so esc quits rather than unwinding into the browser.
182			m.startedInSearch = true
183			m.enterBrowser()
184			m.globalActive = true
185			m.globalSearching = true
186			m.globalQuery = opts.initialQuery
187			m.globalInput.SetValue(opts.initialQuery)
188			m.globalInput.Focus() // focus state persists on the stored model; Init returns the blink+query cmds
189			if queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
190				m.searchSeq = 1
191			}
192		case len(sessions) == 0:
193			// `resume` with an empty current project → open straight into the all-projects browser.
194			m.startedInBrowser = true
195			m.enterBrowser()
196		}
197		return m
198	}
199	
200	func (m sessionTUI) Init() tea.Cmd {
201		// Search starts focused in the all-projects input; kick the blink and any pre-seeded query.
202		if m.globalSearching {
203			cmds := []tea.Cmd{m.globalInput.Focus()}
204			if queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
205				cmds = append(cmds, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects))
206			}
207			return tea.Batch(cmds...)
208		}
209		return nil
210	}
211	
212	func (m sessionTUI) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
213		switch msg := msg.(type) {
214		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
215			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
216			m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
217			m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
218			return m, nil
219		case indexWarmedMsg:
220			return m.refreshAfterWarm()
221		case searchDebounceMsg:
222			// Fire the actual query only if no newer keystroke has arrived (debounce).
223			if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
224				if m.searchCancel != nil {
225					m.searchCancel() // abort any prior in-flight query, freeing its connection
226				}
227				ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
228				m.searchCancel = cancel
229				return m, m.runSearch(msg.seq, msg.kind, ctx)
230			}
231			return m, nil
232		case searchResultMsg:
233			// Apply only the latest query's results (discard stale async results).
234			if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
235				m.applySearchResults(msg.kind, msg.sessions)
236				return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(msg.kind)
237			}
238			return m, nil
239		case snippetResultMsg:
240			if msg.seq == m.snippetSeq {
241				if msg.kind == modeProjects {
242					if m.globalSnippets == nil {
243						m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
244					}
245					for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
246						m.globalSnippets[key] = snip
247					}
248				} else {
249					if m.filteredSnippets == nil {
250						m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
251					}
252					for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
253						m.filteredSnippets[key] = snip
254					}
255				}
256			}
257			return m, nil
258		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
259			switch {
260			case m.previewing:
261				// The preview is a top-level overlay: it opens over the list OR the global
262				// results, so it must be checked before any mode-specific routing.
263				return m.updatePreview(msg)
264			case m.mode == modeTarget:
265				return m.updateTarget(msg)
266			case m.mode == modeProjects:
267				switch {
268				case m.globalSearching:
269					return m.updateGlobalSearch(msg)
270				case m.globalActive:
271					return m.updateGlobalResults(msg)
272				case m.projSearching:
273					return m.updateProjectSearch(msg)
274				default:
275					return m.updateProjects(msg)
276				}
277			case m.searching:
278				return m.updateSearch(msg)
279			default:
280				return m.updateList(msg)
281			}
282		}
283		return m, nil
284	}
285	
286	// refreshAfterWarm re-queries the index after the background current-project warm pass lands,
287	// so newly created or changed sessions for THIS project appear without a restart. It refreshes
288	// in place only when the home project's session list is what's on screen (preserving the
289	// cursor by session id), or re-runs a settled cross-project search to fold in the fresh rows.
290	// In any other view it just caches the refreshed sessions for when the user returns home.
291	func (m sessionTUI) refreshAfterWarm() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
292		sessions, err := m.store.ListByProject(m.homeProjectID)
293		if err != nil {
294			slog.Debug("resume: refresh after warm failed", "error", err)
295			return m, nil
296		}
297		m.homeSessions = sessions
298	
299		// In-place refresh when the home project's list is the active view.
300		if m.mode == modeList && !m.inBrowser && m.projectID == m.homeProjectID {
301			selID := ""
302			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
303				selID = sel.SessionID
304			}
305			m.all = sessions
306			m.rebuildAgentCycle()
307			m.applyFilter()
308			m.cursor = indexOfSession(m.filtered, selID)
309			m.clampScroll()
310			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
311		}
312	
313		// A settled cross-project search (not mid-typing): re-run it so refreshed rows show up.
314		// While typing, the user's own debounced queries already see the fresh data.
315		if m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive && !m.globalSearching && queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
316			m.searchSeq++
317			return m, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
318		}
319		return m, nil
320	}
321	
322	// indexOfSession returns the position of the session with id in list, or 0 when not present
323	// (so a refreshed list with a deleted selection lands safely at the top).
324	func indexOfSession(list []sessionindex.Session, id string) int {
325		if id == "" {
326			return 0
327		}
328		for i := range list {
329			if list[i].SessionID == id {
330				return i
331			}
332		}
333		return 0
334	}
335	
336	// updateList handles keys while browsing the session list.
337	func (m sessionTUI) updateList(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
338		switch msg.String() {
339		case "q", "ctrl+c":
340			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
341			return m, tea.Quit
342		case "esc":
343			// In a drilled-in project, esc returns to the browser; at home it quits.
344			if m.inBrowser {
345				m.enterBrowser()
346				return m, nil
347			}
348			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
349			return m, tea.Quit
350		case "tab":
351			// Toggle scope: home list ↔ all-projects browser.
352			m.enterBrowser()
353			return m, nil
354		case "up", "k":
355			m.moveCursor(-1)
356			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
357		case "down", "j":
358			m.moveCursor(1)
359			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
360		case "pgup":
361			m.moveCursor(-m.listHeight())
362			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
363		case "pgdown":
364			m.moveCursor(m.listHeight())
365			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
366		case "home", "g":
367			m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
368			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
369		case "end", "G":
370			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
371			m.clampScroll()
372			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
373		case "r":
374			// Resume the highlighted session (enter is deliberately a no-op here, so a stray
375			// return can't accidentally launch an agent).
376			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
377				return m.beginResume(sel)
378			}
379		case " ", "space":
380			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
381				return m.openPreview(sel)
382			}
383		case "/":
384			m.searching = true
385			m.search.SetValue(m.searchQuery)
386			return m, m.search.Focus()
387		case "a":
388			return m, m.cycleAgent()
389		case "v":
390			m.toggleViewMode()
391			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
392		}
393		return m, nil
394	}
395	
396	// updateSearch handles the full-text search input. The typed character is applied to the
397	// input immediately (instant feedback); the FTS query runs async + debounced so a slow
398	// query never blocks typing. See searchDebounceMsg / searchResultMsg.
399	func (m sessionTUI) updateSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
400		switch msg.String() {
401		case "esc":
402			m.searching = false
403			m.search.Blur()
404			m.searchQuery = ""
405			m.searchSeq++ // invalidate any in-flight search
406			m.snippetSeq++
407			m.applyFilter()
408			return m, nil
409		case "enter":
410			m.searching = false
411			m.search.Blur()
412			return m, nil
413		case "up", "down", "pgup", "pgdown":
414			// Arrows do nothing inside the one-line input, so commit the search and let the same
415			// key move the now-focused list — the user reaches a result without a separate enter.
416			m.searching = false
417			m.search.Blur()
418			return m.updateList(msg)
419		}
420		var cmd tea.Cmd
421		m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
422		m.searchQuery = m.search.Value()
423		m.searchSeq++
424		if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
425			// Too short to search: show the full (agent-filtered) list synchronously.
426			m.snippetSeq++
427			m.applyFilter()
428			return m, cmd
429		}
430		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeList))
431	}
432	
433	// updatePreview handles keys while the glamour preview reader is open. Close keys return to
434	// the underlying screen (list or global results); `r` resumes the previewed session;
435	// everything else (↑↓ pgup/pgdn) scrolls the viewport.
436	func (m sessionTUI) updatePreview(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
437		switch msg.String() {
438		case " ", "space", "esc", "q":
439			m.previewing = false
440			m.readerSession = nil
441			return m, nil
442		case "r":
443			if m.readerSession != nil {
444				sess := m.readerSession
445				m.previewing = false
446				m.readerSession = nil
447				return m.beginResume(sess)
448			}
449			return m, nil
450		}
451		var cmd tea.Cmd
452		m.reader, cmd = m.reader.Update(msg)
453		return m, cmd
454	}
455	
456	// openPreview loads the highlighted session into the glamour reader. Identical to search's
457	// openReader so the two previews render the same way.
458	func (m sessionTUI) openPreview(s *sessionindex.Session) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
459		m.readerSession = s
460		m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
461		m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
462		m.reader.SetContent(renderGlamour(sessionMarkdown(m.registry, m.store, s), m.width))
463		m.reader.GotoTop()
464		m.previewing = true
465		return m, nil
466	}
467	
468	// previewHeight is the viewport height inside the preview chrome (title + two rules + footer).
469	func (m sessionTUI) previewHeight() int {
470		const chrome = 4
471		h := m.height - chrome
472		if h < 1 {
473			h = 1
474		}
475		return h
476	}
477	
478	// updateTarget handles the target-agent selection step.
479	func (m sessionTUI) updateTarget(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
480		switch msg.String() {
481		case "esc", "q":
482			m.mode = modeList
483			m.chosen = nil
484		case "ctrl+c":
485			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
486			return m, tea.Quit
487		case "up", "k":
488			if m.targetCursor > 0 {
489				m.targetCursor--
490			}
491		case "down", "j":
492			if m.targetCursor < len(m.installed)-1 {
493				m.targetCursor++
494			}
495		case "enter":
496			m.result = sessionTUIResult{session: m.chosen, targetID: m.installed[m.targetCursor].id}
497			return m, tea.Quit
498		}
499		return m, nil
500	}
501	
502	// ---- list mechanics ----
503	
504	// moveCursorWithin advances *cursor by delta within a list of n items, clamping to
505	// [0, n-1] and then realigning the scroll window via clampScrollWithin. It is the
506	// shared core of the three list cursors (sessions, projects, global search), which
507	// differ only in which fields they track. A no-op for an empty list.
508	func moveCursorWithin(cursor, top *int, delta, n, height int) {
509		if n == 0 {
510			return
511		}
512		*cursor += delta
513		if *cursor < 0 {
514			*cursor = 0
515		}
516		if *cursor > n-1 {
517			*cursor = n - 1
518		}
519		clampScrollWithin(cursor, top, height)
520	}
521	
522	// clampScrollWithin keeps the scroll window [*top, *top+height) covering *cursor.
523	func clampScrollWithin(cursor, top *int, height int) {
524		if *cursor < *top {
525			*top = *cursor
526		}
527		if *cursor >= *top+height {
528			*top = *cursor - height + 1
529		}
530		if *top < 0 {
531			*top = 0
532		}
533	}
534	
535	func (m *sessionTUI) moveCursor(delta int) {
536		moveCursorWithin(&m.cursor, &m.top, delta, len(m.filtered), m.listHeight())
537	}
538	
539	func (m *sessionTUI) clampScroll() {
540		clampScrollWithin(&m.cursor, &m.top, m.listHeight())
541	}
542	
543	func (m sessionTUI) selected() *sessionindex.Session {
544		if m.cursor < 0 || m.cursor >= len(m.filtered) {
545			return nil
546		}
547		return &m.filtered[m.cursor]
548	}
549	
550	// rowsPerSession is how many terminal lines one list row occupies in each view mode.
551	func (m sessionTUI) rowsPerSession() int {
552		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
553			return 2
554		}
555		return 1
556	}
557	
558	// listHeight is how many sessions fit in the list region (height minus chrome).
559	func (m sessionTUI) listHeight() int {
560		const chrome = 5 // header(1) + two rules(2) + footer(1) + margin
561		avail := m.height - chrome
562		if avail < 1 {
563			avail = 1
564		}
565		n := avail / m.rowsPerSession()
566		if n < 1 {
567			n = 1
568		}
569		return n
570	}
571	
572	func (m *sessionTUI) rebuildAgentCycle() {
573		present := map[string]bool{}
574		for _, s := range m.all {
575			present[s.Agent] = true
576		}
577		ids := make([]string, 0, len(present))
578		for id := range present {
579			ids = append(ids, id)
580		}
581		sort.Strings(ids)
582		m.agentCycle = append([]string{""}, ids...)
583	}
584	
585	func (m *sessionTUI) cycleAgent() tea.Cmd {
586		// In the all-projects search, cycle over every known agent and re-run the query; in a
587		// session list, cycle over the agents actually present and re-filter in place.
588		if m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive {
589			m.agentFilter = nextInCycle(m.allAgentCycle(), m.agentFilter)
590			m.searchSeq++
591			return searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
592		}
593		m.agentFilter = nextInCycle(m.agentCycle, m.agentFilter)
594		m.applyFilter()
595		return m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
596	}
597	
598	// allAgentCycle is the agent-filter ring for cross-project search: "" then every known agent.
599	func (m sessionTUI) allAgentCycle() []string {
600		ids := make([]string, 0, len(m.agents))
601		for id := range m.agents {
602			ids = append(ids, id)
603		}
604		sort.Strings(ids)
605		return append([]string{""}, ids...)
606	}
607	
608	// nextInCycle returns the element after cur in cycle, wrapping around.
609	func nextInCycle(cycle []string, cur string) string {
610		idx := 0
611		for i, id := range cycle {
612			if id == cur {
613				idx = i
614				break
615			}
616		}
617		return cycle[(idx+1)%len(cycle)]
618	}
619	
620	func (m *sessionTUI) toggleViewMode() {
621		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
622			m.viewMode = "dense"
623		} else {
624			m.viewMode = "sparse"
625		}
626		m.clampScroll()
627	}
628	
629	// applyFilter rebuilds the visible list from the agent filter and search query. When a
630	// search is active, snippets are fetched lazily for the visible rows; otherwise the list
631	// is the project's sessions filtered by agent and snippets are cleared.
632	func (m *sessionTUI) applyFilter() {
633		m.filtered = m.filtered[:0]
634		m.filteredSnippets = nil
635	
636		if queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
637			var out []sessionindex.Session
638			if sessions, err := m.store.Search(ftsQuery(m.searchQuery), m.projectID); err == nil {
639				for _, s := range sessions {
640					if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
641						out = append(out, s)
642					}
643				}
644			}
645			m.filtered = out
646			m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
647		} else {
648			out := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, len(m.all))
649			for _, s := range m.all {
650				if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
651					out = append(out, s)
652				}
653			}
654			m.filtered = out
655		}
656	
657		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
658			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
659		}
660		if m.cursor < 0 {
661			m.cursor = 0
662		}
663		m.top = 0
664		m.clampScroll()
665	}
666	
667	// beginResume advances from a chosen session toward launch. When a target agent was
668	// pre-selected via `resume <agent>`, that choice is honored immediately and the picker exits
669	// — the user is never asked to pick a target. Otherwise it moves to the target-selection step.
670	func (m sessionTUI) beginResume(sess *sessionindex.Session) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
671		m.chosen = sess
672		if m.presetTo != "" {
673			m.result = sessionTUIResult{session: sess, targetID: m.presetTo}
674			return m, tea.Quit
675		}
676		m.mode = modeTarget
677		m.targetCursor = m.defaultTargetIndex()
678		return m, nil
679	}
680	
681	func (m sessionTUI) defaultTargetIndex() int {
682		// Prefer the last-resumed agent, else the chosen session's own agent (same-agent resume).
683		// An explicit preset (`resume <agent>`) never reaches here — beginResume skips the
684		// target-selection step entirely in that case.
685		want := m.lastAgent
686		if want == "" && m.chosen != nil {
687			want = m.chosen.Agent
688		}
689		for i, a := range m.installed {
690			if a.id == want {
691				return i
692			}
693		}
694		return 0
695	}
696	
697	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into a safe FTS5 prefix query (alnum tokens only).
698	// minQueryLen is the shortest query we run a full-text search for. A single-character
699	// prefix (e.g. "t*") matches almost the entire corpus, and ranking + snippet generation
700	// over that set is pathologically slow (~80s on a ~800-session index), so we never fire
701	// it. Shared by resume and search so both behave the same.
702	const minQueryLen = 2
703	
704	// queryReady reports whether input has enough searchable characters (letters/digits, the
705	// same runes ftsQuery keeps) to run an FTS query. Below minQueryLen we don't search.
706	func queryReady(input string) bool {
707		n := 0
708		for _, r := range input {
709			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
710				n++
711				if n >= minQueryLen {
712					return true
713				}
714			}
715		}
716		return false
717	}
718	
719	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into an FTS5 query string. Three shapes:
720	//
721	//   - Bare words become independent prefix terms, AND-ed (matched anywhere, any order) —
722	//     a loose search:  thank you      -> thank* you*
723	//   - A double-quoted span becomes a phrase: tokens adjacent and in order —
724	//     an exact phrase:  "thank you"    -> "thank you"
725	//   - A still-open quote (the user is mid-typing the phrase) keeps the final token a prefix,
726	//     so results keep updating live:  "thank yo    -> thank + yo*
727	//
728	// A single typed word that contains punctuation (e.g. a filename, "poem.txt") is itself an
729	// adjacency phrase. The index's unicode61 tokenizer splits on every non-alphanumeric run, so
730	// "poem.txt" is stored as the two adjacent tokens poem, txt — never the fused "poemtxt". We
731	// must tokenize the query the same way, or the term can't match what was indexed. So a bare
732	// "poem.txt" becomes the phrase poem + txt* (adjacent, prefix the last token for type-ahead);
733	// quoted, it is the committed phrase "poem txt" (adjacent, no prefix).
734	//
735	// Only letters and digits survive tokenization, so no FTS5 syntax character from the raw
736	// input can reach MATCH — every query this builds is safe to execute.
737	func ftsQuery(input string) string {
738		// Splitting on '"' yields alternating runs: even indexes are outside quotes, odd indexes
739		// are inside. An odd-indexed run that is also the LAST run means the closing quote hasn't
740		// been typed yet (an open phrase), so its final token stays a prefix for live type-ahead.
741		segs := strings.Split(input, `"`)
742		var parts []string
743		for i, seg := range segs {
744			switch {
745			case i%2 == 0: // outside quotes: each word is its own loose, prefix-terminated term
746				for _, f := range strings.Fields(seg) {
747					if toks := fieldTokens(f); len(toks) > 0 {
748						parts = append(parts, strings.Join(toks, " + ")+"*")
749					}
750				}
751			case i == len(segs)-1: // open phrase (still typing): adjacency across all tokens + prefix last
752				if toks := segmentTokens(seg); len(toks) > 0 {
753					parts = append(parts, strings.Join(toks, " + ")+"*")
754				}
755			default: // closed phrase: exact adjacency, no prefix (a committed phrase)
756				if toks := segmentTokens(seg); len(toks) > 0 {
757					parts = append(parts, `"`+strings.Join(toks, " ")+`"`)
758				}
759			}
760		}
761		return strings.Join(parts, " ")
762	}
763	
764	// fieldTokens splits one whitespace-delimited field into its FTS5 tokens the way the index's
765	// default unicode61 tokenizer does: every run of non-alphanumeric characters is a token
766	// boundary. So "poem.txt" -> [poem, txt] and "max-cpu!" -> [max, cpu]. Mirroring the index
767	// tokenizer here is the whole point — previously punctuation was stripped and the pieces
768	// fused into one token ("poemtxt") that the index, holding poem and txt separately, never had.
769	func fieldTokens(field string) []string {
770		var tokens []string
771		var b strings.Builder
772		flush := func() {
773			if b.Len() > 0 {
774				tokens = append(tokens, b.String())
775				b.Reset()
776			}
777		}
778		for _, r := range field {
779			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
780				b.WriteRune(r)
781			} else {
782				flush()
783			}
784		}
785		flush()
786		return tokens
787	}
788	
789	// segmentTokens tokenizes a whole quoted segment into one flat, ordered token list — both the
790	// whitespace between words and any punctuation within them are token boundaries (unicode61),
791	// so the phrase matches the adjacency the index actually stored.
792	func segmentTokens(seg string) []string {
793		var tokens []string
794		for _, f := range strings.Fields(seg) {
795			tokens = append(tokens, fieldTokens(f)...)
796		}
797		return tokens
798	}
799	
800	// ---- rendering ----
801	
802	var (
803		styDim    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
804		styFaint  = lipgloss.NewStyle().Faint(true)
805		styBold   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
806		styCursor = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("213")).Bold(true)
807		stySel    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("255")).Bold(true)
808	)
809	
810	func (m sessionTUI) View() tea.View {
811		var content string
812		switch {
813		case m.previewing:
814			content = m.renderPreview()
815		case m.mode == modeTarget:
816			content = m.renderTarget()
817		case m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive:
818			content = m.renderGlobalResults()
819		case m.mode == modeProjects:
820			content = m.renderProjects()
821		default:
822			content = m.renderListScreen()
823		}
824		v := tea.NewView(content)
825		v.AltScreen = true
826		return v
827	}
828	
829	func (m sessionTUI) renderListScreen() string {
830		var b strings.Builder
831		b.WriteString(m.renderHeader())
832		b.WriteString("\n")
833		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
834		b.WriteString("\n")
835		b.WriteString(m.renderRows())
836		b.WriteString("\n")
837		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
838		b.WriteString("\n")
839		b.WriteString(m.renderFooter())
840		return b.String()
841	}
842	
843	func (m sessionTUI) lineWidth() int {
844		if m.width < 20 {
845			return 80
846		}
847		return m.width
848	}
849	
850	// headerLeft is the shared "<title> · <scope>" left segment used by every screen.
851	func (m sessionTUI) headerLeft(scope string) string {
852		return styBold.Render(m.title) + styDim.Render(" · ") + scope
853	}
854	
855	// projectScope labels the current project, e.g. "project: getspecstory" (name in white).
856	func (m sessionTUI) projectScope() string {
857		return styDim.Render("project: ") + stySel.Render(m.projectName)
858	}
859	
860	// agentScope mirrors the project-scope label: an unstyled "all agents" phrase (rendered like
861	// "all projects") when unfiltered, else "agent: <name>" matching projectScope's "project: <name>".
862	func (m sessionTUI) agentScope() string {
863		if m.agentFilter == "" {
864			return "all agents"
865		}
866		return styDim.Render("agent: ") + stySel.Render(m.agentName(m.agentFilter))
867	}
868	
869	func (m sessionTUI) renderHeader() string {
870		// Agent filter sits left, right after the project, rather than tucked in the far corner.
871		left := m.headerLeft(m.projectScope()) + styDim.Render("  ·  ") + m.agentScope()
872		return headerRow(left, "", m.lineWidth())
873	}
874	
875	func (m sessionTUI) renderRows() string {
876		if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
877			return styFaint.Render("  No sessions match.")
878		}
879		h := m.listHeight()
880		end := min(m.top+h, len(m.filtered))
881		var b strings.Builder
882		for i := m.top; i < end; i++ {
883			b.WriteString(m.sessionRow(m.filtered[i], i == m.cursor, m.snippetAt(i)))
884			if i < end-1 {
885				b.WriteString("\n")
886			}
887		}
888		return b.String()
889	}
890	
891	// snippetAt returns the search snippet for filtered row i, or "" (no active search).
892	func (m sessionTUI) snippetAt(i int) string {
893		if i >= 0 && i < len(m.filtered) {
894			s := m.filtered[i]
895			return m.filteredSnippets[sessionindex.FingerprintKey(s.Agent, s.SessionID)]
896		}
897		return ""
898	}
899	
900	// rowCursor renders the two-column selection gutter shared by every list row.
901	func rowCursor(selected bool) string {
902		if selected {
903			return styCursor.Render("▸ ")
904		}
905		return "  "
906	}
907	
908	// rowLabel picks a list row's primary text: the highlighted FTS snippet when a search
909	// is active, otherwise the session title. titleWidth/snippetWidth are the separate
910	// column budgets for each (snippet markup needs slightly more room). Shared by
911	// sessionRow and globalRow.
912	func rowLabel(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string, titleWidth, snippetWidth int) string {
913		if snippet != "" {
914			return renderSnippet(snippet, snippetWidth)
915		}
916		return renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, titleWidth)
917	}
918	
919	// sessionRow renders one list row. When snippet is non-empty (search active) the row
920	// shows the highlighted match context instead of the session title.
921	func (m sessionTUI) sessionRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
922		cursor := rowCursor(selected)
923		agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
924		when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
925	
926		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
927			turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d prompts", s.UserTurns))
928			label := rowLabel(s, selected, snippet, m.lineWidth()-24, m.lineWidth()-26)
929			head := cursor + agent + "  " + label + "   " + turns
930			sub := "    " + styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s ago · %s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt), shortID(s.SessionID)))
931			return head + "\n" + sub
932		}
933		turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%4d", s.UserTurns))
934		// A year-stamped date ("Dec 31 '25") is wider than the 4-col slot; shrink the label by the
935		// overflow so the right-hand turns column can't get pushed off the line and wrap.
936		extra := len(when) - 4
937		if extra < 0 {
938			extra = 0
939		}
940		label := rowLabel(s, selected, snippet, m.lineWidth()-22-extra, m.lineWidth()-24-extra)
941		return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + label + "  " + turns
942	}
943	
944	// renderSnippet renders a FTS snippet (matched terms wrapped in the control-char marks)
945	// with the matches highlighted, collapsing whitespace and clipping to maxWidth columns.
946	func renderSnippet(snip string, maxWidth int) string {
947		if maxWidth < 8 {
948			maxWidth = 8
949		}
950		snip = strings.NewReplacer("\n", " ", "\t", " ", "\r", " ").Replace(snip)
951		hl := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("232")).Background(lipgloss.Color("221"))
952	
953		var b strings.Builder
954		var seg strings.Builder
955		matched := false
956		visible := 0
957		flush := func() {
958			if seg.Len() == 0 {
959				return
960			}
961			if matched {
962				b.WriteString(hl.Render(seg.String()))
963			} else {
964				b.WriteString(seg.String())
965			}
966			seg.Reset()
967		}
968		for _, r := range snip {
969			switch r {
970			case '\x02':
971				flush()
972				matched = true
973				continue
974			case '\x03':
975				flush()
976				matched = false
977				continue
978			}
979			if visible >= maxWidth {
980				flush()
981				b.WriteString("…")
982				return b.String()
983			}
984			seg.WriteRune(r)
985			visible++
986		}
987		flush()
988		return b.String()
989	}
990	
991	func renderName(name string, selected bool, width int) string {
992		if width < 8 {
993			width = 8
994		}
995		t := truncate(name, width)
996		if selected {
997			return stySel.Render(t)
998		}
999		return t
1000	}
1001	
1002	func (m sessionTUI) renderFooter() string {
1003		if m.searching {
1004			return m.search.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render("esc clear · enter apply")
1005		}
1006		scopeKey := "tab all-projects"
1007		if m.inBrowser {
1008			scopeKey = "tab/esc back"
1009		}
1010		keys := []string{"↑↓ move", "r resume", "space preview", "/ search", "a agent", scopeKey, "v " + m.viewMode, "q quit"}
1011		return styDim.Render(strings.Join(keys, " · "))

1019				styDim.Render(" · ") + sessionTitle(*s)

1049			}

1074	// sessionTitle is the human label for a session: name, then slug, then short id.
1075	func sessionTitle(s sessionindex.Session) string {
1076		switch {
1077		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Name) != "":
1078			return s.Name
1079		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Slug) != "":
1080			return s.Slug
1081		default:
1082			return shortID(s.SessionID)
1083		}
1084	}
1085	
1086	// truncate shortens s to n runes with an ellipsis.
1087	func truncate(s string, n int) string {
1088		r := []rune(s)
1089		if len(r) <= n {
1090			return s
1091		}
1092		if n < 1 {
1093			return ""
1094		}
1095		return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
1096	}
1097	
1098	// relativeTime renders an ISO 8601 timestamp as a compact "2m"/"3h"/"5d"/"Jun 2".
1099	func relativeTime(iso string) string {
1100		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
1101		if err != nil {
1102			return ""
1103		}
1104		d := time.Since(t)
1105		switch {
1106		case d < time.Minute:
1107			return "now"
1108		case d < time.Hour:
1109			return fmt.Sprintf("%dm", int(d.Minutes()))
1110		case d < 24*time.Hour:
1111			return fmt.Sprintf("%dh", int(d.Hours()))
1112		case d < 7*24*time.Hour:
1113			return fmt.Sprintf("%dd", int(d.Hours()/24))
1114		default:
1115			lt := t.Local()
1116			if lt.Year() == time.Now().Year() {
1117				return lt.Format("Jan 2")
1118			}
1119			// Disambiguate prior years so "Dec 31" can't be mistaken for this year: "Dec 31 '25".
1120			return lt.Format("Jan 2 '06")
1121		}
1122	}
1123	
1124	// headerRow lays out a left and right segment across width with a gap between.
1125	func headerRow(left, right string, width int) string {
1126		gap := width - lipgloss.Width(left) - lipgloss.Width(right)
1127		if gap < 1 {
1128			gap = 1
1129		}
1130		return left + strings.Repeat(" ", gap) + right
1131	}
1132	
1133	// renderGlamour renders markdown to styled terminal output for the preview reader.
1134	func renderGlamour(md string, width int) string {
1135		if width < 20 {
1136			width = 80
1137		}
1138		r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"), glamour.WithWordWrap(width-2))
1139		if err != nil {
1140			return md
1141		}
1142		out, err := r.Render(md)
1143		if err != nil {
1144			return md
1145		}
1146		return out
1147	}
1148	
1149	// sessionMarkdown returns the session as markdown for the preview — the real specstory
1150	// render when the session can be re-parsed (needs a cwd), else the plain FTS body.
1151	func sessionMarkdown(registry *factory.Registry, store *sessionindex.Store, s *sessionindex.Session) string {
1152		if s.OriginCwd != "" {
1153			if prov, err := registry.Get(s.Agent); err == nil {
1154				if full, err := prov.GetAgentChatSession(s.OriginCwd, s.SessionID, false); err == nil &&
1155					full != nil && full.SessionData != nil {
1156					if md, err := session.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(full.SessionData, false, true); err == nil {
1157						return md
1158					}
1159				}
1160			}
1161		}
1162		if body, _ := store.SessionBody(s.Agent, s.SessionID); strings.TrimSpace(body) != "" {
1163			return "```\n" + body + "\n```"
1164		}
1165		return "_(no readable content for this session)_"
1166	}
1167	
1168	// ---- integration with the resume command ----
1169	
1170	// openOrBuildResumeIndex opens sessions.db, building it first (with the normal reindex
1171	// UI) when it is missing or empty, then proceeding straight into the picker. builtFresh
1172	// reports whether a full foreground build just ran — when it did, the index is already
1173	// fully current, so callers skip the background warm (it would be redundant).
1174	func openOrBuildResumeIndex() (store *sessionindex.Store, builtFresh bool, err error) {
1175		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
1176		if err != nil {
1177			return nil, false, err
1178		}
1179		if _, statErr := os.Stat(dbPath); statErr == nil {
1180			s, err := sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
1181			if err != nil {
1182				return nil, false, err
1183			}
1184			if n, _ := s.Count(); n > 0 {
1185				return s, false, nil
1186			}
1187			_ = s.Close() // empty → rebuild below
1188		}
1189		if err := runReindex(false); err != nil {
1190			return nil, false, err
1191		}
1192		s, err := sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
1193		return s, true, err
1194	}
1195	
1196	// indexWarmedMsg is sent to the running TUI when the background warm finishes refreshing the
1197	// CURRENT project (the first warm pass). The model re-queries so new/changed sessions for this
1198	// project appear without a restart. The subsequent full pass is silent (no refresh).
1199	type indexWarmedMsg struct{}
1200	
1201	// startIndexWarm launches the background two-pass index warm for projectID, refreshing the
1202	// running TUI (via p.Send) when the current-project pass completes. The returned cancel func
1203	// should be invoked when the TUI exits: the warm is best-effort and must never block process
1204	// exit. When builtFresh is true the index was just rebuilt in the foreground, so there is
1205	// nothing to warm and this only returns a cancel func.
1206	func startIndexWarm(p *tea.Program, projectID string, builtFresh bool) context.CancelFunc {
1207		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1208		if builtFresh {
1209			return cancel
1210		}
1211		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
1212		if err != nil {
1213			slog.Debug("warm: no db path; skipping", "error", err)
1214			return cancel
1215		}
1216		go warmIndexInBackground(ctx, dbPath, projectID, func() { p.Send(indexWarmedMsg{}) })
1217		return cancel
1218	}
1219	
1220	// selectResumeViaTUI runs the picker for the current project and returns the chosen
1221	// resume plan (or nil if the user cancelled). On a successful selection it persists the
1222	// view-mode and target-agent preferences to the user config.
1223	func selectResumeViaTUI(registry *factory.Registry, store *sessionindex.Store, projectID, projectName, presetTo string, builtFresh bool) (*resumePlan, error) {
1224		sessions, err := store.ListByProject(projectID)
1225		if err != nil {
1226			return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading sessions: %w", err)
1227		}
1228		// An empty current project is fine — the picker opens in the all-projects browser.
1229		// Only bail when the whole index is empty (nothing to resume anywhere).
1230		if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
1231			fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent here, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
1232			return nil, nil
1233		}
1234	
1235		agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
1236		var installed []agentChoice
1237		for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
1238			prov, err := registry.Get(id)
1239			if err != nil {
1240				continue
1241			}
1242			agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
1243			if prov.Check("").Success {
1244				installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
1245			}
1246		}
1247		if len(installed) == 0 {
1248			fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo installed agents found to resume into.")
1249			return nil, nil
1250		}
1251	
1252		// A preset target (`resume <agent>`) must be installed to resume into. The command already
1253		// confirmed it is a known provider; here, with the installed set known, confirm it is
1254		// available and resolve it to its canonical ID. When set, the picker skips the
1255		// target-selection step entirely (see beginResume).
1256		if presetTo != "" {
1257			resolved := ""
1258			for _, a := range installed {
1259				if strings.EqualFold(a.id, presetTo) {
1260					resolved = a.id
1261					break
1262				}
1263			}
1264			if resolved == "" {
1265				return nil, utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
1266					"agent %q is not installed, so it can't be a resume target.", presetTo)}
1267			}
1268			presetTo = resolved
1269		}
1270	
1271		viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
1272		if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
1273			viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
1274			lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
1275		}
1276	
1277		model := newSessionTUI(store, registry, projectID, projectName, sessions, agents, installed, sessionTUIOpts{
1278			title:     "SpecStory Resume",
1279			presetTo:  presetTo,
1280			lastAgent: lastAgent,
1281			viewMode:  viewMode,
1282		})
1283		p := tea.NewProgram(model)
1284		cancelWarm := startIndexWarm(p, projectID, builtFresh)
1285		final, err := p.Run()
1286		cancelWarm() // stop warming the moment the picker exits (never block on it)
1287		if err != nil {
1288			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resume picker failed: %w", err)
1289		}
1290		rm, ok := final.(sessionTUI)
1291		if !ok {
1292			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resume picker returned unexpected model type %T", final)
1293		}
1294		if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
1295			return nil, nil
1296		}
1297	
1298		fromID := rm.result.session.Agent
1299		fromProv, err := registry.Get(fromID)
1300		if err != nil {
1301			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", fromID, err)
1302		}
1303		toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
1304		if err != nil {
1305			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
1306		}
1307	
1308		if err := config.SaveResumePrefs(rm.viewMode, rm.result.targetID); err != nil {
1309			slog.Debug("resume: could not save prefs", "error", err)
1310		}
1311	
1312		return &resumePlan{
1313			from:      fromProv,
1314			fromID:    fromID,
1315			sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
1316			fromCwd:   rm.result.session.OriginCwd,
1317			to:        toProv,
1318			toID:      rm.result.targetID,
1319		}, nil
1320	}
1321	
1322	// colorForAgent returns a stable accent color per provider for the list.
1323	func colorForAgent(id string) color.Color {
1324		switch id {
1325		case "claude":
1326			return lipgloss.Color("170") // purple
1327		case "codex":
1328			return lipgloss.Color("42") // green
1329		case "cursor":
1330			return lipgloss.Color("39") // blue
1331		case "gemini":
1332			return lipgloss.Color("45") // cyan
1333		case "droid":
1334			return lipgloss.Color("214") // orange
1335		case "deepseek":
1336			return lipgloss.Color("203") // red
1337		default:
1338			return lipgloss.Color("250")
1339		}
1340	}

1342	// ---- all-projects browser (Stage B) ----
1343	
1344	// enterBrowser switches to the all-projects browser, loading the project rollup lazily.
1345	func (m *sessionTUI) enterBrowser() {
1346		if !m.projectsLoaded {

1348				m.projects = ps
1349			}
1350			m.projectsLoaded = true
1351		}

1356	// gotoHome returns the session list to the current directory's project.
1357	func (m *sessionTUI) gotoHome() {
1358		m.projectID, m.projectName = m.homeProjectID, m.homeProjectName
1359		m.all = m.homeSessions
1360		m.inBrowser = false
1361		m.agentFilter, m.searchQuery = "", ""
1362		m.search.SetValue("")
1363		m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
1364		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
1365		m.applyFilter()
1366		m.mode = modeList
1367	}
1368	
1369	// drillInto opens a project's session list from the browser.
1370	func (m *sessionTUI) drillInto(p sessionindex.ProjectSummary) {
1371		sessions, err := m.store.ListByProject(p.ProjectID)
1372		if err != nil {
1373			return
1374		}
1375		m.projectID = p.ProjectID
1376		m.projectName = projectDisplayName(p)
1377		m.all = sessions
1378		m.inBrowser = true
1379		m.agentFilter, m.searchQuery = "", ""
1380		m.search.SetValue("")
1381		m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
1382		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
1383		m.applyFilter()
1384		m.mode = modeList
1385	}
1386	
1387	func (m sessionTUI) updateProjects(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1388		switch msg.String() {
1389		case "q", "ctrl+c":
1390			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1391			return m, tea.Quit
1392		case "esc":
1393			if m.startedInBrowser {
1394				m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1395				return m, tea.Quit
1396			}
1397			m.gotoHome()
1398		case "tab":
1399			if !m.startedInBrowser {
1400				m.gotoHome()
1401			}
1402		case "up", "k":
1403			m.moveProjCursor(-1)
1404		case "down", "j":
1405			m.moveProjCursor(1)
1406		case "pgup":
1407			m.moveProjCursor(-m.projectsHeight())
1408		case "pgdown":
1409			m.moveProjCursor(m.projectsHeight())
1410		case "home", "g":
1411			m.projCursor, m.projTop = 0, 0
1412		case "end", "G":
1413			m.projCursor = len(m.projFiltered) - 1
1414			m.clampProjScroll()
1415		case "enter":
1416			if m.projCursor >= 0 && m.projCursor < len(m.projFiltered) {
1417				m.drillInto(m.projFiltered[m.projCursor])
1418			}
1419		case "/":
1420			// FTS over sessions across ALL projects (consistent with / in a session list).
1421			m.globalActive = true
1422			m.globalSearching = true
1423			m.globalInput.SetValue(m.globalQuery)
1424			return m, m.globalInput.Focus()
1425		case "p":
1426			// Filter the project list by name.
1427			m.projSearching = true
1428			m.projSearch.SetValue(m.projSearchQuery)
1429			return m, m.projSearch.Focus()
1430		}
1431		return m, nil
1432	}
1433	
1434	func (m sessionTUI) updateProjectSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1435		switch msg.String() {
1436		case "esc":
1437			m.projSearching = false
1438			m.projSearch.Blur()
1439			m.projSearchQuery = ""
1440			m.applyProjectFilter()
1441			return m, nil
1442		case "enter":
1443			m.projSearching = false
1444			m.projSearch.Blur()
1445			return m, nil
1446		}
1447		var cmd tea.Cmd
1448		m.projSearch, cmd = m.projSearch.Update(msg)
1449		m.projSearchQuery = m.projSearch.Value()
1450		m.applyProjectFilter()
1451		return m, cmd
1452	}
1453	
1454	func (m *sessionTUI) applyProjectFilter() {
1455		q := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(m.projSearchQuery))
1456		if q == "" {
1457			m.projFiltered = m.projects
1458		} else {
1459			out := make([]sessionindex.ProjectSummary, 0, len(m.projects))
1460			for _, p := range m.projects {
1461				if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(projectDisplayName(p)), q) {
1462					out = append(out, p)
1463				}
1464			}
1465			m.projFiltered = out
1466		}
1467		if m.projCursor > len(m.projFiltered)-1 {
1468			m.projCursor = len(m.projFiltered) - 1
1469		}
1470		if m.projCursor < 0 {
1471			m.projCursor = 0
1472		}
1473		m.projTop = 0
1474	}
1475	
1476	func (m *sessionTUI) moveProjCursor(delta int) {
1477		moveCursorWithin(&m.projCursor, &m.projTop, delta, len(m.projFiltered), m.projectsHeight())
1478	}
1479	
1480	func (m *sessionTUI) clampProjScroll() {
1481		clampScrollWithin(&m.projCursor, &m.projTop, m.projectsHeight())
1482	}
1483	
1484	// projectsHeight reserves room for chrome plus a few date-bucket header lines.
1485	func (m sessionTUI) projectsHeight() int {
1486		const chrome = 9
1487		avail := m.height - chrome
1488		if avail < 1 {
1489			avail = 1
1490		}
1491		return avail
1492	}
```

```text
1571	// dateBucket groups a timestamp into a relative bucket for the browser rollup.
1572	func dateBucket(iso string) string {
1573		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
1574		if err != nil {
1575			return "Older"
1576		}
1577		switch d := dayDiff(t.Local()); {
1578		case d <= 0:
1579			return "Today"
1580		case d == 1:
1581			return "Yesterday"
1582		case d < 7:
1583			return "Previous 7 days"
1584		case d < 30:
1585			return "Previous 30 days"
1586		default:
1587			return "Older"
1588		}
1589	}
1590	
1591	// dayDiff returns whole calendar days between now and t (both local), now - t.
1592	func dayDiff(t time.Time) int {
1593		now := time.Now()
1594		ny, nm, nd := now.Date()
1595		ty, tm, td := t.Date()
1596		a := time.Date(ny, nm, nd, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1597		c := time.Date(ty, tm, td, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1598		return int(a.Sub(c).Hours() / 24)
1599	}

1603	func (m sessionTUI) updateGlobalSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1604		switch msg.String() {

1621		case "up", "down", "pgup", "pgdown":
1622			// Arrows are useless in the one-line input, so commit and let the same key move into
1623			// the results — reach a hit without a separate enter.
1624			m.globalSearching = false
1625			m.globalInput.Blur()

1633			// Too short to search the whole corpus: no results until the 2nd character.
1634			m.globalResults = nil
1635			m.globalSnippets = nil
1636			m.snippetSeq++
1637			return m, cmd
1638		}
1639		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects))
1640	}

1643		switch msg.String() {
1644		case "q", "ctrl+c":
1645			m.result = sessionTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1646			return m, tea.Quit
1647		case "esc":
1648			// In `search`, the search is the root → esc quits. In `resume` (reached via the

1673		case "home", "g":

1699	// globalSelected returns the highlighted cross-project hit, or nil.
1700	func (m sessionTUI) globalSelected() *sessionindex.Session {
1701		if m.globalCursor < 0 || m.globalCursor >= len(m.globalResults) {
1702			return nil
1703		}

1706	
1707	// toggleGlobalScope flips the cross-project search between all projects and a single project
1708	// (tab): the highlighted hit's project when a result is selected, else the current directory's
1709	// project (nothing to point at). A second tab widens back to all projects. It then re-runs the
1710	// query from the top against the new scope.
1711	func (m *sessionTUI) toggleGlobalScope() tea.Cmd {
1712		switch {
1713		case m.globalScopeID != "":
1714			// Already scoped → widen back to all projects.
1715			m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = "", ""
1716		case m.globalSelected() != nil:
1717			// A result is highlighted → scope to its project.
1718			sel := m.globalSelected()
1719			m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = sel.ProjectID, sessionProject(*sel)
1720		default:
1721			// No results to point at → scope to the current directory's project.
1722			m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = m.homeProjectID, m.homeProjectName
1723		}
1724		m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1725		m.searchSeq++
1726		if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
1727			m.globalResults = nil
1728			m.globalSnippets = nil
1729			m.snippetSeq++
1730			return nil
1731		}
1732		return searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects)
1733	}

1735	func (m *sessionTUI) exitGlobal() {

1740		m.globalQuery = ""
1741		m.globalScopeID, m.globalScopeName = "", "" // re-enter search at all-projects scope
1742		m.globalResults = nil
1743		m.globalSnippets = nil
1744		m.snippetSeq++
1745		m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1746	}

1836	// globalRow renders a cross-project search hit: agent · time · project · highlighted match
1837	// snippet. Honors the dense/sparse view mode, matching the project session list.
1838	func (m sessionTUI) globalRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
1839		cursor := rowCursor(selected)
1840		agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
1841		proj := fmt.Sprintf("%-18s", truncate(sessionProject(s), 18))
1842	
1843		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
1844			label := rowLabel(s, selected, snippet, m.lineWidth()-30, m.lineWidth()-32)
1845			head := cursor + agent + "  " + styFaint.Render(proj) + "  " + label
1846			sub := "    " + styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s ago · %s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt), shortID(s.SessionID)))
1847			return head + "\n" + sub
1848		}
1849	
1850		when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
1851		label := rowLabel(s, selected, snippet, m.lineWidth()-46, m.lineWidth()-48)
1852		return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + styFaint.Render(proj) + "  " + label
1853	}

1855	func sessionProject(s sessionindex.Session) string {
1856		if strings.TrimSpace(s.ProjectName) != "" {
1857			return s.ProjectName
1858		}
1859		if s.ProjectID == unknownProjectID || s.ProjectID == "" {
1860			return "(unknown)"
1861		}
1862		return s.ProjectID
1863	}
1864	
1865	// ---- async, debounced full-text search ----
1866	
1867	// searchDebounceMsg fires after a brief typing pause; the query runs only if its seq is
1868	// still current. searchResultMsg carries the async FTS results back to the model. Both
1869	// keep typing instant: the input updates synchronously while the query runs off-thread.
1870	type searchDebounceMsg struct {
1871		seq  int
1872		kind tuiMode // modeList (project-scoped) or modeProjects (global)
1873	}
1874	
1875	type searchResultMsg struct {
1876		seq      int
1877		kind     tuiMode
1878		sessions []sessionindex.Session
1879	}
1880	
1881	type snippetResultMsg struct {
1882		seq      int
1883		kind     tuiMode
1884		snippets map[string]string
1885	}
1886	
1887	const searchDebounceDelay = 50 * time.Millisecond
1888	
1889	// searchDebounce schedules a debounce tick. On fire, the model checks the seq and decides
1890	// whether to actually run the query (so only the last keystroke in a burst queries).
1891	func searchDebounce(seq int, kind tuiMode) tea.Cmd {
1892		return tea.Tick(searchDebounceDelay, func(time.Time) tea.Msg {
1893			return searchDebounceMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind}
1894		})
1895	}
1896	
1897	// runSearch returns a command that performs the FTS query off the UI thread.
1898	func (m sessionTUI) runSearch(seq int, kind tuiMode, ctx context.Context) tea.Cmd {
1899		store := m.store
1900		query, projectID := m.searchQuery, m.projectID
1901		if kind == modeProjects {
1902			query, projectID = m.globalQuery, m.globalScopeID // "" = all projects, else scoped
1903		}
1904		fq := ftsQuery(query)
1905		return func() tea.Msg {
1906			if !queryReady(query) {
1907				return searchResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind}
1908			}
1909			sessions, _ := store.SearchContext(ctx, fq, projectID)
1910			return searchResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind, sessions: sessions}
1911		}
1912	}
1913	
1914	// applySearchResults installs async FTS results into the matching list (scoped or global).
1915	func (m *sessionTUI) applySearchResults(kind tuiMode, sessions []sessionindex.Session) {
1916		if kind == modeProjects {
1917			m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
1918			var out []sessionindex.Session
1919			for _, s := range sessions {
1920				if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
1921					out = append(out, s)
1922				}
1923			}
1924			m.globalResults = out
1925			if m.globalCursor > len(m.globalResults)-1 {
1926				m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1927			}
1928			if m.globalCursor < 0 {
1929				m.globalCursor = 0
1930			}
1931			m.globalTop = 0
1932			return
1933		}
1934	
1935		var out []sessionindex.Session
1936		for _, s := range sessions {
1937			if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
1938				out = append(out, s)
1939			}
1940		}
1941		m.filtered = out
1942		m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
1943		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
1944			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
1945		}
1946		if m.cursor < 0 {
1947			m.cursor = 0
1948		}
1949		m.top = 0
1950		m.clampScroll()
1951	}
1952	
1953	func (m *sessionTUI) requestVisibleSnippets(kind tuiMode) tea.Cmd {
1954		var query string
1955		var rows []sessionindex.Session
1956		if kind == modeProjects {
1957			if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) || len(m.globalResults) == 0 {
1958				return nil
1959			}
1960			h := m.globalHeight()
1961			end := min(m.globalTop+h, len(m.globalResults))
1962			if m.globalTop >= end {
1963				return nil
1964			}
1965			query = m.globalQuery
1966			rows = append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.globalResults[m.globalTop:end]...)
1967		} else {
1968			if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) || len(m.filtered) == 0 {
1969				return nil
1970			}
1971			h := m.listHeight()
1972			end := min(m.top+h, len(m.filtered))
1973			if m.top >= end {
1974				return nil
1975			}
1976			query = m.searchQuery
1977			rows = append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.filtered[m.top:end]...)

1979	
1980		store := m.store
1981		fq := ftsQuery(query)
1982		m.snippetSeq++
1983		seq := m.snippetSeq
1984		return func() tea.Msg {
1985			snips, _ := store.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), fq, rows)
1986			return snippetResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind, snippets: snips}
```

```text
=== ScanReporter impl ===
// render a live "scanning" display. It is safe for concurrent use and safe to call on a nil
// receiver (a no-op), so providers can report unconditionally.
type ScanReporter struct {
	found atomic.Int64
}

// Add records that n more sessions have been found. Files that yield no session (warmup-only or
// sidechain-only transcripts) are deliberately not counted, so the running total reflects real
// sessions rather than raw files.
func (r *ScanReporter) Add(n int) {
	if r != nil {
		r.found.Add(int64(n))
	}
}

// Found returns the number of sessions found so far.
func (r *ScanReporter) Found() int64 {
	if r == nil {
		return 0
	}
	return r.found.Load()
}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
154	+// projectDirNameRegex matches any character that is not alphanumeric or a dash.
155	+// Claude Code replaces each such character with a dash to map a working directory

158	+
159	+// encodeProjectDirName converts a real (symlink-resolved) path into Claude Code's
160	+// project directory name: non-alphanumeric/dash characters become dashes, with a
161	+// guaranteed leading dash. Example: "/Users/sean/app" -> "-Users-sean-app".
162	+func encodeProjectDirName(realPath string) string {
163	+	name := projectDirNameRegex.ReplaceAllString(realPath, "-")
164	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") {
165	+		name = "-" + name
166	+	}
167	+	return name
168	+}
169	+
170	+// resolveClaudeProjectDir returns ~/.claude/projects/<encoded> for the given
171	+// project path, resolving symlinks, WITHOUT requiring the directory to exist.
172	+// Used when writing a reconstructed session into the store (the caller creates
173	+// the directory). Distinct from GetClaudeCodeProjectDir, which requires the
174	+// projects directory to already exist.
175	+func resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
176	+	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
177	+	if err != nil {
178	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
179	+	}
180	+
181	+	cwd := projectPath
182	+	if cwd == "" {
183	+		cwd, err = os.Getwd()
184	+		if err != nil {
185	+			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
186	+		}
187	+	}
188	+
189	+	// Resolve symlinks to match Claude Code's behavior; fall back to the raw path
190	+	// if resolution fails (e.g., a path component does not exist yet).
191	+	realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
192	+	if err != nil {
193	+		realPath = cwd
194	+	}
195	+
196	+	return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects", encodeProjectDirName(realPath)), nil
197	+}

396	+// extractCommandName pulls the slash command from a Claude Code command record, e.g.
397	+// "<command-name>/code-review</command-name>…" -> "/code-review". Falls back to the
398	+// bare command-message name (prefixed with "/"). Returns "" when none is present.
399	+func extractCommandName(content string) string {
400	+	if c := between(content, "<command-name>", "</command-name>"); c != "" {
401	+		return c
402	+	}
403	+	if c := between(content, "<command-message>", "</command-message>"); c != "" {
404	+		return "/" + c
405	+	}
406	+	return ""
407	+}

411	+// between returns the text between the first open/close markers, trimmed, or "".
412	+func between(s, openTag, closeTag string) string {
413	+	_, after, ok := strings.Cut(s, openTag)
414	+	if !ok {
415	+		return ""
416	+	}
417	+	inner, _, ok := strings.Cut(after, closeTag)
418	+	if !ok {
419	+		return ""
420	+	}
421	+	return strings.TrimSpace(inner)
422	+}

433	+	if !scan.foundRealMessage {
434	+		return nil, nil
435	+	}

445	+		Name:      spi.GenerateReadableName(scan.firstUserMessage),

449	+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Claude Code session across all projects. It is the
450	+// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
451	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
452	+	return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
453	+}
454	+
455	+// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Claude Code session across all projects by
456	+// walking ~/.claude/projects/*/ for *.jsonl (the originating cwd comes from inside each session;
457	+// the project directory name is a lossy, irreversible encoding of the path), reporting scan
458	+// progress into r (nil-safe). Headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
459	+// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
460	+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
461	+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
462	+	projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
463	+	if err != nil {
464	+		// No projects directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
465	+		return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
466	+	}
467	+
468	+	// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
469	+	var paths []string
470	+	walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(projectsDir, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
471	+		if err != nil {
472	+			return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the whole sweep
473	+		}
474	+		if d.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(path, ".jsonl") {
475	+			return nil
476	+		}
477	+		paths = append(paths, path)
478	+		return nil
479	+	})
480	+	if walkErr != nil {
481	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk claude projects: %w", walkErr)
482	+	}
483	+
484	+	// Phase 2: scan session headers in parallel — each file's read+parse is independent and
485	+	// CPU-bound, so this scales across cores.
486	+	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
487	+	if workers < 1 {
488	+		workers = 1
489	+	}
490	+	pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
491	+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
492	+	var mu sync.Mutex
493	+	var wg sync.WaitGroup
494	+	for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
495	+		wg.Add(1)
496	+		go func() {
497	+			defer wg.Done()
498	+			for path := range pathCh {
499	+				scan, scanErr := scanClaudeSession(path)
500	+				if scanErr != nil {
501	+					slog.Warn("reindex: failed to scan claude session", "file", path, "error", scanErr)
502	+					continue
503	+				}
504	+				if !scan.foundRealMessage {
505	+					continue // warmup-only or sidechain-only transcript (not a session)
506	+				}
507	+				ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
508	+					SessionID:  scan.sessionID,
509	+					CreatedAt:  scan.timestamp,
510	+					Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(scan.firstUserMessage),
511	+					Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(scan.firstUserMessage),
512	+					NativePath: path,
513	+					OriginCwd:  scan.cwd,
514	+				}
515	+				mu.Lock()
516	+				refs = append(refs, ref)
517	+				mu.Unlock()
518	+				r.Add(1) // count sessions found, not files scanned (warmup/sidechain files yield none)
519	+			}
520	+		}()
521	+	}
522	+	for _, path := range paths {
523	+		pathCh <- path
524	+	}
525	+	close(pathCh)
526	+	wg.Wait()
527	+	return refs, nil
528	+}

578	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go
579	new file mode 100644
580	index 0000000..5040b40
581	--- /dev/null
582	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go
583	@@ -0,0 +1,118 @@
584	+package claudecode
585	+
586	+import (
587	+	"bytes"
588	+	"encoding/json"
589	+	"fmt"
590	+	"path/filepath"
591	+	"time"
592	+
593	+	"github.com/google/uuid"
594	+
595	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
596	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
597	+)
598	+
599	+const (
600	+	// reconstructedClaudeModel is a placeholder model label for reconstructed
601	+	// assistant records. It is only a historical display value — on resume Claude
602	+	// Code uses its own configured model for the next turn.
603	+	reconstructedClaudeModel = "claude-opus-4-8"
604	+
605	+	// reconstructedClaudeVersion mirrors the `version` field Claude Code writes on
606	+	// its records. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the
607	+	// chance the loader accepts a synthesized file.
608	+	reconstructedClaudeVersion = "2.1.178"
609	+)
610	+
611	+// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Claude Code native JSONL session from the neutral
612	+// SessionData so `claude --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
613	+//
614	+// The forward parser collapses Claude's parentUuid DAG and drops the API
615	+// message.id and thinking signatures, so reconstruction synthesizes a fresh,
616	+// linear parentUuid chain of plain user/assistant text records (tool calls and
617	+// thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData). This is
618	+// the structure validated by the Stage 1 spike. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
619	+func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
620	+	turns, err := spi.PrepareTurns(data, opts)
621	+	if err != nil {
622	+		return nil, err
623	+	}
624	+	cwd := spi.ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts, data)
625	+
626	+	newID := uuid.NewString()
627	+	base := time.Now().UTC()
628	+
629	+	var buf bytes.Buffer
630	+	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
631	+	// Preserve <, >, & in conversation/markdown text rather than \u-escaping them.
632	+	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
633	+
634	+	var prevUUID string
635	+	for i, turn := range turns {
636	+		recUUID := uuid.NewString()
637	+		ts := base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second).Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
638	+
639	+		rec := map[string]interface{}{
640	+			"isSidechain": false,
641	+			"userType":    "external",
642	+			"cwd":         cwd,
643	+			"sessionId":   newID,
644	+			"version":     reconstructedClaudeVersion,
645	+			"gitBranch":   "",
646	+			"uuid":        recUUID,
647	+			"timestamp":   ts,
648	+		}
649	+
650	+		// Linear chain: the first record roots the DAG (parentUuid null) and
651	+		// carries provenance back to the source session.
652	+		if prevUUID == "" {
653	+			rec["parentUuid"] = nil
654	+			rec["specstorySourceSessionId"] = data.SessionID
655	+		} else {
656	+			rec["parentUuid"] = prevUUID
657	+		}
658	+
659	+		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
660	+			rec["type"] = "user"
661	+			rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
662	+				"role":    "user",
663	+				"content": turn.Text,
664	+			}
665	+		} else {
666	+			rec["type"] = "assistant"
667	+			rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
668	+				"id":          "msg_" + recUUID,
669	+				"type":        "message",
670	+				"role":        "assistant",
671	+				"model":       reconstructedClaudeModel,
672	+				"content":     []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
673	+				"stop_reason": "end_turn",
674	+				"usage":       map[string]interface{}{"input_tokens": 1, "output_tokens": 1},
675	+			}
676	+		}
677	+
678	+		if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
679	+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode reconstructed record: %w", err)
680	+		}
681	+
682	+		prevUUID = recUUID
683	+	}
684	+
685	+	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
686	+		SessionID: newID,
687	+		Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
688	+		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
689	+	}, nil
690	+}
691	+
692	+// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session file belongs in Claude
693	+// Code's store: ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not
694	+// required to exist (the caller creates it).
695	+func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
696	+	dir, err := resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath)
697	+	if err != nil {
698	+		return "", err
699	+	}
700	+	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
701	+}

722	+func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
723	+
724	+// reconstructSampleData returns a SessionData exercising user text, agent text,
725	+// thinking, and a tool call (all of which flatten to user/agent text turns).
726	+func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
727	+	fm := "Wrote `hello.c`:\n\n```c\n#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void){ printf(\"Hello, World!\\n\"); }\n```"
728	+	return &schema.SessionData{
729	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
730	+		Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "claude-code", Name: "Claude Code", Version: "x"},
731	+		SessionID:     "orig-claude-123",
732	+		CreatedAt:     "2026-06-17T10:00:00.000Z",
733	+		WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
734	+		Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
735	+			{
736	+				ExchangeID: "orig-claude-123:0",
737	+				Messages: []schema.Message{
738	+					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a Hello World in C."}}},
739	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "claude-x", Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
740	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeThinking, Text: "Need a main function."}}},
741	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
742	+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
743	+				},
744	+			},
745	+		},
746	+	}
747	+}
748	+
749	+func parseClaudeJSONL(t *testing.T, content []byte) []JSONLRecord {
750	+	t.Helper()
751	+	var records []JSONLRecord
752	+	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(content))
753	+	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
754	+	for scanner.Scan() {
755	+		line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
756	+		if line == "" {
757	+			continue
758	+		}
759	+		var m map[string]interface{}
760	+		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &m); err != nil {
761	+			t.Fatalf("invalid JSONL line %q: %v", line, err)
762	+		}
763	+		records = append(records, JSONLRecord{Data: m})
764	+	}
765	+	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
766	+		t.Fatalf("scanning content: %v", err)
767	+	}
768	+	return records
769	+}
770	+
771	+// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs a session to native Claude JSONL,
772	+// re-parses it through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is
773	+// preserved.
774	+func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
775	+	data := reconstructSampleData()
776	+	expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
777	+
778	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
779	+	if err != nil {
780	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
781	+	}
782	+	if out.SessionID == "" {
783	+		t.Fatal("expected a fresh session ID")
784	+	}
785	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(out.Filename, ".jsonl") {
786	+		t.Errorf("filename %q should end in .jsonl", out.Filename)
787	+	}
788	+
789	+	records := parseClaudeJSONL(t, out.Content)
790	+	regenerated, err := GenerateAgentSession(Session{SessionUuid: out.SessionID, Records: records}, "/tmp/proj")
791	+	if err != nil {
792	+		t.Fatalf("GenerateAgentSession: %v", err)
793	+	}
794	+
795	+	actual := spi.FlattenSessionData(regenerated, "")
796	+	if len(actual) != len(expected) {
797	+		t.Fatalf("round-trip produced %d turns, want %d\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", len(actual), len(expected), actual, expected)
798	+	}
799	+	for i := range expected {
800	+		if actual[i] != expected[i] {
801	+			t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
802	+		}
803	+	}
804	+}
805	+
806	+// TestReconstructSession_Chain verifies the records form a valid linear parentUuid
807	+// chain rooted at null, all sharing the fresh session ID.
808	+func TestReconstructSession_Chain(t *testing.T) {
809	+	out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(reconstructSampleData(), spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
810	+	if err != nil {
811	+		t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
812	+	}
813	+
814	+	records := parseClaudeJSONL(t, out.Content)
815	+	if len(records) == 0 {
816	+		t.Fatal("no records produced")
817	+	}
818	+
819	+	var prev string
820	+	for i, r := range records {
821	+		if sid, _ := r.Data["sessionId"].(string); sid != out.SessionID {
822	+			t.Errorf("record %d sessionId = %q, want %q", i, sid, out.SessionID)
823	+		}
824	+		parent := r.Data["parentUuid"]
825	+		if i == 0 {
826	+			if parent != nil {
827	+				t.Errorf("first record parentUuid = %v, want nil", parent)
828	+			}
829	+			if src, _ := r.Data["specstorySourceSessionId"].(string); src != "orig-claude-123" {
830	+				t.Errorf("first record provenance = %q, want orig-claude-123", src)
831	+			}
832	+		} else if ps, _ := parent.(string); ps != prev {
833	+			t.Errorf("record %d parentUuid = %v, want %q", i, parent, prev)
834	+		}
835	+		prev, _ = r.Data["uuid"].(string)
836	+	}
837	+}
```
