839	+func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
840	+	path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath("/tmp/some-claude-proj", "abc.jsonl")
841	+	if err != nil {
842	+		t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
843	+	}
844	+	if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".claude", "projects")) {
845	+		t.Errorf("path %q should be under .claude/projects", path)
846	+	}
847	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(path, "abc.jsonl") {
848	+		t.Errorf("path %q should end with the filename", path)
849	+	}
850	+	// The project segment encodes the cwd (non-alphanumerics become dashes).
851	+	if !strings.Contains(path, "-tmp-some-claude-proj") {
852	+		t.Errorf("path %q should contain the encoded project dir", path)
853	+	}

1	package claudecode
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/google/uuid"
11	
12		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
14	)
15	
16	const (
17		// reconstructedClaudeModel is a placeholder model label for reconstructed
18		// assistant records. It is only a historical display value — on resume Claude
19		// Code uses its own configured model for the next turn.
20		reconstructedClaudeModel = "claude-opus-4-8"
21	
22		// reconstructedClaudeVersion mirrors the `version` field Claude Code writes on
23		// its records. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the
24		// chance the loader accepts a synthesized file.
25		reconstructedClaudeVersion = "2.1.178"
26	)
27	
28	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Claude Code native JSONL session from the neutral
29	// SessionData so `claude --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
30	//
31	// The forward parser collapses Claude's parentUuid DAG and drops the API
32	// message.id and thinking signatures, so reconstruction synthesizes a fresh,
33	// linear parentUuid chain of plain user/assistant text records (tool calls and
34	// thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData). This is
35	// the structure validated by the Stage 1 spike. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
36	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
37		turns, err := spi.PrepareTurns(data, opts)
38		if err != nil {
39			return nil, err
40		}
41		cwd := spi.ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts, data)
42	
43		newID := uuid.NewString()
44		base := time.Now().UTC()
45	
46		var buf bytes.Buffer
47		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
48		// Preserve <, >, & in conversation/markdown text rather than \u-escaping them.
49		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
50	
51		var prevUUID string
52		for i, turn := range turns {
53			recUUID := uuid.NewString()
54			ts := base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second).Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
55	
56			rec := map[string]interface{}{
57				"isSidechain": false,
58				"userType":    "external",
59				"cwd":         cwd,
60				"sessionId":   newID,
61				"version":     reconstructedClaudeVersion,
62				"gitBranch":   "",
63				"uuid":        recUUID,
64				"timestamp":   ts,
65			}
66	
67			// Linear chain: the first record roots the DAG (parentUuid null) and
68			// carries provenance back to the source session.
69			if prevUUID == "" {
70				rec["parentUuid"] = nil
71				rec["specstorySourceSessionId"] = data.SessionID
72			} else {
73				rec["parentUuid"] = prevUUID
74			}
75	
76			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
77				rec["type"] = "user"
78				rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
79					"role":    "user",
80					"content": turn.Text,
81				}
82			} else {
83				rec["type"] = "assistant"
84				rec["message"] = map[string]interface{}{
85					"id":          "msg_" + recUUID,
86					"type":        "message",
87					"role":        "assistant",
88					"model":       reconstructedClaudeModel,
89					"content":     []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
90					"stop_reason": "end_turn",
91					"usage":       map[string]interface{}{"input_tokens": 1, "output_tokens": 1},
92				}
93			}
94	
95			if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
96				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode reconstructed record: %w", err)
97			}
98	
99			prevUUID = recUUID
100		}
101	
102		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
103			SessionID: newID,
104			Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
105			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
106		}, nil
107	}
108	
109	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session file belongs in Claude
110	// Code's store: ~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not
111	// required to exist (the caller creates it).
112	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
113		dir, err := resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath)
114		if err != nil {
115			return "", err
116		}
117		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
118	}
119

```text
1	package claudecode
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"log/slog"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"regexp"
9		"strings"
10	)
11	
12	// projectDirNameRegex matches any character that is not alphanumeric or a dash.
13	// Claude Code replaces each such character with a dash to map a working directory
14	// to a project folder name under ~/.claude/projects.
15	var projectDirNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
16	
17	// encodeProjectDirName converts a real (symlink-resolved) path into Claude Code's
18	// project directory name: non-alphanumeric/dash characters become dashes, with a
19	// guaranteed leading dash. Example: "/Users/sean/app" -> "-Users-sean-app".
20	func encodeProjectDirName(realPath string) string {
21		name := projectDirNameRegex.ReplaceAllString(realPath, "-")
22		if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") {
23			name = "-" + name
24		}
25		return name
26	}
27	
28	// resolveClaudeProjectDir returns ~/.claude/projects/<encoded> for the given
29	// project path, resolving symlinks, WITHOUT requiring the directory to exist.
30	// Used when writing a reconstructed session into the store (the caller creates
31	// the directory). Distinct from GetClaudeCodeProjectDir, which requires the
32	// projects directory to already exist.
33	func resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
34		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
35		if err != nil {
36			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
37		}
38	
39		cwd := projectPath
40		if cwd == "" {
41			cwd, err = os.Getwd()
42			if err != nil {
43				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
44			}
45		}
46	
47		// Resolve symlinks to match Claude Code's behavior; fall back to the raw path
48		// if resolution fails (e.g., a path component does not exist yet).
49		realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
50		if err != nil {
51			realPath = cwd
52		}
53	
54		return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects", encodeProjectDirName(realPath)), nil
55	}
56	
57	// GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir returns the path to the Claude Code projects directory
58	func GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir() (string, error) {
59		// Get user's home directory
60		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
61		if err != nil {
62			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
63		}
64	
65		// Construct the path to .claude/projects
66		projectsDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects")
67	
68		// Check if the directory exists
69		if _, err := os.Stat(projectsDir); err != nil {
70			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
71				return "", fmt.Errorf("claude projects directory not found at %s", projectsDir)
72			}
73			return "", fmt.Errorf("error checking Claude projects directory: %v", err)
74		}
75	
76		return projectsDir, nil
77	}
78	
79	// GetClaudeCodeProjectDir returns the Claude Code project directory for the given path.
80	// If no path is provided (empty string), it uses the current working directory.
81	// It resolves any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior, which uses the real path
82	// when creating project directories. Claude Code requires a specific naming convention to map
83	// working directories to project folders: each non-alphanumeric character is replaced with a dash
84	// to create a unique, filesystem-safe identifier
85	func GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
86		// Use provided path or get current working directory
87		var cwd string
88		var err error
89		if projectPath == "" {
90			cwd, err = os.Getwd()
91			if err != nil {
92				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
93			}
94		} else {
95			cwd = projectPath
96		}
97	
98		// Resolve any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior
99		// Claude Code uses the real path when creating project directories, so
100		// if the user's working directory contains symlinks, we need to resolve
101		// them to find the correct project directory in ~/.claude/projects/
102		realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
103		if err != nil {
104			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve symlinks in working directory: %v", err)
105		}
106	
107		// Log if the path was changed by symlink resolution
108		if realPath != cwd {
109			slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Resolved symlinks in working directory",
110				"original", cwd,
111				"resolved", realPath)
112		}
113	
114		// Convert path to project directory format (matching Claude Code's behavior).
115		// Example: "/Users/sean/My Projects(1)/app" becomes "-Users-sean-My-Projects-1--app"
116		projectDirName := encodeProjectDirName(realPath)
117	
118		// Log the transformation for debugging path issues
119		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Transformed working directory to project name",
120			"cwd", cwd,
121			"projectDirName", projectDirName)
122	
123		// Get Claude projects directory
124		projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
125		if err != nil {
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get Claude projects directory: %v", err)
127		}
128	
129		fullProjectPath := filepath.Join(projectsDir, projectDirName)
130		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Constructed full project path",
131			"projectPath", fullProjectPath)
132	
133		return fullProjectPath, nil
134	}
135

410		indexedAt   string
411	
412		mu       sync.Mutex
413		lastSeen map[string]string // session id -> last indexed UpdatedAt, to skip no-op upserts
414	}
415	
416	// NewLiveIndexer opens the index for writing and resolves the project for cwd. It returns nil
417	// (not an error) when the index can't be opened, so the caller keeps running with real-time
418	// indexing simply disabled. The caller owns Close.
419	func NewLiveIndexer(cwd string) *LiveIndexer {
420		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
421		if err != nil {
422			slog.Debug("live index: no db path; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
423			return nil
424		}
425		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
426		if err != nil {
427			slog.Debug("live index: cannot open index; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
428			return nil
429		}
430		projectID, projectName, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
431		if err != nil || projectID == "" {
432			projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, ""
433		}
434		return &LiveIndexer{
435			store:       store,
436			cwd:         cwd,
437			projectID:   projectID,
438			projectName: projectName,
439			indexedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
440			lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
441		}
442	}
443	
444	// Record upserts the live session into the index. agentID is the provider's registry id (e.g.
445	// "claude"). It skips the write when the session's last activity hasn't advanced since the
446	// previous record (the watcher can fire repeatedly without new content). Safe on a nil
447	// receiver, and serialized so concurrent provider watchers don't contend on the single writer.
448	func (li *LiveIndexer) Record(agentID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
449		if li == nil || sess == nil || sess.SessionData == nil {
450			return
451		}
452		data := sess.SessionData
453	
454		updatedAt := sess.CreatedAt
455		if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
456			updatedAt = ts
457		}
458	
459		li.mu.Lock()
460		defer li.mu.Unlock()
461	
462		// Nothing advanced since the last write → skip the (FTS-heavy) upsert.
463		if updatedAt != "" && li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] == updatedAt {
464			return
465		}
466	
467		// Skip the whole-table FTS delete (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED, so it scans the entire
468		// FTS) when this session has no existing row to replace. Only the first write of a session
469		// in this process can be new, and even then only if it isn't already in the index from a
470		// prior run — so we check by primary key just once, before lastSeen records the write.
471		isNew := false
472		if _, written := li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID]; !written {
473			if exists, err := li.store.Exists(agentID, sess.SessionID); err == nil {
474				isNew = !exists
475			}
476		}
477	
478		userTurns, totalTurns := countTurns(data)
479		row := sessionindex.Session{
480			ProjectID:    li.projectID,
481			ProjectName:  li.projectName,
482			Agent:        agentID,
483			SessionID:    sess.SessionID,
484			CreatedAt:    sess.CreatedAt,
485			UpdatedAt:    updatedAt,
486			UserTurns:    userTurns,
487			TotalTurns:   totalTurns,
488			Slug:         sess.Slug,
489			OriginCwd:    li.cwd,
490			IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
491			IndexedAt:    li.indexedAt,
492			Body:         flattenBody(data),
493			IsNew:        isNew,
494		}
495		if err := li.store.Upsert(row); err != nil {
496			slog.Debug("live index: upsert failed", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", err)
497			return
498		}
499		li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] = updatedAt
500	}
501	
502	// Close releases the writer handle. Safe on a nil receiver.
503	func (li *LiveIndexer) Close() {
504		if li == nil {
505			return
506		}
507		if err := li.store.Close(); err != nil {
508			slog.Debug("live index: close failed", "error", err)
509		}
510	}
511	
512	// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
513	// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
514	// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
515	// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
516	// dropped.
517	func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) sessionindex.Session {
518		ref := item.ref
519		projectID, projectName := cache.resolve(ref.OriginCwd)

```text
1	package spi
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
8	)
9	
10	// ErrReconstructionUnsupported is returned by a provider that carries the
11	// ReconstructSession responsibility but does not yet have a native serializer.
12	// Reconstruction is on the Provider interface so every provider implements the
13	// method, but only some providers (initially Claude Code and Codex CLI) produce
14	// real native output.
15	var ErrReconstructionUnsupported = errors.New("session reconstruction not supported by this provider")
16	
17	// ReconstructOptions controls how a session is reconstructed into native form.
18	type ReconstructOptions struct {
19		// WorkspaceRoot is the target working directory the reconstructed session
20		// should belong to. Native paths/IDs are derived from it. When empty, the
21		// provider falls back to the SessionData's own WorkspaceRoot.
22		WorkspaceRoot string
23	
24		// MigrationNote, when set, is prepended as a leading agent turn so the agent
25		// understands the prior turns were imported from another session. Optional.
26		MigrationNote string
27	}
28	
29	// ReconstructedSession is the native output of reconstruction. It is data only;
30	// the caller decides where (and whether) to write it.
31	type ReconstructedSession struct {
32		SessionID string // freshly minted, native-format session ID
33		Filename  string // suggested native filename (base name, not a full path)
34		Content   []byte // native session file bytes
35	}
36	
37	// Turn is a single role-tagged text turn in the flattened transcript. The
38	// reconstruction model collapses everything an agent said, thought, or did into
39	// plain user/agent text turns (see docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md), which each
40	// provider then serializes into its native format.
41	type Turn struct {
42		Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
43		Text string
44	}
45	
46	// FlattenSessionData reduces a SessionData into an ordered list of plain
47	// user/agent text turns. This is the single place the cross-provider flattening
48	// policy lives, so every provider's serializer consumes the same transcript.
49	//
50	// Rules (per the locked design):
51	//   - user/agent text content -> a turn of that role
52	//   - thinking content -> folded into the agent turn's text (not dropped)
53	//   - tool calls -> folded into the agent turn's text via the pre-rendered
54	//     Summary / FormattedMarkdown captured on the forward pass
55	//   - model, usage, and path hints -> dropped
56	//
57	// migrationNote, when non-empty, becomes a leading agent turn.
58	func FlattenSessionData(data *schema.SessionData, migrationNote string) []Turn {
59		var turns []Turn
60	
61		if strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote) != "" {
62			turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Text: strings.TrimSpace(migrationNote)})
63		}
64	
65		if data == nil {
66			return turns
67		}
68	
69		for i := range data.Exchanges {
70			for j := range data.Exchanges[i].Messages {
71				msg := &data.Exchanges[i].Messages[j]
72	
73				// Only user and agent roles produce turns; anything else is skipped.
74				if msg.Role != schema.RoleUser && msg.Role != schema.RoleAgent {
75					continue
76				}
77	
78				text := messageTurnText(msg)
79				if strings.TrimSpace(text) == "" {
80					continue
81				}
82	
83				// Drop synthetic, non-conversational turns (slash-command invocations,
84				// local-command output, etc.) so they are not replayed into the resumed
85				// session. This is intentionally reconstruction-only — archival markdown
86				// stays faithful to the source session.
87				if isSyntheticTurnText(text) {
88					continue
89				}
90	
91				turns = append(turns, Turn{Role: msg.Role, Text: text})
92			}
93		}
94	
95		return turns
96	}
97	
98	// PrepareTurns validates session data and flattens it into the neutral turn list
99	// every provider's serializer consumes. It centralizes the two guards each
100	// ReconstructSession must apply before it can build native output: the source
101	// SessionData must be non-nil, and flattening must yield at least one turn.
102	// Keeping these here means every provider rejects the same cases with the same
103	// messages and cannot drift apart.
104	func PrepareTurns(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) ([]Turn, error) {
105		if data == nil {
106			return nil, errors.New("cannot reconstruct nil session data")
107		}
108	
109		turns := FlattenSessionData(data, opts.MigrationNote)
110		if len(turns) == 0 {
111			return nil, errors.New("session has no content to reconstruct")
112		}
113	
114		return turns, nil
115	}
116	
117	// ResolveWorkspaceRoot picks the working directory a reconstructed session
118	// belongs to: the explicit target from opts when set, otherwise the source
119	// session's own recorded root. Providers derive native paths/IDs from the
120	// result; the cursor provider is the lone exception that needs no workspace.
121	func ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts ReconstructOptions, data *schema.SessionData) string {
122		if opts.WorkspaceRoot != "" {
123			return opts.WorkspaceRoot
124		}
125		if data == nil {
126			return ""
127		}
128		return data.WorkspaceRoot
129	}
130	
131	// SyntheticCommandTags are the wrapper tags coding agents inject for
132	// non-conversational slash-command records: the command invocation, its piped
133	// local stdout/stderr, and the local-command caveat. They are conversation
134	// scaffolding, never a real user prompt. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for
135	// the tag set, shared by reconstruction's turn filter here (matched anywhere in a
136	// flattened turn) and Claude Code's title-selection filter
137	// (claudecode.isSyntheticMessage, matched as a leading tag), so the two cannot
138	// drift apart.
139	var SyntheticCommandTags = []string{
140		"<command-name>",
141		"<command-message>",
142		"<command-args>",
143		"<local-command-stdout>",
144		"<local-command-stderr>",
145		"<local-command-caveat>",
146	}
147	
148	// extraSyntheticTurnMarkers are synthetic markers beyond the shared command tags
149	// that must also never be replayed into a resumed session: Claude Code's
150	// <TEXTBLOCK> title-generation wrapper and its interrupt marker (which also has a
151	// "…for tool use" variant, caught by the prefix).
152	var extraSyntheticTurnMarkers = []string{
153		"<TEXTBLOCK>",
154		"[Request interrupted by user",
155	}
156	
157	// isSyntheticTurnText reports whether a flattened turn is a synthetic,
158	// non-conversational artifact that should not be replayed into a resumed session.
159	// It matches by Contains because a flattened turn concatenates content parts, so a
160	// marker can appear anywhere in the text. Markdown generation does NOT use this
161	// filter and stays faithful to source.
162	func isSyntheticTurnText(text string) bool {
163		for _, marker := range SyntheticCommandTags {
164			if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
165				return true
166			}
167		}
168		for _, marker := range extraSyntheticTurnMarkers {
169			if strings.Contains(text, marker) {
170				return true
171			}
172		}
173		return false
174	}
175	
176	// messageTurnText flattens a single message into its turn text: all text/thinking
177	// content parts joined, followed by the tool's pre-rendered markdown when present.
178	func messageTurnText(msg *schema.Message) string {
179		var parts []string
180	
181		for _, part := range msg.Content {
182			// Both "text" and "thinking" parts carry their content in Text; thinking
183			// is intentionally treated as ordinary agent text.
184			if strings.TrimSpace(part.Text) != "" {
185				parts = append(parts, part.Text)
186			}
187		}
188	
189		text := strings.Join(parts, "\n\n")
190	
191		if msg.Tool != nil {
192			toolText := toolTurnText(msg.Tool)
193			if toolText != "" {
194				if text != "" {
195					text += "\n\n" + toolText
196				} else {
197					text = toolText
198				}
199			}
200		}
201	
202		return text
203	}
204	
205	// toolTurnText renders a tool call as agent text, reusing the markdown the
206	// forward pass already produced (Summary + FormattedMarkdown). Falls back to a
207	// minimal description when neither is available.
208	func toolTurnText(tool *schema.ToolInfo) string {
209		var b strings.Builder
210	
211		if tool.Summary != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary) != "" {
212			b.WriteString(strings.TrimSpace(*tool.Summary))
213		}
214	
215		if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && strings.TrimSpace(*tool.FormattedMarkdown) != "" {
216			if b.Len() > 0 {
217				b.WriteString("\n\n")
218			}
219			b.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(*tool.FormattedMarkdown, "\n"))
220		}
221	
222		if b.Len() == 0 {
223			// Nothing pre-rendered; emit a minimal, non-empty description so the tool
224			// activity is at least acknowledged in the transcript.
225			b.WriteString("Tool use: " + tool.Name)
226		}
227	
228		return b.String()
229	}
230
```
</details></tool-use>

package codexcli

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// reconstructedCodexCliVersion mirrors the `cli_version` Codex writes into
// session_meta. A concrete value matching observed sessions maximizes the chance
// the loader accepts a synthesized file.
const reconstructedCodexCliVersion = "0.139.0"

// codexTimestamp formats a time the way Codex records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
func codexTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
	return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
}

// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Codex CLI native rollout from the neutral
// SessionData so `codex resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
//
// Codex resume replays the response_item model transcript, while the forward
// parser reads the event_msg UI stream — so reconstruction emits BOTH for each
// turn: a response_item message (what the agent replays) and a matching event_msg
// (what the UI and our own re-parse read). Tool calls and thinking are already
// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData.
//
// We do NOT synthesize an environment_context block: Codex injects its own fresh
// environment context on every resume, consistent with the cross-provider policy
// that the target supplies its own preamble. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
	turns, err := spi.PrepareTurns(data, opts)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	cwd := spi.ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts, data)

	// Codex session IDs are UUIDv7. Fall back to v4 if v7 generation fails.
	newID := uuid.NewString()
	if v7, err := uuid.NewV7(); err == nil {
		newID = v7.String()
	}

	base := time.Now().UTC()

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
	enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)

	encode := func(rec map[string]interface{}) error {
		return enc.Encode(rec)
	}

	// session_meta must be the first record; it also carries provenance.
	metaTS := codexTimestamp(base)
	if err := encode(map[string]interface{}{
		"timestamp": metaTS,
		"type":      "session_meta",
		"payload": map[string]interface{}{
			"id":                       newID,
			"timestamp":                metaTS,
			"cwd":                      cwd,
			"originator":               "codex-tui",
			"cli_version":              reconstructedCodexCliVersion,
			"source":                   "cli",
			"model_provider":           "openai",
			"specstorySourceSessionId": data.SessionID,
		},
	}); err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_meta: %w", err)
	}

	for i, turn := range turns {
		ts := codexTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i+1) * time.Second))

		var role, contentType, eventType string
		if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
			role, contentType, eventType = "user", "input_text", "user_message"
		} else {
			role, contentType, eventType = "assistant", "output_text", "agent_message"
		}

		// response_item: the model-facing transcript the agent replays on resume.
		if err := encode(codexResponseItemMessage(ts, role, contentType, turn.Text)); err != nil {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode response_item: %w", err)
		}
		// event_msg: the UI/history stream the forward parser reads.
		if err := encode(codexEventMessage(ts, eventType, turn.Text)); err != nil {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode event_msg: %w", err)
		}
	}

	filename := fmt.Sprintf("rollout-%s-%s.jsonl", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04-05"), newID)

	return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
		SessionID: newID,
		Filename:  filename,
		Content:   buf.Bytes(),
	}, nil
}

// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed rollout belongs in Codex's
// store: ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/<filename>, dated today. Codex sessions are
// not project-scoped by directory, so projectPath is unused. The directory is not
// required to exist (the caller creates it).
func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %w", err)
	}
	now := time.Now()
	dir := filepath.Join(
		codexSessionsRoot(homeDir),
		fmt.Sprintf("%04d", now.Year()),
		fmt.Sprintf("%02d", int(now.Month())),
		fmt.Sprintf("%02d", now.Day()),
	)
	return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
}

// codexResponseItemMessage builds a response_item "message" record.
func codexResponseItemMessage(ts, role, contentType, text string) map[string]interface{} {
	return map[string]interface{}{
		"timestamp": ts,
		"type":      "response_item",
		"payload": map[string]interface{}{
			"type":    "message",
			"role":    role,
			"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": contentType, "text": text}},
		},
	}
}

// codexEventMessage builds an event_msg record (user_message / agent_message).
func codexEventMessage(ts, eventType, message string) map[string]interface{} {
	return map[string]interface{}{
		"timestamp": ts,
		"type":      "event_msg",
		"payload": map[string]interface{}{
			"type":    eventType,
			"message": message,
		},
	}
}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go`

```text
1	package cursorcli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"crypto/sha256"
6		"database/sql"
7		"encoding/binary"
8		"encoding/hex"
9		"encoding/json"
10		"fmt"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/google/uuid"
16	
17		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
18		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
19	)
20	
21	// Cursor stores a session as a SQLite store.db: a content-addressed Merkle-DAG of
22	// blobs where each blob's id == sha256(data). Conversation messages are PLAIN JSON
23	// blobs; small protobuf "index" nodes thread them into the DAG; `meta['0']` carries
24	// the head pointer (`latestRootBlobId`). Tool calls and thinking are already
25	// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
26	// user/assistant text-message blobs. The structure below (messages + root + a
27	// field-2 metadata subtree of title/empty/preview nodes, with NO system message)
28	// was validated against `cursor-agent --resume`. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
29	
30	// blobID returns the content-addressed id Cursor uses: sha256(data), hex-encoded.
31	func blobID(data []byte) string {
32		sum := sha256.Sum256(data)
33		return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
34	}
35	
36	// pbField encodes a protobuf length-delimited field (wire type 2). fieldNum must be < 16.
37	func pbField(fieldNum int, payload []byte) []byte {
38		out := []byte{byte(fieldNum<<3 | 2)}
39		var lenBuf [binary.MaxVarintLen64]byte
40		n := binary.PutUvarint(lenBuf[:], uint64(len(payload)))
41		out = append(out, lenBuf[:n]...)
42		return append(out, payload...)
43	}
44	
45	// pbRef encodes a length-delimited field carrying a 32-byte blob id reference.
46	func pbRef(fieldNum int, idHex string) []byte {
47		raw, _ := hex.DecodeString(idHex)
48		return pbField(fieldNum, raw)
49	}
50	
51	// cursorTextPart / message structs marshal to Cursor's plain-JSON message shape.
52	type cursorTextPart struct {
53		Type string `json:"type"`
54		Text string `json:"text"`
55	}
56	type cursorUserMsg struct {
57		Role    string           `json:"role"`
58		Content []cursorTextPart `json:"content"`
59	}
60	type cursorAsstMsg struct {
61		ID      string           `json:"id"`
62		Role    string           `json:"role"`
63		Content []cursorTextPart `json:"content"`
64	}
65	
66	// marshalCompact JSON-encodes v without HTML escaping and without a trailing newline.
67	func marshalCompact(v interface{}) ([]byte, error) {
68		var buf bytes.Buffer
69		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
70		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
71		if err := enc.Encode(v); err != nil {
72			return nil, err
73		}
74		return bytes.TrimRight(buf.Bytes(), "\n"), nil
75	}
76	
77	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Cursor CLI native store.db from the neutral SessionData.
78	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
79		turns, err := spi.PrepareTurns(data, opts)
80		if err != nil {
81			return nil, err
82		}
83	
84		newID := uuid.NewString()
85		name := data.Slug
86		if name == "" {
87			name = "Resumed session"
88		}
89	
90		// blobs accumulates every blob (id -> data); helper interns by content address.
91		blobs := map[string][]byte{}
92		put := func(b []byte) string { id := blobID(b); blobs[id] = b; return id }
93	
94		// 1. Message blobs (plain JSON), in order. No system message.
95		var msgIDs []string
96		var lastAssistantText string
97		asstSeq := 0
98		for _, turn := range turns {
99			var raw []byte
100			var err error
101			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
102				raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorUserMsg{Role: "user", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
103			} else {
104				asstSeq++
105				raw, err = marshalCompact(cursorAsstMsg{ID: fmt.Sprintf("%d", asstSeq), Role: "assistant", Content: []cursorTextPart{{Type: "text", Text: turn.Text}}})
106				lastAssistantText = turn.Text
107			}
108			if err != nil {
109				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
110			}
111			msgIDs = append(msgIDs, put(raw))
112		}
113	
114		// 2. Field-2 metadata subtree: title leaf, empty placeholder, preview node.
115		titleID := put(append(pbField(1, []byte(name)), pbField(2, []byte(uuid.NewString()))...))
116		emptyID := put(pbField(3, nil))                                     // {f3:""}
117		previewID := put(pbField(1, pbField(1, []byte(lastAssistantText)))) // {f1:{f1:text}}
118		metaNodeID := put(concat(pbRef(1, titleID), pbRef(2, emptyID), pbRef(2, previewID)))
119	
120		// 3. Root node: field 1 = ordered message refs, field 2 = metadata node.
121		var root []byte
122		for _, mid := range msgIDs {
123			root = append(root, pbRef(1, mid)...)
124		}
125		root = append(root, pbRef(2, metaNodeID)...)
126		rootID := put(root)
127	
128		// 4. meta['0'] head record (hex-encoded JSON).
129		metaJSON, err := marshalCompact(map[string]interface{}{
130			"agentId":          newID,
131			"latestRootBlobId": rootID,
132			"name":             name,
133			"mode":             "default",
134			"createdAt":        time.Now().UTC().UnixMilli(),
135		})
136		if err != nil {
137			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode meta: %w", err)
138		}
139	
140		// 5. Materialize the SQLite store.db and return its bytes. SQLite needs a file
141		// handle, so we build in a temp file (created and removed within this call).
142		content, err := buildStoreDB(blobs, hex.EncodeToString(metaJSON))
143		if err != nil {
144			return nil, err
145		}
146	
147		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
148			SessionID: newID,
149			Filename:  filepath.Join(newID, "store.db"),
150			Content:   content,
151		}, nil
152	}
153	
154	// concat joins byte slices.
155	func concat(parts ...[]byte) []byte {
156		var out []byte
157		for _, p := range parts {
158			out = append(out, p...)
159		}
160		return out
161	}
162	
163	// buildStoreDB writes the blobs + meta into a SQLite database and returns its bytes.
164	func buildStoreDB(blobs map[string][]byte, metaHex string) ([]byte, error) {
165		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp("", "specstory-cursor-*.db")
166		if err != nil {
167			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create temp db: %w", err)
168		}
169		tmpPath := tmp.Name()
170		_ = tmp.Close()
171		defer func() { _ = os.Remove(tmpPath) }()
172	
173		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", tmpPath)
174		if err != nil {
175			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open temp db: %w", err)
176		}
177		if _, err := db.Exec("CREATE TABLE blobs (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, data BLOB)"); err != nil {
178			_ = db.Close()
179			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create blobs table: %w", err)
180		}
181		if _, err := db.Exec("CREATE TABLE meta (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT)"); err != nil {
182			_ = db.Close()
183			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create meta table: %w", err)
184		}
185		for id, data := range blobs {
186			if _, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO blobs (id, data) VALUES (?, ?)", id, data); err != nil {
187				_ = db.Close()
188				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to insert blob: %w", err)
189			}
190		}
191		if _, err := db.Exec("INSERT INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('0', ?)", metaHex); err != nil {
192			_ = db.Close()
193			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to insert meta: %w", err)
194		}
195		if err := db.Close(); err != nil {
196			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to close temp db: %w", err)
197		}
198	
199		return os.ReadFile(tmpPath)
200	}
201	
202	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed store.db belongs in Cursor's store:
203	// ~/.cursor/chats/<md5(canonical cwd)>/<session-id>/store.db. The filename already
204	// includes the session-id subdir, so this joins it under the project hash dir.
205	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
206		if projectPath == "" {
207			var err error
208			projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
209			if err != nil {
210				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
211			}
212		}
213		hashDir, err := GetProjectHashDir(projectPath)
214		if err != nil {
215			return "", err
216		}
217		return filepath.Join(hashDir, filename), nil
218	}
219
```

1	package deepseektui
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"

11		"github.com/google/uuid"

13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
15	)
16	
17	// deepseekTimestamp formats a time the way DeepSeek records do (RFC3339 micros, Z).
18	func deepseekTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
19		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000Z")
20	}
21	
22	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a DeepSeek TUI native session from the neutral
23	// SessionData so `deepseek --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
24	//
25	// DeepSeek stores a session as a single JSON document
26	// `{schema_version, system_prompt, metadata, messages}` where each message is
27	// `{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}`. Tool calls and thinking are already
28	// flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData, so reconstruction emits only
29	// `user`/`assistant` text messages. The session is associated with a project via
30	// metadata.workspace. See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
31	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
32		turns, err := spi.PrepareTurns(data, opts)
33		if err != nil {
34			return nil, err
35		}
36		cwd := spi.ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts, data)
37	
38		newID := uuid.NewString()
39		now := deepseekTimestamp(time.Now().UTC())
40	
41		title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
42		if title == "" {
43			title = "Resumed session"
44		}
45	
46		messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
47		for _, turn := range turns {
48			role := "assistant"
49			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
50				role = "user"
51			}
52			messages = append(messages, map[string]interface{}{
53				"role":    role,
54				"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
55			})
56		}
57	
58		session := map[string]interface{}{
59			"schema_version": 1,
60			"system_prompt":  "",
61			"metadata": map[string]interface{}{
62				"id":            newID,
63				"title":         title,
64				"created_at":    now,
65				"updated_at":    now,
66				"message_count": len(messages),
67				"total_tokens":  0,
68				"model":         "",
69				"workspace":     cwd,
70				"mode":          "agent",
71			},
72			"messages": messages,
73		}
74	
75		var buf bytes.Buffer
76		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
77		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
78		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
79		if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
80			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode deepseek session: %w", err)
81		}
82	
83		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
84			SessionID: newID,
85			Filename:  newID + ".json",
86			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
87		}, nil
88	}
89	
90	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in DeepSeek's
91	// store: ~/.deepseek/sessions/<filename>. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped
92	// by directory (the project is recorded in metadata.workspace), so projectPath is
93	// unused. The directory is not required to exist (the caller creates it).
94	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
95		dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
96		if err != nil {
97			return "", err
98		}
99		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
100	}
101
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package droidcli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strings"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/google/uuid"
13	
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
15		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
16	)
17	
18	// droidTimestamp formats a time the way Factory Droid records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
19	func droidTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
20		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
21	}
22	
23	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Factory Droid native JSONL session from the neutral
24	// SessionData so `droid --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
25	//
26	// Droid stores a session as a `session_start` header followed by `parentId`-chained
27	// `message` records, each `{message:{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}}`. Tool
28	// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
29	// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`assistant` text messages. The store is
30	// project-scoped (see NativeSessionPath). See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
31	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
32		turns, err := spi.PrepareTurns(data, opts)
33		if err != nil {
34			return nil, err
35		}
36		cwd := spi.ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts, data)
37	
38		newID := uuid.NewString()
39		base := time.Now().UTC()
40	
41		title := strings.TrimSpace(data.Slug)
42		if title == "" {
43			title = "Resumed session"
44		}
45	
46		var buf bytes.Buffer
47		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
48		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
49	
50		// session_start header (also carries provenance).
51		if err := enc.Encode(map[string]interface{}{
52			"type":                      "session_start",
53			"id":                        newID,
54			"title":                     title,
55			"sessionTitle":              title,
56			"owner":                     os.Getenv("USER"),
57			"version":                   2,
58			"cwd":                       cwd,
59			"isSessionTitleManuallySet": false,
60			"sessionTitleAutoStage":     "first_message",
61			"specstorySourceSessionId":  data.SessionID,
62		}); err != nil {
63			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode session_start: %w", err)
64		}
65	
66		var prevID string
67		for i, turn := range turns {
68			msgID := uuid.NewString()
69			role := "assistant"
70			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
71				role = "user"
72			}
73	
74			rec := map[string]interface{}{
75				"type":      "message",
76				"id":        msgID,
77				"timestamp": droidTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
78				"message": map[string]interface{}{
79					"role":    role,
80					"content": []map[string]interface{}{{"type": "text", "text": turn.Text}},
81				},
82			}
83			// Linear chain: the first message roots the thread (no parentId).
84			if prevID != "" {
85				rec["parentId"] = prevID
86			}
87	
88			if err := enc.Encode(rec); err != nil {
89				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode message: %w", err)
90			}
91			prevID = msgID
92		}
93	
94		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
95			SessionID: newID,
96			Filename:  newID + ".jsonl",
97			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
98		}, nil
99	}
100	
101	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed session belongs in Droid's store:
102	// ~/.factory/sessions/<cwd-encoded>/<filename>. The directory is not required to
103	// exist (the caller creates it).
104	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
105		if projectPath == "" {
106			var err error
107			projectPath, err = os.Getwd()
108			if err != nil {
109				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
110			}
111		}
112	
113		dir, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
114		if err != nil {
115			return "", err
116		}
117		if dir == "" {
118			return "", fmt.Errorf("droidcli: cannot resolve session directory for project path")
119		}
120		return filepath.Join(dir, filename), nil
121	}
122
```

```text
1	package geminicli
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/google/uuid"
12	
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
15	)
16	
17	// geminiTimestamp formats a time the way Gemini chat records do (RFC3339 millis, Z).
18	func geminiTimestamp(t time.Time) string {
19		return t.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z")
20	}
21	
22	// ReconstructSession rebuilds a Gemini CLI native chat session from the neutral
23	// SessionData so `gemini --resume <id>` can continue the conversation.
24	//
25	// Gemini stores a session as a single JSON document: a top-level object with
26	// `sessionId`/`projectHash`/`startTime`/`lastUpdated`/`kind` and a `messages`
27	// array. Each message is `{id, timestamp, type, content}` where a user message's
28	// content is `[{text}]` and a gemini message's content is a plain string. Tool
29	// calls and thinking are already flattened into agent text by FlattenSessionData,
30	// so reconstruction emits only `user`/`gemini` text messages. See
31	// docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md.
32	func (p *Provider) ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts spi.ReconstructOptions) (*spi.ReconstructedSession, error) {
33		turns, err := spi.PrepareTurns(data, opts)
34		if err != nil {
35			return nil, err
36		}
37		cwd := spi.ResolveWorkspaceRoot(opts, data)
38	
39		newID := uuid.NewString()
40		base := time.Now().UTC()
41	
42		// Best-effort project hash; Gemini stores it but resume keys off the project dir.
43		projectHash, _ := HashProjectPath(cwd)
44	
45		messages := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(turns))
46		for i, turn := range turns {
47			msg := map[string]interface{}{
48				"id":        uuid.NewString(),
49				"timestamp": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Second)),
50			}
51			if turn.Role == schema.RoleUser {
52				msg["type"] = "user"
53				msg["content"] = []map[string]interface{}{{"text": turn.Text}}
54			} else {
55				msg["type"] = "gemini"
56				msg["content"] = turn.Text
57			}
58			messages = append(messages, msg)
59		}
60	
61		session := map[string]interface{}{
62			"sessionId":   newID,
63			"projectHash": projectHash,
64			"startTime":   geminiTimestamp(base),
65			"lastUpdated": geminiTimestamp(base.Add(time.Duration(len(turns)) * time.Second)),
66			"kind":        "main",
67			"messages":    messages,
68		}
69	
70		var buf bytes.Buffer
71		enc := json.NewEncoder(&buf)
72		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
73		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
74		if err := enc.Encode(session); err != nil {
75			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to encode gemini session: %w", err)
76		}
77	
78		// Filename: session-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM>-<first 8 of id>.json (Gemini convention).
79		filename := fmt.Sprintf("session-%s-%s.json", base.Format("2006-01-02T15-04"), newID[:8])
80	
81		return &spi.ReconstructedSession{
82			SessionID: newID,
83			Filename:  filename,
84			Content:   buf.Bytes(),
85		}, nil
86	}
87	
88	// NativeSessionPath returns where a reconstructed chat belongs in Gemini's store
89	// and, unlike the Claude/Codex resolvers, prepares the project directory: Gemini
90	// associates a tmp dir with a project via a `.project_root` marker, so for a
91	// project Gemini has never seen we create `~/.gemini/tmp/<name>/` with that marker
92	// and a `chats/` subdir. Returns `<projectDir>/chats/<filename>`.
93	func (p *Provider) NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error) {
94		if projectPath == "" {
95			var err error
96			projectPath, err = osGetwd()
97			if err != nil {
98				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %w", err)
99			}
100		}
101	
102		// Reuse the project dir Gemini already associates with this project, if any.
103		dir, err := ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
104		if err != nil {
105			dir, err = prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
106			if err != nil {
107				return "", err
108			}
109		}
110	
111		return filepath.Join(dir, "chats", filename), nil
112	}
113	
114	// prepareGeminiProjectDir creates a Gemini project directory for a project that
115	// Gemini has not seen yet: ~/.gemini/tmp/<basename>/ (falling back to the hash dir
116	// if that basename is taken by a different project), with a `.project_root` marker
117	// and a `chats/` subdirectory.
118	func prepareGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
119		tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
120		if err != nil {
121			return "", err
122		}
123		if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDir, 0o755); err != nil {
124			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini tmp dir: %w", err)
125		}
126	
127		canonical := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
128		dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, filepath.Base(projectPath))
129	
130		// If the basename dir exists but belongs to a different project, key off the hash instead.
131		if info, statErr := osStat(dir); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() && !matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonical) {
132			if dir, err = GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath); err != nil {
133				return "", err
134			}
135		}
136	
137		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(dir, "chats"), 0o755); err != nil {
138			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create gemini chats dir: %w", err)
139		}
140	
141		// Write the project-root marker so Gemini maps this dir to the project on resume.
142		rootFile := filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")
143		if _, statErr := osStat(rootFile); os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
144			if err := os.WriteFile(rootFile, []byte(canonical), 0o644); err != nil {
145				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write gemini .project_root: %w", err)
146			}
147		}
148	
149		return dir, nil
150	}
151
```

 `./docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md`

```text
1	# Session Portability (Reverse Data Flow)
2	
3	This document describes the design for **session portability**: reconstructing an agent's native session format *from* the SpecStory CLI's neutral session schema, so a conversation captured from one agent can be resumed in another — and, eventually, on another machine, and by another user.
4	
5	Our sequencing of this is:
6	
7	- 1. cross-provider resume (local only plumbing)
8	- 2. cross-project resume (local only plumbing)
9	- 3. cross-machine resume (SpecStory Cloud plumbing)
10	- 4. cross-user resume (Requires SpecStory Cloud Orgs/Teams)
11	
12	## Overview
13	
14	Today the CLI's data flow is one-directional:
15	
16	```
17	agent-native format  ──►  neutral SessionData  ──►  markdown / cloud
18	   (per provider)            (schema.SessionData)       (rendered output)
19	```
20	
21	Each provider parses its agent's native session store into the unified `schema.SessionData`, which is then rendered to markdown and synced to the cloud.
22	
23	Session portability **reverses** the second arrow, adding a new provider responsibility:
24	
25	```
26	neutral SessionData  ──►  agent-native format
27	 (schema.SessionData)        (per provider)
28	```
29	
30	A provider gains the ability to take a `SessionData` and emit a native session file that its agent can load and resume. Combined with a new `specstory resume` experience, this enables a conversation to continue in a *different* agent than the one that created it, on a different machine than the one that created it (via SpecStory cloud), and by a different user than the one that created it (via SpecStory Cloud teams product).
31	
32	## Use Cases
33	
34	Portability widens **one dimension at a time**, following the staged sequencing above. The stages are cumulative: each builds on the plumbing of the prior one and adds a single new axis of "crossing" — provider, then project, then machine, then user. The dimensions also compose (e.g. a future cross-machine resume can also be cross-provider), so the reconstruction core built in stage 1 is exercised by every later stage.
35	
36	### Stage 1 — Cross-provider resume *(local only)*
37	
38	Start a session in one agent and continue it in another, on the same machine and project (e.g. Claude Code → Codex CLI, or vice versa). The neutral `SessionData` is the interchange format; each provider reconstructs its own native format from it. This is the foundation for everything that follows: it establishes the `SessionData → native` reconstruction and the `specstory resume` flow, entirely with local plumbing.
39	
40	### Stage 2 — Cross-project resume *(local only)*
41	
42	Resume a session into a *different project directory* on the same machine. Everything stays local, but both the source lookup and the target write must be re-pointed at the destination workspace: the native store location and (for Claude Code) the cwd-derived project folder are computed for the destination project rather than the origin. No cloud involvement.
43	
44	### Stage 3 — Cross-machine resume *(SpecStory Cloud plumbing)*
45	
46	A session created on machine A is carried via SpecStory Cloud to machine B and resumed there. Machine B has no local native store for the session, so `SessionData` must be sourced from the cloud. This depends on the cloud persisting and serving `SessionData` (see [SessionData Sourcing](#sessiondata-sourcing)) — today the cloud stores only rendered Markdown + native RawData, so this stage is gated on cloud-side work.
47	
48	### Stage 4 — Cross-user resume *(requires SpecStory Cloud Orgs/Teams)*
49	
50	A *different user* resumes a session shared with them through a team or organization. On top of the cross-machine plumbing, this requires the cloud's sharing, identity, and permission model (the Orgs/Teams product) to govern who may resume whose sessions.
51	
52	## Goals and Non-Goals
53	
54	### Goals
55	
56	- A provider can reconstruct a resumable native session file from `SessionData`.
57	- Cross-provider reconstruction works from day one (Claude ↔ Codex), since the tool/model/usage mapping problems are fundamental and must be confronted early.
58	- The reconstructed session is **valid** to the target agent's loader and conveys the **gist** of the prior conversation as context for the agent's next request.
59	
60	### Non-Goals
61	
62	- **Byte-for-byte or structural round-trip.** The agent only needs the prior conversation as context for subsequent model requests; it does not need a faithful replica of the original session's internal structure.
63	- **Faithful tool replay.** Reconstructed sessions do not reproduce native `tool_use` / `function_call` structures (see [Reconstruction Model](#reconstruction-model)).
64	- **Working reconstruction for every provider at once.** Reconstruction is on the `spi.Provider` interface so all providers carry the responsibility, but only Claude Code and Codex CLI have working implementations initially; the rest return `ErrReconstructionUnsupported` until implemented.
65	
66	### Fidelity Bar
67	
68	Between "the agent parses it without error" and "best-effort readable." The reconstructed session must be structurally valid enough that the target agent loads and resumes it, and must carry enough of the conversation that the agent has useful context going forward. Synthesized IDs, timestamps, and chains are acceptable.
69	
70	## Background: Why the Reverse Path Is Not a Mirror
71	
72	The forward parsers read a **different projection** of each native file than the agent needs in order to *resume*. Reconstruction therefore cannot simply "run the parser backwards" — it must synthesize records the forward path never reads.
73	
74	- **Codex CLI.** The forward parser builds `SessionData` from the `event_msg` UI-event stream (`user_message`, `agent_message`, `agent_reasoning`). But `codex resume` replays the `response_item` stream — the model-facing transcript (`{type:"message", role, content:[…]}` plus tool-call items). Both streams coexist in every rollout file; reconstruction must regenerate the `response_item` transcript.
75	
76	- **Claude Code.** The forward parser collapses the `parentUuid` linked-list into linear exchanges and keeps only the record `uuid`. It drops the API `message.id`, the chain, and thinking-block signatures. `claude --resume` needs a valid `parentUuid` chain and API-shaped messages.
77	
78	Combined with cross-provider conversion (a Claude `Edit` has no clean Codex `apply_patch` equivalent; model names and token-usage fields are meaningless to the other agent), faithful structural tool replay is intractable. The pragmatic, robust target is a **flattened transcript** in the target's native shape.
79	
80	## Reconstruction Model
81	
82	`SessionData` is flattened into an ordered list of **plain user/agent text turns**, then serialized into the target's native format. Everything the agent "said," "thought," or "did" collapses into agent text:
83	
84	| `SessionData` element                                | Becomes                                                          |
85	|------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
86	| user message text                                    | user turn                                                        |
87	| agent message text                                   | agent turn                                                       |
88	| thinking content (`ContentTypeThinking`)             | agent turn (text)                                                |
89	| tool call                                            | agent turn (text, via `Tool.Summary` / `Tool.FormattedMarkdown`) |
90	| `model`, `Usage`, `PathHints`                        | dropped                                                          |
91	| synthetic local-command turns (slash-command invocations, their stdout, caveats; e.g. Claude's `<command-name>`/`<local-command-*>`, `<TEXTBLOCK>`) | dropped — **reconstruction-only** noise filtering |
92	| source agent system / `environment_context` preamble | not copied (target injects its own)                              |
93	
94	Dropping the synthetic local-command turns happens during flattening, **not** in the forward parser — so archival `.specstory` markdown stays a faithful record of the source session, while the reconstructed context fed to a resumed agent is kept clean (the caveat text, in particular, is actively misleading to a resumed agent).
95	
96	The tool-call flattening reuses work already done on the forward pass: each `ToolInfo` already carries a pre-rendered markdown rendition (`FormattedMarkdown`, and for Codex also `Summary`). Reconstruction emits that markdown as the text of an agent turn, so reconstructed sessions contain **only user and agent text turns** — no native tool structures, no dangling tool results, no thinking signatures. This is what makes cross-provider reconstruction safe: there is nothing provider-specific or API-fragile left to mistranslate.
97	
98	## Validated Spike
99	
100	The core hypothesis was validated before any converter code was written (project `~/Source/SpecStory/compositions/cross-portable-1`, 2026-06-16). Hand-built cross-provider session files — a Codex session synthesized from a Claude conversation, and a Claude session synthesized from a Codex conversation — were both loaded and continued successfully by `claude --resume` and `codex resume`.
101	
102	The spike files were deliberately **minimal**: only what the converter could produce from `SessionData` plus the target cwd. This confirmed:
103	
104	- A minimal user/agent text transcript is sufficient to resume. The runtime scaffolding the converter cannot produce was omitted and **not** required — Codex `base_instructions` / developer-permissions / `turn_context`, and Claude `file-history-snapshot` / `mode` / attachment records. Each agent injects its own scaffolding on resume.
105	- Tool-calls-as-agent-text are accepted. No native `tool_use` / `function_call` and no `tool_result` pairing are needed.
106	- Fresh native-format session IDs work, with the ID embedded in the filename.
107	
108	## Architecture
109	
110	### Reconstruction on the Provider Interface
111	
112	Reconstruction is a **first-class provider responsibility**: `ReconstructSession` is added to the `spi.Provider` interface, so every provider carries it. Claude Code and Codex CLI get working implementations first (Stage 1); the remaining providers satisfy the interface with a stub that returns a clear "reconstruction not yet supported" error until their implementations land. Consumers call `ReconstructSession` directly — no capability check, no type assertion.
113	
114	```go
115	// pkg/spi/provider.go — added to the Provider interface
116	
117	// ReconstructSession rebuilds the provider's native session format from the
118	// neutral SessionData so the agent can resume the conversation. Providers that
119	// do not yet implement it return ErrReconstructionUnsupported.
120	ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) (*ReconstructedSession, error)
121	```
122	
123	```go
124	// pkg/spi/reconstruct.go — shared types
125	
126	// ReconstructOptions controls how a session is reconstructed.
127	type ReconstructOptions struct {
128	    WorkspaceRoot string // target cwd; native paths/IDs are derived from this
129	    MigrationNote string // optional one-line note prepended as context
130	}
131	
132	// ReconstructedSession is the native output of reconstruction.
133	type ReconstructedSession struct {
134	    SessionID string // freshly minted, native-format ID
135	    Filename  string // suggested native filename (relative)
136	    Content   []byte // native session file bytes
137	}
138	```
139	
140	`ReconstructSession` is a **pure transform** — no filesystem access — which makes it trivially unit-testable and the natural home for round-trip tests. (The `specstory resume` command, not the provider, writes the result into the live store.)
141	
142	### Shared Flattening Helper
143	
144	The `SessionData → []Turn` flattening (the [Reconstruction Model](#reconstruction-model) table) lives in one shared place so the cross-provider policy is defined once and every provider consumes it.
145	
146	```go
147	type Turn struct {
148	    Role string // schema.RoleUser or schema.RoleAgent
149	    Text string
150	}
151	```
152	
153	### Per-Provider Serializers
154	
155	Each provider converts `[]Turn` into its native, resumable file:
156	
157	- **Claude Code** (`pkg/providers/claudecode/reconstruct.go`): a `parentUuid`-chained JSONL (one `user` record followed by `assistant` text records) written to `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<new-uuid>.jsonl`. Fresh UUIDv4.
158	
159	- **Codex CLI** (`pkg/providers/codexcli/reconstruct.go`): a `session_meta` record plus a linear `response_item` message transcript (and matching `event_msg` stream) written to `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-<ts>-<new-id>.jsonl`. Fresh UUIDv7-shape ID.
160	
161	- **Gemini CLI** (`pkg/providers/geminicli/reconstruct.go`): a single chat-JSON document (`{sessionId, projectHash, startTime, lastUpdated, kind:"main", messages:[…]}`) with `user` (`content:[{text}]`) and `gemini` (`content:"…"`) text messages, fresh UUIDv4. Unlike the others, Gemini associates a tmp dir with a project via a `.project_root` marker, so `NativeSessionPath` *prepares* the store (`~/.gemini/tmp/<name>/` + marker + `chats/`) rather than only computing a path.
162	
163	- **Factory Droid** (`pkg/providers/droidcli/reconstruct.go`): a `session_start` header plus `parentId`-chained `message` records (`{message:{role, content:[{type:"text",text}]}}`) written to `~/.factory/sessions/<cwd-encoded>/<new-uuid>.jsonl`. Project-scoped like Claude. Fresh UUIDv4.
164	
165	- **DeepSeek TUI** (`pkg/providers/deepseektui/reconstruct.go`): a single JSON document (`{schema_version, system_prompt, metadata, messages}`) with `user`/`assistant` `content:[{type:"text",text}]` messages, written to `~/.deepseek/sessions/<new-uuid>.json`. Not project-scoped by directory — the project is recorded in `metadata.workspace`. Fresh UUIDv4.
166	
167	- **Cursor CLI** (`pkg/providers/cursorcli/reconstruct.go`): a SQLite `store.db` content-addressed Merkle-DAG. Each turn becomes a plain-JSON message blob (`user`/`assistant`), `id = sha256(data)`; tiny protobuf index nodes thread them — a `root` node (field 1 = ordered message refs, field 2 = a title/empty/preview metadata subtree); `meta['0']` holds the head pointer (`latestRootBlobId`). Written to `~/.cursor/chats/<md5(cwd)>/<session-id>/store.db`. No system message (validated against `cursor-agent --resume`). Fresh UUIDv4.
168	
169	Implemented serializers reuse existing path-derivation code (`GetClaudeCodeProjectDir`, `codexSessionsRoot`, `ResolveGeminiProjectDir`, `resolveProjectSessionDir`, `resolveSessionsDir`, `GetProjectHashDir`).
170	
171	### Dependency Flow
172	
173	```
174	cmd/resume.go
175	  → spi.Provider.ReconstructSession   (every provider implements it)
176	  → existing ExecAgentAndWatch (resume)
177	
178	providers/* → spi (ReconstructSession + shared flattening helper + schema)
179	```
180	
181	## Cross-Provider Mapping Policy
182	
183	Because reconstruction flattens to text, most mapping problems dissolve. The explicit rules:
184	
185	- **Model name** — dropped. The target agent uses its own configured model going forward.
186	- **Token usage** — dropped. Irrelevant to a resumed conversation.
187	- **Thinking / reasoning** — flattened to agent text. (Not dropped, not emitted as a signed thinking block.)
188	- **Tool calls** — flattened to agent text via the pre-rendered markdown.
189	- **System / `environment_context` preamble** — not copied from the source agent, and not synthesized for the target either; the target agent injects its own on resume (e.g. Codex rebuilds its environment context every turn). An optional one-line migration note may be prepended so the agent understands the prior turns were imported.
190	
191	## Session Identity and Provenance
192	
193	Reconstruction always mints a **fresh native-format session ID** (UUIDv4 for Claude, UUIDv7-shape for Codex) — the session did not previously exist in the target store, and the ID must be valid for the target. The original session ID is recorded as provenance metadata so the lineage is traceable.
194	
195	## The `specstory resume` Flow
196	
197	`specstory resume` is interactive. Its Stage 1 scope is **cross-agent within the current project**. It behaves like `specstory run` plus a selection UI and, for cross-agent, a reconstruct-and-store step before launch.
198	
199	It reuses the existing `ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath, customCommand, resumeSessionID, debugRaw, callback)` — **no new exec/resume method is needed**. All six providers already implement native resume (`claude --resume`, `codex resume`, `cursor --resume`, `gemini --resume`, `droid --resume`, `deepseek --resume`), so same-agent resume works for any agent; cross-agent resume requires the target to reconstruct (Claude/Codex today).
200	
201	Interactive steps (plain numbered stdin menus — no TUI dependency for the first cut):
202	
203	1. **Pick the source agent.** For every registered provider, list its sessions in the current project (`ListAgentChatSessions`). Show only agents with ≥1 session, each with name, session count, and date range.
204	2. **Pick the session.** Show the chosen agent's sessions, reverse-chronological, paginated, labeled by date and name/slug.
205	3. **Pick the target agent.** Show installed agents (`Check().Success`), including the source agent itself (labeled "same agent — native resume").
206	4. **Resume:**
207	   - **Same agent (from == to):** skip reconstruction; native-resume the existing session via `ExecAgentAndWatch(..., resumeSessionID = chosen session, ...)`.
208	   - **Cross-agent (from != to):**
209	     1. `from.GetAgentChatSession(...)` → `SessionData`.
210	     2. `to.ReconstructSession(data, opts)` with a default migration note (cross-agent only) → native bytes + fresh ID. If the target returns `ErrReconstructionUnsupported`, fail with a clear message (only Claude/Codex reconstruct today).
211	     3. `to.NativeSessionPath(projectPath, rec.Filename)` → destination path; the command does `MkdirAll` + `WriteFile`.
212	     4. `to.ExecAgentAndWatch(..., resumeSessionID = rec.SessionID, ...)`, with the same autosave callback `run`/`watch` use.
213	
214	Writing into another application's data store is the one sensitive action. It is **scoped to exactly the single session being resumed**, and only happens when the user explicitly invokes `resume`.
215	
216	### Path resolution
217	
218	`ReconstructSession` stays a pure transform (bytes + base filename + fresh ID; no filesystem). A separate `NativeSessionPath(projectPath, filename) (string, error)` method on `spi.Provider` resolves where the file belongs in the target's native store — e.g. `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/<id>.jsonl` or `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-…-<id>.jsonl` — without requiring the directory to exist; the resume command performs the `MkdirAll` + `WriteFile`. Providers without a serializer return `ErrReconstructionUnsupported`.
219	
220	## SessionData Sourcing
221	
222	The reconstruction input is always `SessionData`. Where that `SessionData` comes from is the main thing that gates each stage:
223	
224	| Stage             | Source of `SessionData`                                                               | Status                                                                |
225	|-------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
226	| 1. Cross-provider | Re-parse the local native store (origin project) through the existing forward path    | First build target                                                    |
227	| 2. Cross-project  | Re-parse the local native store (origin project); reconstruct against destination cwd | Local; follows stage 1                                                |
228	| 3. Cross-machine  | Cloud persists and serves `SessionData`                                               | Gated on cloud-side work (cloud stores only Markdown + RawData today) |
229	| 4. Cross-user     | Cloud serves `SessionData` **and** Orgs/Teams sharing + permissions                   | Gated on the cloud Orgs/Teams product                                 |
230	
231	Stages 1 and 2 are entirely local — `SessionData` is rebuilt on demand by re-parsing the native store through the existing forward path. Stages 3 and 4 need the cloud to carry `SessionData` between machines and users.
232	
233	Note that `SessionData` is currently **ephemeral** — built in memory, used to render markdown, then discarded (it is only written to disk under `--debug-raw`). Persisting/serving it is the cloud-side work that stages 3 and 4 depend on.
234	
235	> A pure same-provider, same-project, same-machine "resume" is **not** a product use case — the agent's own native session already exists, so there is nothing to reconstruct. That trivial round-trip is used only as a development and test vehicle for the reconstruction core.
236	
237	## Build Plan (Stage 1: Cross-Provider Resume)
238	
239	These phases deliver **Stage 1** — the reconstruction core, the two native serializers, and the `specstory resume` command, all with local plumbing. The same core is reused by every later stage; stages 2–4 add sourcing and sharing plumbing around it rather than changing it (see [Later Stages](#later-stages)).
240	
241	- **Phase 1 — Resume-acceptance spike. _(Complete.)_** Hand-built cross-provider files resumed successfully in both agents. Validated the minimal flattened-transcript approach.
242	
243	- **Phase 2 — Shared core + interface.** Add `ReconstructSession` to `spi.Provider`; add `pkg/spi/reconstruct.go` (option/result types, `ErrReconstructionUnsupported`, and the shared `SessionData → []Turn` flattening helper); add not-supported stubs to the four providers without serializers so the build compiles. Unit-tested.
244	
245	- **Phase 3 — Native serializers.** `reconstruct.go` in `claudecode` and `codexcli`, each minting a fresh ID and recording provenance. Round-trip tests: reconstruct → re-parse via the forward parser → assert turns match.
246	
247	- **Phase 4 — `specstory resume` command (Stage 1: cross-agent). _(In progress.)_** `pkg/cmd/resume.go` + wiring, mirroring `watch`/`run` setup. Interactive numbered menus (source agent → session → target agent); adds `NativeSessionPath` to the SPI; cross-agent injects a default migration note and reconstructs+writes before launch; same-agent bypasses reconstruction and native-resumes; reuses `ExecAgentAndWatch` for launch + autosave. Errors clearly when the chosen target can't reconstruct.
248	
249	- **Phase 5 — End-to-end validation and edge hardening. _(Done for Claude → Codex.)_** Cross-agent resume confirmed through the command in a fresh project: Codex resumed cleanly **without** a synthesized `environment_context` (it injects its own), the migration note appeared as the leading turn, no synthetic local-command noise leaked, and a follow-up ("what did we just do?") proved the reconstructed transcript fed the model's context. Two fixes landed along the way: synthetic local-command turns are filtered at flatten time, and the synthesized `environment_context` was removed. Edge cases handled with tests: blank/whitespace turns and tool-only sessions flatten correctly; large tool outputs are already truncated upstream; UUID-based filenames avoid collisions; JSON encoding preserves unicode. Remaining: a Codex → Claude confirmation pass.
250	
251	- **Phase 6 — Other providers. _(All six providers done.)_** Gemini CLI, Factory Droid, DeepSeek TUI, and Cursor CLI now have working serializers + `NativeSessionPath` (all structurally tested), so every provider can be a cross-agent resume target. Cursor's SQLite `store.db` format was reverse-engineered (sha256 content-addressed Merkle-DAG; JSON message blobs + protobuf index nodes) and a hand-built spike confirmed `cursor-agent --resume` loads a reconstructed session with full prior context. Real-agent resume confirmed for Codex, Gemini, and Cursor; Droid and DeepSeek await a confirmation pass.
252	
253	- **Phase 7 — Docs and analytics.** README/help for `resume`; resume analytics events; provenance recorded.
254	
255	## Risks and Open Questions
256	
257	- **Loader strictness across agent versions.** The spike validated current Claude Code and Codex CLI versions. Future versions could tighten their loaders. Round-trip tests guard our own parsing; real-agent validation (Phase 5) guards theirs.
258	- **Codex `environment_context` necessity.** Resolved by design: reconstruction no longer synthesizes one — Codex injects a fresh environment context on every resume, so a fabricated block is redundant and inconsistent with the "target supplies its own preamble" policy. Pending one real-agent confirmation that resume works without it.
259	- **Loss of tool fidelity.** By design, the resumed agent cannot inspect exact prior tool inputs/outputs beyond what the flattened text conveys. Accepted under the gist fidelity bar.
260	
261	## Later Stages
262	
263	These follow Stage 1 in the sequencing and are not part of the initial build:
264	
265	- **Stage 2 — Cross-project resume.** Re-point source lookup and target write at a destination workspace; derive the destination project folder / native store path from the new cwd. Local-only; reuses the Stage 1 reconstruction core unchanged.
266	- **Stage 3 — Cross-machine resume.** Requires the cloud to persist and serve `SessionData` (today it stores only Markdown + native RawData), plus a CLI path that sources `SessionData` from the cloud instead of a local re-parse.
267	- **Stage 4 — Cross-user resume.** Requires the SpecStory Cloud Orgs/Teams product — session sharing, identity, and permissions governing who may resume whose sessions.
268	
269	## Deferred
270	
271	- Nothing in Stage 1 — all six providers (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Factory Droid, DeepSeek TUI, Cursor CLI) have working serializers. Remaining work is the later stages (cross-project, cross-machine, cross-user) and real-agent confirmation passes for Droid and DeepSeek.
272	
273	## Related Documents
274	
275	- [PROVIDER-SPI.md](PROVIDER-SPI.md) — the provider interface and registry.
276	- [SPI-SESSION-DATA-SCHEMA.md](SPI-SESSION-DATA-SCHEMA.md) — the neutral `SessionData` schema.
277
```

43	func parseCodexJSONL(t *testing.T, content []byte) []map[string]interface{} {
44		t.Helper()
45		var records []map[string]interface{}
46		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(content))
47		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
48		for scanner.Scan() {
49			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
50			if line == "" {
51				continue
52			}
53			var m map[string]interface{}
54			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &m); err != nil {
55				t.Fatalf("invalid JSONL line %q: %v", line, err)
56			}
57			records = append(records, m)
58		}
59		if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
60			t.Fatalf("scanning content: %v", err)
61		}
62		return records
63	}
64	
65	// TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip reconstructs to native Codex rollout format,
66	// re-parses through the forward path, and asserts the flattened transcript is

68	func TestReconstructSession_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
69		data := reconstructSampleData()
70		expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
71

92		}
93		for i := range expected {
94			if actual[i] != expected[i] {
95				t.Errorf("turn %d mismatch:\n got: %+v\nwant: %+v", i, actual[i], expected[i])
96			}
97		}
98	}
99

103	func TestReconstructSession_Structure(t *testing.T) {
104		out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(reconstructSampleData(), spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
105		if err != nil {

107		}

112		}
113

116			t.Fatalf("first record type = %v, want session_meta", meta["type"])
117		}
118		payload, _ := meta["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
119		if payload["id"] != out.SessionID {
120			t.Errorf("session_meta id = %v, want %q", payload["id"], out.SessionID)
121		}
122		if payload["specstorySourceSessionId"] != "orig-codex-123" {
123			t.Errorf("provenance = %v, want orig-codex-123", payload["specstorySourceSessionId"])
124		}
125	
126		// Exactly one user_message event per user turn; no synthesized environment_context anywhere.
127		userEvents := 0

131			}
132			if r["type"] != "event_msg" {

134			}
135			p, _ := r["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
136			if p["type"] == "user_message" {
