671					}
672					slog.Error("Error getting session from provider", "sessionId", sessionID, "provider", specifiedProvider.Name(), "error", err)
673					errorCount++
674					lastError = err
675					continue
676				}
677				if session == nil {
678					if !printToStdout {
679						fmt.Printf("❌ Session '%s' not found in %s\n", sessionID, specifiedProvider.Name())
680					}
681					slog.Warn("Session not found in provider", "sessionId", sessionID, "provider", specifiedProvider.Name())
682					notFoundCount++
683					continue
684				}
685				foundAgentID = args[0]
686			} else {
687				// Case B: No provider specified - try all providers
688				providerIDs := registry.ListIDs()
689				for _, id := range providerIDs {
690					provider, err := registry.Get(id)
691					if err != nil {
692						continue
693					}
694	
695					session, err = provider.GetAgentChatSession(cwd, sessionID, debugRaw)
696					if err != nil {
697						slog.Debug("Error checking provider for session", "provider", id, "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
698						continue
699					}
700					if session != nil {
701						foundAgentID = id
702						if !silent && !printToStdout {
703							fmt.Printf("✅ Found session '%s' for %s\n", sessionID, provider.Name())
704						}
705						break // Found it, don't check other providers
706					}
707				}
708	
709				if session == nil {
710					if !printToStdout {
711						fmt.Printf("❌ Session '%s' not found in any provider\n", sessionID)
712					}
713					slog.Warn("Session not found in any provider", "sessionId", sessionID)
714					notFoundCount++
715					continue
716				}
717			}
718	
719			// Process the found session
720			if printToStdout {
721				sessionpkg.ValidateSessionData(session, debugRaw)
722				sessionpkg.WriteDebugSessionData(session, debugRaw)
723	
724				markdownContent, err := sessionpkg.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(session.SessionData, false, useUTC)
725				if err != nil {
726					slog.Error("Failed to generate markdown", "sessionId", session.SessionID, "error", err)
727					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownError, analytics.Properties{
728						"session_id": session.SessionID,
729						"error":      err.Error(),
730						"mode":       "print",
731					})
732					errorCount++
733					lastError = err
734					continue
735				}
736	
737				// Separate multiple sessions with a horizontal rule
738				if printedSessions > 0 {
739					fmt.Print("\n---\n\n")
740				}
741				fmt.Print(markdownContent)
742				printedSessions++
743				successCount++
744				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownSuccess, analytics.Properties{
745					"session_id": session.SessionID,
746					"mode":       "print",
747				})
748			} else {
749				// Normal sync: write to file and optionally cloud sync
750				if _, err := sessionpkg.ProcessSingleSession(context.Background(), session, config, sessionpkg.ProcessingOptions{
751					OnlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
752					ShowOutput:         true,
753					DebugRaw:           debugRaw,
754					UseUTC:             useUTC,
755					NoTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
756				}); err != nil {
757					errorCount++
758					lastError = err
759				} else {
760					successCount++
761					// Mirror the synced session into the restore index.
762					liveIndex.Record(foundAgentID, session)
763				}
764			}

3	import (
4		"crypto/sha256"
5		"database/sql"
6		"encoding/hex"

13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"

15	)
16	
17	func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }
18	
19	func reconstructSampleData() *schema.SessionData {
20		fm := "Created `hello.rs`:\n\n```rust\nfn main() { println!(\"Hello, World!\"); }\n```"
21		return &schema.SessionData{
22			SchemaVersion: "1.0",
23			Provider:      schema.ProviderInfo{ID: "cursor", Name: "Cursor CLI", Version: "x"},
24			SessionID:     "orig-cursor-123",
25			CreatedAt:     "2026-06-18T10:00:00.000Z",
26			WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj",
27			Slug:          "hello world in rust",
28			Exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
29				{
30					ExchangeID: "orig-cursor-123:0",
31					Messages: []schema.Message{
32						{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Create a hello world in Rust."}}},
33						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "I'll create it."}}},
34						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "write", Type: schema.ToolTypeWrite, FormattedMarkdown: strptr(fm)}},
35						{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Done."}}},
36					},
37				},
38			},
39		}
40	}
41	
42	// openStoreBytes writes the reconstructed store.db bytes to a temp file and reads
43	// back its blobs + meta head record.
44	func openStoreBytes(t *testing.T, content []byte) (map[string][]byte, map[string]interface{}) {
45		t.Helper()
46		path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "store.db")
47		if err := os.WriteFile(path, content, 0o644); err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("write store: %v", err)
49		}
50		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", path+"?mode=ro")
51		if err != nil {
52			t.Fatalf("open store: %v", err)
53		}
54		defer func() { _ = db.Close() }()
55	
56		blobs := map[string][]byte{}
57		rows, err := db.Query("SELECT id, data FROM blobs")
58		if err != nil {
59			t.Fatalf("query blobs: %v", err)
60		}
61		for rows.Next() {
62			var id string
63			var data []byte
64			if err := rows.Scan(&id, &data); err != nil {
65				t.Fatalf("scan: %v", err)
66			}
67			blobs[id] = data
68		}
69		_ = rows.Close()
70	
71		var metaHex string
72		if err := db.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='0'").Scan(&metaHex); err != nil {
73			t.Fatalf("meta: %v", err)
74		}
75		metaBytes, err := hex.DecodeString(metaHex)
76		if err != nil {
77			t.Fatalf("meta hex: %v", err)
78		}
79		var meta map[string]interface{}
80		if err := json.Unmarshal(metaBytes, &meta); err != nil {
81			t.Fatalf("meta json: %v", err)
82		}
83		return blobs, meta
84	}
85	
86	// refsOf scans a protobuf node for 32-byte blob-id references in the given field.
87	func refsOf(node []byte, fieldNum int) []string {
88		tag := byte(fieldNum<<3 | 2)
89		var refs []string
90		for i := 0; i+34 <= len(node); i++ {
91			if node[i] == tag && node[i+1] == 0x20 {
92				refs = append(refs, hex.EncodeToString(node[i+2:i+34]))
93			}
94		}
95		return refs
96	}
97	
98	// TestReconstructSession_Structure validates the store.db has the content-addressed
99	// shape Cursor requires: sha256 ids, a head pointer, and a root whose field-1 lists
100	// the message blobs in order with a field-2 metadata node.
101	func TestReconstructSession_Structure(t *testing.T) {
102		data := reconstructSampleData()
103		expected := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "") // user + 3 agent turns
104	
105		out, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(data, spi.ReconstructOptions{WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp/proj"})
106		if err != nil {
107			t.Fatalf("ReconstructSession: %v", err)
108		}
109		if out.SessionID == "" || filepath.Base(out.Filename) != "store.db" {
110			t.Fatalf("unexpected id/filename: %q / %q", out.SessionID, out.Filename)
111		}
112	
113		blobs, meta := openStoreBytes(t, out.Content)
114	
115		// Every blob is content-addressed by sha256.
116		for id, d := range blobs {
117			if sum := sha256.Sum256(d); hex.EncodeToString(sum[:]) != id {
118				t.Errorf("blob id %s != sha256(data)", id)
119			}
120		}
121	
122		head, _ := meta["latestRootBlobId"].(string)
123		root, ok := blobs[head]
124		if !ok {
125			t.Fatalf("head %q not present in blobs", head)
126		}
127		if meta["agentId"] != out.SessionID {
128			t.Errorf("meta agentId %v != session id %q", meta["agentId"], out.SessionID)
129		}
130	
131		// Root field 1 = message blobs in order; verify roles + text match the turns.
132		msgRefs := refsOf(root, 1)
133		if len(msgRefs) != len(expected) {
134			t.Fatalf("root references %d messages, want %d", len(msgRefs), len(expected))
135		}
136		for i, ref := range msgRefs {
137			var m struct {
138				Role    string `json:"role"`
139				Content []struct {
140					Text string `json:"text"`
141				} `json:"content"`
142			}
143			if err := json.Unmarshal(blobs[ref], &m); err != nil {
144				t.Fatalf("message %d not JSON: %v", i, err)
145			}
146			wantRole := "assistant"
147			if expected[i].Role == schema.RoleUser {
148				wantRole = "user"
149			}
150			if m.Role != wantRole {
151				t.Errorf("message %d role = %q, want %q", i, m.Role, wantRole)
152			}
153			if len(m.Content) == 0 || m.Content[0].Text != expected[i].Text {
154				t.Errorf("message %d text mismatch:\n got: %q\nwant: %q", i, m.Content, expected[i].Text)
155			}
156		}
157	
158		// Root field 2 = metadata node, which must resolve.
159		metaRefs := refsOf(root, 2)
160		if len(metaRefs) != 1 || blobs[metaRefs[0]] == nil {
161			t.Errorf("root should reference exactly one metadata node; got %v", metaRefs)
162		}
163	}
164	
165	func TestNativeSessionPath(t *testing.T) {
166		path, err := NewProvider().NativeSessionPath(t.TempDir(), filepath.Join("sess-id", "store.db"))
167		if err != nil {
168			t.Fatalf("NativeSessionPath: %v", err)
169		}
170		if !strings.Contains(path, filepath.Join(".cursor", "chats")) {
171			t.Errorf("path %q should be under .cursor/chats", path)
172		}
173		if !strings.HasSuffix(path, filepath.Join("sess-id", "store.db")) {
174			t.Errorf("path %q should end with <session-id>/store.db", path)
175		}
176	}
177	
178	func TestReconstructSession_Empty(t *testing.T) {
179		_, err := NewProvider().ReconstructSession(&schema.SessionData{SessionID: "x", WorkspaceRoot: "/tmp"}, spi.ReconstructOptions{})
180		if err == nil {
181			t.Fatal("expected error reconstructing a session with no content")
182		}
183	}

	GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*AgentChatSession, error)

// ScanReporter accumulates a provider's enumeration progress (sessions found) so the CLI can
// render a live "scanning" display. It is safe for concurrent use and safe to call on a nil
// receiver (a no-op), so providers can report unconditionally.
type ScanReporter struct {
	found atomic.Int64
}

// Add records that n more sessions have been found. Files that yield no session (warmup-only or
// sidechain-only transcripts) are deliberately not counted, so the running total reflects real
// sessions rather than raw files.
func (r *ScanReporter) Add(n int) {
	if r != nil {
		r.found.Add(int64(n))
	}
}

// Found returns the number of sessions found so far.
func (r *ScanReporter) Found() int64 {
	if r == nil {
		return 0
	}
	return r.found.Load()
}

// ProgressEnumerator is an OPTIONAL Provider capability: enumerate all sessions while reporting
// scan progress into r (which may be nil — ScanReporter is nil-safe, so report unconditionally).
// Providers that don't implement it are enumerated via ListAllAgentChatSessions with no live
// feedback. reindex uses this to render a per-agent "Scanning agents…" display.
type ProgressEnumerator interface {
	ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *ScanReporter) ([]GlobalSessionRef, error)
}

+// GetAgentChatSessionByPath parses a single Codex session directly from its native file
+// path, implementing spi.PathSessionReader. It skips findCodexSessions' whole-tree walk —
+// which is what makes the by-id GetAgentChatSession O(N) per call, and a reindex of every
+// session O(N²) — by reading session_meta from the known file and handing off to the same
+// processSessionToAgentChat the by-id path uses. originCwd is the workspace root, matching
+// what GetAgentChatSession passes as projectPath.
+func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
+	meta, err := loadCodexSessionMeta(nativePath)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to load codex session meta: %w", err)
+	}
+	// debugRaw is only set when a caller wants provider-specific raw dumps + RawData; reindex
+	// (the only caller) never does. Honor it via the full path so that behavior is unchanged.
+	if debugRaw {
+		info := codexSessionInfo{
+			SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
+			SessionPath: nativePath,
+			Meta:        meta,
+		}
+		return processSessionToAgentChat(&info, originCwd, debugRaw)
+	}
+	// Index fast path: read records only (skip the whole-file rawData string reindex never
+	// reads) and skip slug derivation (reindex uses the slug from enumeration). The SessionData
+	// produced here is identical to the full path's.
+	records, err := readSessionRecords(nativePath)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read session data: %w", err)
+	}
+	if len(records) == 0 {
+		return nil, nil
+	}
+	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(records, originCwd)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to generate SessionData: %w", err)
+	}
+	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
+		SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
+		CreatedAt:   meta.Timestamp,
+		SessionData: sessionData,
+	}, nil
+}
+

 // The function blocks until the Codex CLI exits. During execution, it watches for JSONL file changes
 // and invokes sessionCallback for each update, enabling real-time markdown generation and cloud sync.

+// Codex sessions are hundreds of MB) is never read when indexing, so building it is pure waste.
+func readSessionRecords(sessionPath string) ([]map[string]interface{}, error) {

+}

+// readCodexJSONL reads a Codex session's JSONL records, and — when collectRaw is true — the raw
+// JSONL content as one string. Callers that only need records pass collectRaw=false to skip the
+// whole-file string builder.
+func readCodexJSONL(sessionPath string, collectRaw bool) ([]map[string]interface{}, string, error) {
 	file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
 	if err != nil {

+
+// codexSessionHeader is the minimal metadata enumeration needs from a session file: the
+// session_meta fields plus the first user message (for slug/name), produced in a SINGLE pass.
+type codexSessionHeader struct {
+	sessionID        string
+	cwd              string
+	createdAt        string
+	firstUserMessage string
+}
+
+// userMessageMarker is the cheap substring screen for a Codex user_message record. Every real
+// user_message record contains it (it is the payload "type" value), so screening on it before a
+// full JSON parse never misses one; a rare false positive (the literal text inside an injected
+// context record) just costs one extra parse that codexUserMessageText then rejects.
+const userMessageMarker = `"user_message"`
+
+// scanCodexSessionHeader reads a Codex session file ONCE and extracts the session_meta
+// (id/cwd/timestamp from line 1) and the first user message. It deliberately avoids fully
+// parsing the large injected-context records (environment, instructions, AGENTS.md — often MB
+// each) that precede the first user message: each line is first cheaply screened for the
+// user_message marker, and only a candidate line is JSON-decoded. The result is identical to the
+// prior loadCodexSessionMeta + extractCodexSessionMetadata pair, in one pass instead of two opens.
+// Returns (nil, nil) for a session with no user message — treated as empty, as before.
+func scanCodexSessionHeader(sessionPath string) (*codexSessionHeader, error) {
+	file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
+	}
+	defer func() { _ = file.Close() }()
+
+	reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
+	h := &codexSessionHeader{}
+	lineNum := 0
+	for {
+		line, readErr := reader.ReadString('\n')
+		if readErr != nil && readErr != io.EOF {
+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read line: %w", readErr)
+		}
+		lineNum++
+		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
+
+		if trimmed != "" {
+			if lineNum == 1 {
+				// Line 1 is session_meta. Keep loadCodexSessionMeta's 64 KB first-line limit so
+				// the set of indexed sessions is unchanged from the Scanner-based path.
+				if len(trimmed) > bufio.MaxScanTokenSize {
+					return nil, errors.New("codex session meta exceeds line limit")
+				}
+				var meta codexSessionMeta
+				if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &meta); err != nil {
+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse codex session meta: %w", err)
+				}
+				if meta.Type != "session_meta" {
+					return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected codex session record type: %s", meta.Type)
+				}
+				h.sessionID = strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID)
+				h.cwd = meta.Payload.CWD
+				h.createdAt = meta.Timestamp
+			} else if strings.Contains(trimmed, userMessageMarker) {
+				// Cheap screen passed: only now pay for a full parse. The big context records
+				// before the first user message lack the marker and are skipped without a parse.
+				if msg := codexUserMessageText(trimmed); msg != "" {
+					h.firstUserMessage = msg
+					break
+				}
+			}
+		}
+
+		if readErr == io.EOF {
+			break
+		}
+	}
+
+	if h.sessionID == "" {
+		return nil, errors.New("codex session meta not found")
+	}
+	if h.firstUserMessage == "" {
+		return nil, nil // no user message → empty session, excluded from enumeration
+	}
+	return h, nil
+}
+
+// codexUserMessageText returns the message text when line is an event_msg user_message with a
+// non-empty message, else "". It is only called for lines that passed the userMessageMarker
+// screen, and applies the same structural checks extractCodexSessionMetadata used.
+func codexUserMessageText(line string) string {
+	var record map[string]interface{}
+	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {
+		return ""
+	}
+	if recordType, _ := record["type"].(string); recordType != "event_msg" {
+		return ""
+	}
+	payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
+	if !ok {
+		return ""
+	}
+	if payloadType, _ := payload["type"].(string); payloadType != "user_message" {
+		return ""
+	}
+	message, _ := payload["message"].(string)
+	return message
+}
+
+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects. It is the
+// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
+	return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
+}
+
+// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects by
+// walking ~/.codex/sessions for *.jsonl rollouts (Codex sessions are not project-keyed by
+// directory; the originating cwd lives in the session_meta record), reporting scan progress into r
+// (nil-safe). The per-file header read is the dominant cost of `specstory reindex` (parsing each
+// file's injected context), so headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
+// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
+	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to determine home directory: %w", err)
+	}
+	sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
+	if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsRoot); err != nil {
+		// No sessions directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
+		return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
+	}
+
+	// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
+	var paths []string
+	walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(sessionsRoot, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
+		if err != nil {
+			return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the sweep
+		}
+		if d.IsDir() || filepath.Ext(path) != ".jsonl" {
+			return nil
+		}
+		paths = append(paths, path)
+		return nil
+	})
+	if walkErr != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk codex sessions: %w", walkErr)
+	}
+
+	// Phase 2: scan headers in parallel. Each file's single-pass read is CPU-bound (JSON) and the
+	// files are independent, so this scales across cores.
+	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
+	if workers < 1 {
+		workers = 1
+	}
+	pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
+	refs := make([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, 0, len(paths))
+	var mu sync.Mutex
+	var wg sync.WaitGroup
+	for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
+		wg.Add(1)
+		go func() {
+			defer wg.Done()
+			for path := range pathCh {
+				h, err := scanCodexSessionHeader(path)
+				if err != nil {
+					slog.Debug("reindex: failed to scan codex session header", "path", path, "error", err)
+					continue
+				}
+				if h == nil {
+					continue // empty session (no user message)
+				}
+				ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
+					SessionID:  h.sessionID,
+					CreatedAt:  h.createdAt,
+					Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(h.firstUserMessage),
+					Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(h.firstUserMessage),
+					NativePath: path,
+					OriginCwd:  h.cwd,
+				}
+				mu.Lock()
+				refs = append(refs, ref)
+				mu.Unlock()
+				r.Add(1) // count sessions found, not files scanned (warmup/empty files yield none)
+			}
+		}()
+	}
+	for _, path := range paths {
+		pathCh <- path
+	}
+	close(pathCh)
+	wg.Wait()
+	return refs, nil
+}

+
+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
+// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
+//
+// Cursor stores sessions at ~/.cursor/chats/<projectHash>/<sessionID>/store.db, where
+// projectHash = md5(canonical project path). Cursor records NO workspace path inside
+// the store (the meta blob has only agent/model fields), and md5 is one-way — so
+// OriginCwd cannot be filled from the store alone and is left empty here. The hash is
+// embedded in NativePath, so `specstory reindex` recovers the cwd by reverse-matching
+// that hash against the project paths the other providers surface.
+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
+	chatsDir, err := GetCursorChatsDir()
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	hashEntries, err := os.ReadDir(chatsDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read cursor chats directory: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
+	for _, hashEntry := range hashEntries {
+		if !hashEntry.IsDir() {
+			continue
+		}
+		hashDir := filepath.Join(chatsDir, hashEntry.Name())
+		sessionIDs, err := GetCursorSessionDirs(hashDir)
+		if err != nil {
+			continue
+		}
+		for _, sessionID := range sessionIDs {
+			if !HasStoreDB(hashDir, sessionID) {
+				continue
+			}
+			sessionPath := filepath.Join(hashDir, sessionID)
+			metadata, err := extractCursorSessionMetadata(sessionPath, sessionID)
+			if err != nil {
+				slog.Warn("reindex: failed to extract cursor session metadata", "sessionID", sessionID, "error", err)
+				continue
+			}
+			if metadata == nil {
+				continue // empty session
+			}
+			refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
+				SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
+				CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
+				Slug:       metadata.Slug,
+				Name:       metadata.Name,
+				NativePath: filepath.Join(sessionPath, "store.db"),
+				OriginCwd:  "", // not stored by Cursor; recovered at reindex via the hash in NativePath
+			})
+		}
+	}
+	return refs, nil
+}

 }
+
+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every DeepSeek TUI session in
+// ~/.deepseek/sessions/. DeepSeek sessions are not project-scoped by directory; the
+// originating workspace is recorded in each session's metadata.workspace field. See
+// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
+	if err != nil {
+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
+		}
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
+	for _, file := range files {
+		metadata, err := extractSessionMetadata(file.Path)
+		if err != nil {
+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to extract deepseek session metadata", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
+			continue
+		}
+		if metadata == nil {
+			continue // empty session
+		}
+		refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
+			SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
+			CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
+			Slug:       metadata.Slug,
+			Name:       metadata.Name,
+			NativePath: file.Path,
+			OriginCwd:  extractWorkspaceFast(file.Path),
+		})
+	}
+	return refs, nil
+}

+
+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Factory Droid session across all projects
+// in ~/.factory/sessions/. The originating cwd is read from inside each session
+// (parseFactorySession resolves it into WorkspaceRoot, preferring the recorded cwd).
+// See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
+	if err != nil {
+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
+		}
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
+	for _, file := range files {
+		session, err := parseFactorySession(file.Path)
+		if err != nil {
+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to parse droid session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
+			continue
+		}
+		if session == nil || len(session.Blocks) == 0 {
+			continue // empty session
+		}
+		refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
+			SessionID:  session.ID,
+			CreatedAt:  session.CreatedAt,
+			Slug:       session.Slug,
+			Name:       generateDroidSessionName(session),
+			NativePath: file.Path,
+			OriginCwd:  strings.TrimSpace(session.WorkspaceRoot),
+		})
+	}
+	return refs, nil
+}

--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/provider.go
+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/provider.go
@@ -416,3 +416,59 @@ func extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session *GeminiSession) *spi.SessionMetadata {
 		Name:      name,
 	}
 }
+
+// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
+// regardless of project. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
+//
+// Gemini associates a tmp directory (~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/) with a project via a
+// .project_root marker file holding the project path, which is the originating cwd
+// for every session in that directory.
+func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
+	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
+			return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	var refs []spi.GlobalSessionRef
+	for _, entry := range entries {
+		if !entry.IsDir() {
+			continue
+		}
+		dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, entry.Name())
+
+		// The .project_root marker holds the project path — the originating cwd shared
+		// by every session in this tmp directory.
+		var cwd string
+		if content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root")); err == nil {
+			cwd = strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
+		}
+
+		sessions, err := FindSessions(dir)
+		if err != nil {
+			slog.Debug("reindex: failed to find gemini sessions", "dir", dir, "error", err)
+			continue
+		}
+		for _, session := range sessions {
+			metadata := extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session)
+			if metadata == nil {
+				continue // empty session
+			}
+			refs = append(refs, spi.GlobalSessionRef{
+				SessionID:  metadata.SessionID,
+				CreatedAt:  metadata.CreatedAt,
+				Slug:       metadata.Slug,
+				Name:       metadata.Name,
+				NativePath: session.FilePath,
+				OriginCwd:  cwd,
+			})
+		}
+	}
+	return refs, nil
+}

```text
// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects. It is the
// no-progress form of ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress.
func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
	return p.ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)
}

// ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress enumerates every Codex CLI session across all projects by
// walking ~/.codex/sessions for *.jsonl rollouts (Codex sessions are not project-keyed by
// directory; the originating cwd lives in the session_meta record), reporting scan progress into r
// (nil-safe). The per-file header read is the dominant cost of `specstory reindex` (parsing each
// file's injected context), so headers are scanned in parallel across CPUs; output order is
// irrelevant (reindex dedups and sorts later). Implements spi.ProgressEnumerator. See
// docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
func (p *Provider) ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *spi.ScanReporter) ([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, error) {
	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to determine home directory: %w", err)
	}
	sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
	if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsRoot); err != nil {
		// No sessions directory yet → nothing to enumerate (not an error).
		return []spi.GlobalSessionRef{}, nil
	}

	// Phase 1: collect file paths (dirents only — no opens).
	var paths []string
	walkErr := filepath.WalkDir(sessionsRoot, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
		if err != nil {
			return nil // skip unreadable entries rather than abort the sweep
		}
		if d.IsDir() || filepath.Ext(path) != ".jsonl" {
			return nil
		}
		paths = append(paths, path)
		return nil
	})
	if walkErr != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to walk codex sessions: %w", walkErr)
	}

	// Phase 2: scan headers in parallel. Each file's single-pass read is CPU-bound (JSON) and the
	// files are independent, so this scales across cores.
	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
	if workers < 1 {
		workers = 1
	}
	pathCh := make(chan string, workers)
	refs := make([]spi.GlobalSessionRef, 0, len(paths))
	var mu sync.Mutex
	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
		wg.Add(1)
		go func() {
			defer wg.Done()
			for path := range pathCh {
				h, err := scanCodexSessionHeader(path)
				if err != nil {
					slog.Debug("reindex: failed to scan codex session header", "path", path, "error", err)
					continue
				}
				if h == nil {
					continue // empty session (no user message)
				}
				ref := spi.GlobalSessionRef{
					SessionID:  h.sessionID,
					CreatedAt:  h.createdAt,
					Slug:       spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(h.firstUserMessage),
					Name:       spi.GenerateReadableName(h.firstUserMessage),
					NativePath: path,
					OriginCwd:  h.cwd,
				}
				mu.Lock()
				refs = append(refs, ref)
				mu.Unlock()
				r.Add(1) // count sessions found, not files scanned (warmup/empty files yield none)
			}
		}()
	}
	for _, path := range paths {
		pathCh <- path
	}
	close(pathCh)
	wg.Wait()
	return refs, nil
}
```

 `./pkg/spi/global.go`

```text
39	type PathSessionReader interface {
40		GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*AgentChatSession, error)
41	}
42	
43	// ScanReporter accumulates a provider's enumeration progress (sessions found) so the CLI can
44	// render a live "scanning" display. It is safe for concurrent use and safe to call on a nil
45	// receiver (a no-op), so providers can report unconditionally.
46	type ScanReporter struct {
47		found atomic.Int64
48	}
49	
50	// Add records that n more sessions have been found. Files that yield no session (warmup-only or
51	// sidechain-only transcripts) are deliberately not counted, so the running total reflects real
52	// sessions rather than raw files.
53	func (r *ScanReporter) Add(n int) {
54		if r != nil {
55			r.found.Add(int64(n))
56		}
57	}
58	
59	// Found returns the number of sessions found so far.
60	func (r *ScanReporter) Found() int64 {
61		if r == nil {
62			return 0
63		}
64		return r.found.Load()
65	}
66	
67	// ProgressEnumerator is an OPTIONAL Provider capability: enumerate all sessions while reporting
68	// scan progress into r (which may be nil — ScanReporter is nil-safe, so report unconditionally).
69	// Providers that don't implement it are enumerated via ListAllAgentChatSessions with no live
70	// feedback. reindex uses this to render a per-agent "Scanning agents…" display.
71	type ProgressEnumerator interface {
72		ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(r *ScanReporter) ([]GlobalSessionRef, error)
73	}
```
</details></tool-use>

## Review: PR #244 (spi reconstruct/provider/global + config)

### pkg/spi/reconstruct.go

Line 58-96 (new): `FlattenSessionData` flattens SessionData into user/agent turns.

- (1) ✅ Well-structured. The `migrationNote` is trimmed once at line 61 and again at 62 — minor double-trim, but harmless and readable.
- (2) ✅ Iterating with `for i := range ... { msg := &data.Exchanges[i].Messages[j] }` (lines 69-71) correctly avoids copying `Message` structs by value. Idiomatic.
- (3) ✅ The "synthetic turn" drop (lines 83-89) has a strong why-comment explaining it's reconstruction-only and markdown stays faithful. Good.
- (4) ❓ Return value is naked `nil` for nil input (via `var turns []Turn`), which `TestFlattenSessionData_Nil` asserts. Fine, but note `PrepareTurns` guards nil separately — so `FlattenSessionData`'s own nil tolerance is only exercised by tests and the `migrationNote`-only path. Not dead, just worth knowing the nil branch is defensive.

Line 104-115 (new): `PrepareTurns` validation wrapper.

- (5) ✅ Good DRY centralization — confirmed all six providers (`codexcli`, `claudecode`, `droidcli`, `geminicli`, `deepseektui`, `cursorcli`) call `spi.PrepareTurns`, so the shared-guard claim in the comment is real and the messages can't drift.
- (6) ✅ Multiple returns but all guard clauses — within the "guard clauses OK" convention.

Line 121-129 (new): `ResolveWorkspaceRoot`.

- (7) ✅ Simple precedence helper, used by 5 providers (verified). Comment notes cursor is the exception that needs no workspace — good why-comment.

Line 139-146 (new): `SyntheticCommandTags`.

- (8) ✅ The "SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH" claim is verified: `pkg/providers/claudecode/provider.go:505` iterates `spi.SyntheticCommandTags` in `isSyntheticMessage`. The cross-package sharing is real, not aspirational.

Line 148-174 (new): `extraSyntheticTurnMarkers` + `isSyntheticTurnText`.

- (9) ⚠️ Naming asymmetry: `SyntheticCommandTags` is exported (shared) but `extraSyntheticTurnMarkers` is unexported. The comment explains why (`<TEXTBLOCK>`/interrupt are Claude-specific extras), but a reader may wonder why interrupt markers — which are Claude-specific — live in the shared spi layer rather than the claudecode provider. Defensible (the flattened turn filter is provider-agnostic), but worth a one-line why about placement.
- (10) ✅ `isSyntheticTurnText` matches by `Contains` with a clear comment explaining the concatenation rationale. Two near-identical loops (lines 163-167, 168-172) could be one loop over a combined slice, but the split mirrors the two-variable structure intentionally. Minor.

Line 178-203 (new): `messageTurnText`.

- (11) ✅ Clear. The text/thinking-both-use-Text comment (lines 182-183) is a good why-comment for a non-obvious schema fact.
- (12) ✅ Single logical exit. Join + optional tool-text append is readable.

Line 208-229 (new): `toolTurnText`.

- (13) ✅ Fallback (`"Tool use: " + tool.Name`) has a why-comment. Sensible.

### pkg/spi/global.go (new file)

- (14) ✅ Excellent why-comments throughout — `GlobalSessionRef` (lines 209-219) and `PathSessionReader` (lines 228-242) explain the O(N²)→O(N) motivation concretely. This is exactly the "why not what" style the conventions ask for.
- (15) ✅ `ScanReporter` (lines 250-269) is nil-safe on every method with the rationale stated ("safe to call on a nil receiver… so providers can report unconditionally"). Verified callers rely on this: `reindex.go` passes `nil` via `ListAllAgentChatSessions() → ListAllAgentChatSessionsProgress(nil)`. Real, not speculative.
- (16) ✅ `atomic.Int64` is the right primitive for concurrent `Add`/`Found`; `reindex.go:188` calls these from per-provider goroutines. Correct.
- (17) ✅ All three optional-capability interfaces (`PathSessionReader`, `ProgressEnumerator`) are actually type-asserted in `reindex.go` (lines 194, 566). No dead abstraction.

### pkg/spi/provider.go

Line 282-312 (new interface methods): `ReconstructSession`, `NativeSessionPath`, `ListAllAgentChatSessions`.

- (18) ✅ All three are implemented by every provider and called from `pkg/cmd` (`resume.go:325,341`, `reindex.go:197`). The "every provider carries this responsibility; unsupported returns ErrReconstructionUnsupported" doc matches the actual pattern. No dead interface surface.
- (19) ✅ Doc comments are thorough and explain the contract (pure transform, no filesystem writes). Good.

### pkg/config/config.go

Line 137 + 142-149 (new): `Resume` field + `ResumeConfig`.

- (20) ✅ Struct + field comments are clear and distinguish the user-set `ViewMode` from the auto-written `LastAgent`.

Line 746-755 (new): `GetResumeViewMode` / `GetResumeLastAgent`.

- (21) ⚠️ Minor doc/template inconsistency: `GetResumeViewMode` defaults to `"dense"` (line 749-751), but the template example at config.go shows `# view_mode = "sparse"`. Commented examples often show the non-default, but here it could mislead a reader into thinking sparse is the default. Consider matching the example to the real default, or adding "(default)" next to dense in the comment.

Line 763-797 (new): `SaveResumePrefs` + `upsertResumeSection`.

- (22) ⚠️ Lost blank-line separator on the replace path. In `upsertResumeSection`, when replacing an existing `[resume]` block, `end` stops at the next section header, so the blank line that separated `[resume]` from the following section is consumed and not re-emitted. Output becomes `last_agent = "gemini"\n[providers]` with no blank line between sections. Still valid TOML (and the test only checks `Contains`), but it's a cosmetic regression in the self-documenting file. The append path (lines 106-112) correctly inserts `\n\n`; the replace path should preserve/emit a trailing separator too.
- (23) ⚠️ First-save clobbers the `[resume]` help comments. Because the template ships an active (uncommented) `[resume]` header with commented help keys, the first `SaveResumePrefs` finds that header and replaces the whole block — dropping the `# Default view mode…` documentation. The doc comment says only the `[resume]` block is rewritten and other sections are untouched, so this is by-design, but the resume section uniquely loses its self-documentation after first use. Worth a why-line acknowledging it.
- (24) ❓ No slog observability. `SaveResumePrefs` returns errors but logs nothing; one caller (`search.go:113`) discards the error entirely (`_ = config.SaveResumePrefs(...)`). Since the convention asks for consistent slog tracing, a `slog.Debug` on the write (or at least not silently swallowing the error at the call site) would help. Note: the call sites are outside this review slice, but the silent-discard is enabled by this function having no internal logging.
- (25) ✅ `SaveResumePrefs` guard clauses + single happy-path return are within convention. Permissions (0o755 dir, 0o644 file) match `getUserConfigPath` conventions elsewhere.
- (26) ✅ Reuses `processTemplate`/`getUserConfigPath` rather than duplicating path logic — good DRY.

### pkg/config/config_test.go

- (27) ✅ `TestUpsertResumeSection_AppendWhenAbsent` / `_ReplacePreservingOthers` test real logic and round-trip through `toml.Decode` to prove validity — not tautological. The duplicate-count assertion (`strings.Count(out, "[resume]") != 1`) is a good guard against the duplication bug.
- (28) ⚠️ Neither test asserts the blank-line separator behavior (issue 22), so the cosmetic regression is uncaught. If the separator is intended to be preserved, add an assertion; if not, that's fine.
- (29) ✅ `TestGetResumeViewModeDefaults` is slightly trivial but exercises both the default and the override branch — acceptable, not pure tautology.

### pkg/spi/reconstruct_test.go

- (30) ⚠️ Repetitive vs data-driven. Seven separate `TestFlattenSessionData_*` functions (`main`, `MigrationNote`, `ToolFallback`, `DropsSyntheticTurns`, `Nil`, `SkipsBlankTurns`, `ToolOnlySession`) all follow the identical shape: build `SessionData` → `FlattenSessionData` → compare `[]Turn`. They could collapse into one table-driven test with a `name`/`data`/`migrationNote`/`want` row each. Each case is meaningful (distinct rule), so this is a structure nit, not a correctness one — but it's exactly the repetition the conventions flag.
- (31) ✅ `TestPrepareTurns` and `TestResolveWorkspaceRoot` ARE properly table-driven and cover real branches (nil, empty, migration-note-as-content, precedence). Well designed.
- (32) ✅ Tests assert real, non-trivial behavior (synthetic-tag dropping, thinking-folding, tool fallback). Not testing stdlib. Deterministic — no flakiness risk (no time/goroutines/maps-ordering).
- (33) ❓ `strptr` is redefined in this file and in all six provider `reconstruct_test.go` files (verified). Per-package test helpers can't easily be shared in Go without an exported helper, so this is acceptable, but a single exported `schema`-package test helper (or `Ptr[T]`) would remove the duplication if desired.

---

### (a) Most important issues in this slice

1. **`upsertResumeSection` drops the blank-line separator on the replace path** (config.go:124-136) — sections become visually adjacent in the self-documenting config file, and no test covers it. Cosmetic but the file is user-facing.
2. **First-save clobbers the `[resume]` help comments** (config.go, by design but undocumented) — the resume section uniquely loses its self-documentation after the first resume; the discrepancy with the "comments untouched" doc deserves a why-line.
3. **`SaveResumePrefs` has no slog observability and is called with the error discarded** (config.go:763) — silent failure to persist prefs; a `slog.Debug` would align with the project's "consistent observability" rule.

### (b) Test-quality assessment

- **config_test.go**: Good. Real logic, TOML round-trip validation, duplicate guard. Not tautological. Gap: no assertion on the separator/comment-preservation cosmetic behavior.
- **reconstruct_test.go**: Substantively strong and reliable (deterministic, tests real flattening rules, not stdlib). The `FlattenSessionData_*` family is **repetitive and should be table-driven** — that's the main quality nit. `TestPrepareTurns`/`TestResolveWorkspaceRoot` are exemplary table-driven tests. `strptr` duplication across packages is acceptable Go convention.

No dead code found — every new spi symbol and config function is wired to real callers in `pkg/cmd` and the providers.

---

Seven separate `TestFlattenSessionData_*` functions (`main`, `MigrationNote`, `ToolFallback`, `DropsSyntheticTurns`, `Nil`, `SkipsBlankTurns`, `ToolOnlySession`) all follow the identical shape: build `SessionData` → `FlattenSessionData` → compare `[]Turn`. They could collapse into one table-driven test with a `name`/`data`/`migrationNote`/`want` row each. Each case is meaningful (distinct rule), so this is a structure nit, not a correctness one — but it's exactly the repetition the conventions flag.
- (31) ✅ `TestPrepareTurns` and `TestResolveWorkspaceRoot` ARE properly table-driven and cover real branches (nil, empty, migration-note-as-content, precedence). Well designed.
- (32) ✅ Tests assert real, non-trivial behavior (synthetic-tag dropping, thinking-folding, tool fallback). Not testing stdlib. Deterministic — no flakiness risk (no time/goroutines/maps-ordering).
- (33) ❓ `strptr` is redefined in this file and in all six provider `reconstruct_test.go` files (verified). Per-package test helpers can't easily be shared in Go without an exported helper, so this is acceptable, but a single exported `schema`-package test helper (or `Ptr[T]`) would remove the duplication if desired.

---

### (a) Most important issues in this slice

1. **`upsertResumeSection` drops the blank-line separator on the replace path** (config.go:124-136) — sections become visually adjacent in the self-documenting config file, and no test covers it. Cosmetic but the file is user-facing.
2. **First-save clobbers the `[resume]` help comments** (config.go, by design but undocumented) — the resume section uniquely loses its self-documentation after the first resume; the discrepancy with the "comments untouched" doc deserves a why-line.
3. **`SaveResumePrefs` has no slog observability and is called with the error discarded** (config.go:763) — silent failure to persist prefs; a `slog.Debug` would align with the project's "consistent observability" rule.

### (b) Test-quality assessment

- **config_test.go**: Good. Real logic, TOML round-trip validation, duplicate guard. Not tautological. Gap: no assertion on the separator/comment-preservation cosmetic behavior.
- **reconstruct_test.go**: Substantively strong and reliable (deterministic, tests real flattening rules, not stdlib). The `FlattenSessionData_*` family is **repetitive and should be table-driven** — that's the main quality nit. `TestPrepareTurns`/`TestResolveWorkspaceRoot` are exemplary table-driven tests. `strptr` duplication across packages is acceptable Go convention.

No dead code found — every new spi symbol and config function is wired to real callers in `pkg/cmd` and the providers.</result>

286	// Same-agent resume reuses the existing session; cross-agent reconstructs the

288	func prepareResumeTarget(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, out io.Writer) (string, error) {
289		// Same agent: native resume on the existing session, no transform.
290		if plan.fromID == plan.toID {

296		// failure rates are visible. Fired at each terminal branch below.

310		// lives elsewhere, and providers like Codex locate it by its recorded cwd. The

I have everything I need. Here is the line-by-line review.

---

`pkg/cmd/reindex.go`

Line 27-47 (new): package constants with `reindexVersion` changelog.
- (1) ✅ Excellent why-comments. The `reindexVersion` history embedded as a comment is a great pattern — explains exactly why a bump re-parses everything. `unknownProjectID` and `reindexWriteBatch` are well-documented.

Line 63-66 (new): `RunE` ignores the error from `cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")`.
- (2) ✅ Idiomatic for a flag the command itself declared; cannot fail. Fine.

Line 83-159 (new): `runReindex`.
- (3) ⚠️ ~75 lines but cleanly sectioned with phase comments. Acceptable, though the "found == 0" guard (line 120) runs *after* `dedupRefs` and `selectWork` have already executed. Functionally harmless (empty `order` makes both trivial), but the guard reads as if it should short-circuit earlier. Minor.
- (4) ✅ Graceful Ctrl+C via `signal.NotifyContext` (line 87) with a clear why-comment.
- (5) ⚠️ Lines 148-149: `store.ProjectCount(...)` and `UnattributedCount(...)` errors are discarded with `_`. For a summary line this is defensible, but a `slog.Debug` on error would aid diagnosis when counts render as 0. Minor.
- (6) ✅ Analytics `EventReindexCompleted` (line 151) is fired with useful properties; event constant exists in `pkg/analytics/events.go`. Note it is *not* fired on the "all up to date" (line 126) or "no sessions" (line 121) early returns — likely intentional (no work done), but worth a conscious decision.

Line 167-208 (new): `enumerateAll`.
- (7) ✅ Index-aligned slices documented; explicit param capture into the goroutine (line 188) is safe pre-Go1.22 and clear.
- (8) ⚠️ A provider goroutine that panics during enumeration takes down the whole reindex (no `recover`). Providers are trusted internal code, so low risk, but one malformed session tree shouldn't abort the entire index. Consider a `defer recover()` per provider that logs and yields nil refs.
- (9) ✅ Enumeration errors are logged via `slog.Warn` (line 200) and the run continues — correct best-effort behavior.

Line 217-246 (new): `dedupRefs`.
- (10) ✅ The dedup rationale (resumed sessions spanning multiple files ping-ponging the fingerprint) is one of the best comments in the file — exactly the kind of non-obvious "why" the conventions ask for.
- (11) ⚠️ `statNative` (line 229) is called for *every* ref including ones immediately discarded as the older duplicate. For large corpora (the comment cites Codex at ~18k sessions) that's some redundant `os.Stat` calls. Probably negligible vs. parsing, but the freshest-wins comparison only needs the stat of refs that survive. Minor perf note.

Line 252-275 (new): `selectWork`.
- (12) ✅ The `isNew` derivation and its FTS-delete-skip optimization are well-explained (lines 266-270), including the subtle `--force` caveat.

Line 279-343 (new): reporter interface + `processWork`.
- (13) ❗ Lines 307-342: **potential deadlock on a write error.** The writer goroutine runs `drainToStore`; if `UpsertBatch` fails it returns early and closes `writerDone`, so nothing drains `sessionsCh` anymore. Workers keep producing into `sessionsCh` (buffer 256, line 307). Once the buffer fills, every worker blocks forever on `sessionsCh <- sess` (line 324), so `wwg.Wait()` (line 339) never returns and the command hangs instead of surfacing the write error. This only bites when >256 work items remain after the error (i.e. a real reindex of thousands), but that's the common case. The writer should signal failure back (e.g. close a `cancel`/done channel the feed loop and the `sessionsCh` send select on) so the pipeline tears down on write failure. This is the most important issue.
- (14) ⚠️ Line 333 uses `goto closing`. It works, but a labeled `break` on the feed loop is more idiomatic Go and avoids the bare label. Style only.
- (15) ✅ Largest-first scheduling (line 305) with a clear straggler-overlap rationale; good performance thinking.

Line 351-391 (new): `warmIndexInBackground`.
- (16) ✅ Two-pass design (current project, then full corpus) and the fingerprint reload between passes (line 386) are well-justified. Errors logged at debug only — appropriate for best-effort warmth.
- (17) ✅ `ctx.Err()` checks at lines 361/377 give prompt cancellation. Note this shares the `processWork` deadlock risk (16): a write error during warm would hang the background goroutine for the TUI's lifetime, though silently.

Line 405-510 (new): `LiveIndexer`.
- (18) ✅ Nil-receiver safety on `Record`/`Close`, mutex serialization, and the `lastSeen` no-op skip are all documented and correct. The `isNew` "check by PK once" logic (lines 471-476) is subtle but well-commented.

Line 517-556 (new): `buildSession`.
- (19) ⚠️ On parse failure it logs debug and stores a metadata-only row *with the native file's size/mtime fingerprint* (via the item). The next reindex will see an unchanged fingerprint and skip re-parsing, so a *transient* parse failure (e.g. a momentarily locked file) sticks as metadata-only until the file changes or `reindexVersion` bumps. Likely acceptable, but worth a one-line comment acknowledging that transient failures are not retried.
- (20) ✅ Three return points but all are guard-style; reads cleanly.

Line 565-570 (new): `parseFullSession`.
- (21) ✅ The O(N) vs O(N²) rationale for preferring `PathSessionReader` over by-id discovery is exactly the kind of load-bearing comment that justifies the capability check.

Line 573-591 (new): `drainToStore`.
- (22) ✅ Clean batched-flush closure. (Its early-return-on-error is the upstream half of issue 13 — the function itself is fine; the pipeline around it is what hangs.)

Line 598-646 (new): field derivation helpers.
- (23) ✅ `flattenBody`, `countTurns`, `lastTimestamp`, `statNative` are small, single-purpose, well-named. `statNative` documents its units (epoch ms) — good, since `time` defaults bite easily.

Line 650-674 (new): `projectIDCache`.
- (24) ✅ Memoization rationale (each resolution walks to git root) justifies the cache; mutex-guarded; unknown fallback consistent with `resolve("")`.

Line 695-841 (new): `reindexProgress`.
- (25) ✅ Concurrency model is sound: workers only touch atomic counters and the mutex-guarded `projects` set; only the loop goroutine draws; `linesDrawn`/`draw(true)` in `end()` run after the loop goroutine has exited (lines 734-736). No data race.
- (26) ✅ Non-TTY branch prints plain `indexed N/M…` lines (line 799) — this is intended user-facing progress on stderr, correctly *not* routed through slog. Good distinction per the conventions.

Line 844-854 (new): `progressBar`.
- (27) ✅ Divide-by-zero guarded (line 847) and clamped (line 851); covered by tests.

Line 858-864 (new): `isTerminal`.
- (28) ✅ Stdlib-only TTY check with a why-comment explaining the avoided `isatty` dependency — aligns with the "prefer stdlib" convention.

Line 885-1017 (new): `scanProgress`.
- (29) ✅ Nil-`*scanProgress` is uniformly a no-op so the background warm passes nil silently — clean. `markDone`'s read-then-`Add` (line 933) is not atomic as a unit, but each reporter has exactly one writer (its own provider goroutine) and the loop goroutine only *reads* via `Found()`, so there's no race. Worth a one-line comment stating that single-writer invariant, since it's load-bearing.
- (30) ✅ Spinner/`linesDrawn`/`spin` are all touched only by the single loop goroutine (and `end()` after it stops). Safe.

Line 1021-1043 (new): summary + `prettyPath`.
- (31) ✅ Straightforward, branches on `unchanged`/`unattributed` for readable output. `prettyPath` home-abbreviation is a nice touch.

General:
- (32) ⚠️ The file is 1043 lines spanning four concerns: the command + engine, the live indexer, two progress renderers, and the summary. It's cohesive and each function is reasonably sized, but splitting the progress renderers into `reindex_progress.go` (and possibly `live_indexer.go`) would lower the cognitive load. Not blocking.
- (33) ✅ No dead code: `nopReporter`, `grandTotal`, `projectCount`, `totalDone`, `isTerminal`, `progressBar` all have callers; `CreateReindexCommand`, `warmIndexInBackground`, `NewLiveIndexer` are wired in `main.go:1458`, `session_tui.go:1216`, `resume.go:254`/`watch.go:193`.

---

`pkg/cmd/reindex_test.go`

- (34) ✅ `TestDedupRefs` (line 114) is high-value: real temp files with set mtimes exercise the freshest-wins path, blank-id drop, and nil-provider skip in one focused case. The comment explains why real files are used.
- (35) ✅ `TestSelectWork` (line 162) is properly table-driven and covers the meaningful matrix: unchanged, changed mtime, version bump, new, and force. Each asserts `isNew` correctly. This is the strongest test.
- (36) ✅ `TestLiveIndexerRecordConcurrent` (line 258) targets the mutex with 24 goroutines against a real store and is explicitly meant for `-race`. Good — directly validates the serialization claim.
- (37) ⚠️ `TestProjectIDCacheMemoizes` (line 73) seeds the map then reads it back — it verifies `resolve` consults the cache but, because the value is pre-seeded, it doesn't prove memoization *writes* a computed value. Slightly tautological; a stronger version would resolve a real temp git dir twice and assert the second call doesn't recompute (e.g. via a spy/counter). Low priority.
- (38) ⚠️ Coverage gap: the concurrency core — `processWork`/`drainToStore` — has no test, so the issue-13 deadlock-on-write-error is uncaught. A test injecting an `UpsertBatch` failure with >256 queued items would both document the contract and catch the hang.
- (39) ✅ No flakiness: no real sleeps, no wall-clock dependence, fixed timestamps, `t.TempDir()` for isolation.

---

Most important issues

1. (13) Deadlock on write error in `processWork` — if `UpsertBatch` fails mid-run with more than ~256 items still queued, workers block on the unread `sessionsCh` and the command hangs instead of returning the write error. The writer needs to signal teardown back to the feed loop and worker sends. Same risk silently affects `warmIndexInBackground`.
2. (8) No panic recovery per provider goroutine in `enumerateAll`/the worker pool — a single malformed session tree can abort the entire reindex.
3. (32/14) Maintainability polish: the 1043-line file would benefit from splitting out the progress renderers, and `goto closing` should be a labeled break for idiomatic Go.

Test-quality assessment

Strong overall: data-driven where it matters (`TestSelectWork`, `TestProgressBar`), the dedup and concurrent-live-indexer tests exercise real behavior against real files/stores, and there's no flakiness (fixed timestamps, `t.TempDir`). The notable gap is the write pipeline (`processWork`/`drainToStore`) — its error and cancellation paths, including the deadlock in issue 13, are untested. `TestProjectIDCacheMemoizes` is mildly tautological. Nothing trivial-but-padding; coverage is well-targeted apart from the pipeline gap.

Line 63-66 (new): `RunE` ignores the error from `cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")`.
- (2) ✅ Idiomatic for a flag the command itself declared; cannot fail. Fine.

Line 83-159 (new): `runReindex`.
- (3) ⚠️ ~75 lines but cleanly sectioned with phase comments. Acceptable, though the "found == 0" guard (line 120) runs *after* `dedupRefs` and `selectWork` have already executed. Functionally harmless (empty `order` makes both trivial), but the guard reads as if it should short-circuit earlier. Minor.
- (4) ✅ Graceful Ctrl+C via `signal.NotifyContext` (line 87) with a clear why-comment.
- (5) ⚠️ Lines 148-149: `store.ProjectCount(...)` and `UnattributedCount(...)` errors are discarded with `_`. For a summary line this is defensible, but a `slog.Debug` on error would aid diagnosis when counts render as 0. Minor.
- (6) ✅ Analytics `EventReindexCompleted` (line 151) is fired with useful properties; event constant exists in `pkg/analytics/events.go`. Note it is *not* fired on the "all up to date" (line 126) or "no sessions" (line 121) early returns — likely intentional (no work done), but worth a conscious decision.

Line 167-208 (new): `enumerateAll`.
- (7) ✅ Index-aligned slices documented; explicit param capture into the goroutine (line 188) is safe pre-Go1.22 and clear.
- (8) ⚠️ A provider goroutine that panics during enumeration takes down the whole reindex (no `recover`). Providers are trusted internal code, so low risk, but one malformed session tree shouldn't abort the entire index. Consider a `defer recover()` per provider that logs and yields nil refs.
- (9) ✅ Enumeration errors are logged via `slog.Warn` (line 200) and the run continues — correct best-effort behavior.

Line 217-246 (new): `dedupRefs`.
- (10) ✅ The dedup rationale (resumed sessions spanning multiple files ping-ponging the fingerprint) is one of the best comments in the file — exactly the kind of non-obvious "why" the conventions ask for.
- (11) ⚠️ `statNative` (line 229) is called for *every* ref including ones immediately discarded as the older duplicate. For large corpora (the comment cites Codex at ~18k sessions) that's some redundant `os.Stat` calls. Probably negligible vs. parsing, but the freshest-wins comparison only needs the stat of refs that survive. Minor perf note.

Line 252-275 (new): `selectWork`.
- (12) ✅ The `isNew` derivation and its FTS-delete-skip optimization are well-explained (lines 266-270), including the subtle `--force` caveat.

Line 279-343 (new): reporter interface + `processWork`.
- (13) ❗ Lines 307-342: **potential deadlock on a write error.** The writer goroutine runs `drainToStore`; if `UpsertBatch` fails it returns early and closes `writerDone`, so nothing drains `sessionsCh` anymore. Workers keep producing into `sessionsCh` (buffer 256, line 307). Once the buffer fills, every worker blocks forever on `sessionsCh &lt;- sess` (line 324), so `wwg.Wait()` (line 339) never returns and the command hangs instead of surfacing the write error. This only bites when &gt;256 work items remain after the error (i.e. a real reindex of thousands), but that's the common case. The writer should signal failure back (e.g. close a `cancel`/done channel the feed loop and the `sessionsCh` send select on) so the pipeline tears down on write failure. This is the most important issue.
- (14) ⚠️ Line 333 uses `goto closing`. It works, but a labeled `break` on the feed loop is more idiomatic Go and avoids the bare label. Style only.
- (15) ✅ Largest-first scheduling (line 305) with a clear straggler-overlap rationale; good performance thinking.

Line 351-391 (new): `warmIndexInBackground`.
- (16) ✅ Two-pass design (current project, then full corpus) and the fingerprint reload between passes (line 386) are well-justified. Errors logged at debug only — appropriate for best-effort warmth.
- (17) ✅ `ctx.Err()` checks at lines 361/377 give prompt cancellation. Note this shares the `processWork` deadlock risk (16): a write error during warm would hang the background goroutine for the TUI's lifetime, though silently.

Line 405-510 (new): `LiveIndexer`.
- (18) ✅ Nil-receiver safety on `Record`/`Close`, mutex serialization, and the `lastSeen` no-op skip are all documented and correct. The `isNew` "check by PK once" logic (lines 471-476) is subtle but well-commented.

Line 517-556 (new): `buildSession`.
- (19) ⚠️ On parse failure it logs debug and stores a metadata-only row *with the native file's size/mtime fingerprint* (via the item). The next reindex will see an unchanged fingerprint and skip re-parsing, so a *transient* parse failure (e.g. a momentarily locked file) sticks as metadata-only until the file changes or `reindexVersion` bumps. Likely acceptable, but worth a one-line comment acknowledging that transient failures are not retried.
- (20) ✅ Three return points but all are guard-style; reads cleanly.

Line 565-570 (new): `parseFullSession`.
- (21) ✅ The O(N) vs O(N²) rationale for preferring `PathSessionReader` over by-id discovery is exactly the kind of load-bearing comment that justifies the capability check.

Line 573-591 (new): `drainToStore`.
- (22) ✅ Clean batched-flush closure. (Its early-return-on-error is the upstream half of issue 13 — the function itself is fine; the pipeline around it is what hangs.)

Line 598-646 (new): field derivation helpers.
- (23) ✅ `flattenBody`, `countTurns`, `lastTimestamp`, `statNative` are small, single-purpose, well-named. `statNative` documents its units (epoch ms) — good, since `time` defaults bite easily.

Line 650-674 (new): `projectIDCache`.
- (24) ✅ Memoization rationale (each resolution walks to git root) justifies the cache; mutex-guarded; unknown fallback consistent with `resolve("")`.

Line 695-841 (new): `reindexProgress`.
- (25) ✅ Concurrency model is sound: workers only touch atomic counters and the mutex-guarded `projects` set; only the loop goroutine draws; `linesDrawn`/`draw(true)` in `end()` run after the loop goroutine has exited (lines 734-736). No data race.
- (26) ✅ Non-TTY branch prints plain `indexed N/M…` lines (line 799) — this is intended user-facing progress on stderr, correctly *not* routed through slog. Good distinction per the conventions.

Line 844-854 (new): `progressBar`.
- (27) ✅ Divide-by-zero guarded (line 847) and clamped (line 851); covered by tests.

Line 858-864 (new): `isTerminal`.
- (28) ✅ Stdlib-only TTY check with a why-comment explaining the avoided `isatty` dependency — aligns with the "prefer stdlib" convention.

Line 885-1017 (new): `scanProgress`.
- (29) ✅ Nil-`*scanProgress` is uniformly a no-op so the background warm passes nil silently — clean. `markDone`'s read-then-`Add` (line 933) is not atomic as a unit, but each reporter has exactly one writer (its own provider goroutine) and the loop goroutine only *reads* via `Found()`, so there's no race. Worth a one-line comment stating that single-writer invariant, since it's load-bearing.
- (30) ✅ Spinner/`linesDrawn`/`spin` are all touched only by the single loop goroutine (and `end()` after it stops). Safe.

Line 1021-1043 (new): summary + `prettyPath`.
- (31) ✅ Straightforward, branches on `unchanged`/`unattributed` for readable output. `prettyPath` home-abbreviation is a nice touch.

General:
- (32) ⚠️ The file is 1043 lines spanning four concerns: the command + engine, the live indexer, two progress renderers, and the summary. It's cohesive and each function is reasonably sized, but splitting the progress renderers into `reindex_progress.go` (and possibly `live_indexer.go`) would lower the cognitive load. Not blocking.
- (33) ✅ No dead code: `nopReporter`, `grandTotal`, `projectCount`, `totalDone`, `isTerminal`, `progressBar` all have callers; `CreateReindexCommand`, `warmIndexInBackground`, `NewLiveIndexer` are wired in `main.go:1458`, `session_tui.go:1216`, `resume.go:254`/`watch.go:193`.

---

`pkg/cmd/reindex_test.go`

- (34) ✅ `TestDedupRefs` (line 114) is high-value: real temp files with set mtimes exercise the freshest-wins path, blank-id drop, and nil-provider skip in one focused case. The comment explains why real files are used.
- (35) ✅ `TestSelectWork` (line 162) is properly table-driven and covers the meaningful matrix: unchanged, changed mtime, version bump, new, and force. Each asserts `isNew` correctly. This is the strongest test.
- (36) ✅ `TestLiveIndexerRecordConcurrent` (line 258) targets the mutex with 24 goroutines against a real store and is explicitly meant for `-race`. Good — directly validates the serialization claim.
- (37) ⚠️ `TestProjectIDCacheMemoizes` (line 73) seeds the map then reads it back — it verifies `resolve` consults the cache but, because the value is pre-seeded, it doesn't prove memoization *writes* a computed value. Slightly tautological; a stronger version would resolve a real temp git dir twice and assert the second call doesn't recompute (e.g. via a spy/counter). Low priority.
- (38) ⚠️ Coverage gap: the concurrency core — `processWork`/`drainToStore` — has no test, so the issue-13 deadlock-on-write-error is uncaught. A test injecting an `UpsertBatch` failure with &gt;256 queued items would both document the contract and catch the hang.
- (39) ✅ No flakiness: no real sleeps, no wall-clock dependence, fixed timestamps, `t.TempDir()` for isolation.

---

Most important issues

1. (13) Deadlock on write error in `processWork` — if `UpsertBatch` fails mid-run with more than ~256 items still queued, workers block on the unread `sessionsCh` and the command hangs instead of returning the write error. The writer needs to signal teardown back to the feed loop and worker sends. Same risk silently affects `warmIndexInBackground`.
2. (8) No panic recovery per provider goroutine in `enumerateAll`/the worker pool — a single malformed session tree can abort the entire reindex.
3. (32/14) Maintainability polish: the 1043-line file would benefit from splitting out the progress renderers, and `goto closing` should be a labeled break for idiomatic Go.

Test-quality assessment

Strong overall: data-driven where it matters (`TestSelectWork`, `TestProgressBar`), the dedup and concurrent-live-indexer tests exercise real behavior against real files/stores, and there's no flakiness (fixed timestamps, `t.TempDir`). The notable gap is the write pipeline (`processWork`/`drainToStore`) — its error and cancellation paths, including the deadlock in issue 13, are untested. `TestProjectIDCacheMemoizes` is mildly tautological. Nothing trivial-but-padding; coverage is well-targeted apart from the pipeline gap.</result>
